Name: Berkeley Group Plc
Description:
The Berkeley Group builds homes and neighbourhoods in London, Birmingham and the South of England. 
Our passion and purpose is to build quality homes, strengthen local communities and make a positive difference to people’s lives. 
We have an ambitious long-term business strategy in place, called Our Vision 2030. It sets out a balanced and holistic approach to leading our business over the next decade. It will help us to be a world-class business, trusted to transform the most challenging sites into exceptional places and to maximise our positive impact on society, the economy and the natural world.

Name: Zenith Group
Description:
Zenith Group, overseeing operations across 12 subsidiary companies engaged in over 30 activities in the property sector. These subsidiaries cover a broad spectrum, including property development, real estate, comprehensive in-house construction activities, design, and property management.

Our strength lies in our comprehensive In-house capabilities, spanning from visionary design to robust construction, culminating in meticulous property management. We are a self-sustaining ecosystem, excelling in every facet of the industry, ensuring excellence and innovation are built into the very core of every project we touch.

By consolidating all aspects of property development and management under one entity, Zenith effectively manages projects, ensuring timely delivery with top-notch finishes and risk mitigation through close collaboration among participating members.
This full-chain approach enhances our capability, transparency, cost-effectiveness, and successful project delivery, and fosters better coordination among involved parties in property development.

The Advantages of Our Investment and Partnership Plans

1. Strategic Alliances
Zenith Group thrives on collaboration. We seek out synergies with visionary partners who share our passion for growth. Our robust network ensures access to exclusive opportunities, from groundbreaking developments to joint ventures that redefine industry norms.

2. Risk Mitigation
When all team members work under one umbrella, coordination and buildability significantly increase. This unified approach minimizes conflicts of interest and reduces the chances of cheating among participants in the development process.

3. Legacy Building
Beyond profits, we aspire to leave a legacy. By investing with Zenith, you become part of a narrative that transcends generations. Together, we shape skylines, empower communities, and redefine what's possible.

Name: Southern Housing
Description:
At Southern Housing, we believe a safe, secure home can change everything. 

We provide homes and services to over 167,000 people across London, the South East, the Isle of Wight and the Midlands - offering support, stability and the chance to thrive. 

As one of the UK’s largest housing providers, we’re here to create places people feel proud to live. We invest in wellbeing, build lasting relationships, and work side by side with residents to shape services that matter. 

Our social purpose drives us.  We reinvest into improving homes and lives, and we’re serious about inclusion, sustainability and co-creation. 

Whether it’s helping someone build digital confidence, supporting a young person into work, or designing safer neighbourhoods - our work has real impact.  

We’re looking for people who care deeply about communities, bring fresh ideas and want to grow in a place that values them.  

Join us and help shape the future of housing.

Name: Orbit Group
Description:
One of the UK’s leading not-for-profit housing groups, Orbit was established in 1967 by two people on a park bench contemplating how to tackle homelessness. 

Our purpose today is every bit as important as it was when Orbit was founded. We now manage around 47,000 affordable homes in the Midlands, East and South East, and build around 1,000 new affordable homes every year. 

Our aim is to ensure the homes we provide and the places we create are good quality, affordable and safe, and we reinvest our surplus to improve the quality of our homes, services and our communities, and provide more much needed affordable housing.

Follow us for the latest updates on our homes, communities, and career opportunities.

Name: Thomas & Piron
Description:
Active depuis plus de 47 ans dans la construction, le groupe Thomas & Piron aujourd’hui leader de son secteur avec + de 1800 logements construits ou rénovés chaque année en Belgique et au Grand-Duché de Luxembourg.  

En 2014, Thomas & Piron Holding a été mis en place pour chapeauter un groupe d’entités opérationnelles avec des spécificités propres. Nous couvrons également les domaines de la construction de maisons, d’immeubles à appartements, de bâtiments industriels et de bureaux, les marchés publics, rénovation, … 

Formé en 1976, le Groupe Thomas & Piron n’a eu de cesse d’évoluer, dans un marché toujours plus exigeant, avec cette volonté permanente d’offrir une qualité irréprochable.  

Thomas & Piron crée le futur et entretient ses atouts depuis plus de quatre décennies ! L’entreprise est un modèle d’intégration verticale composé de 3100 collaborateurs ouvriers (Chauffagistes, Maçons, Couvreurs, ...) et employés (développeurs, ingénieurs, commerciaux, deviseurs, dessinateurs, personnel administratif et de support, ...). 

Que ce soit pour répondre aux besoins des particuliers, du secteur public ou encore des investisseurs désireux de se constituer un patrimoine immobilier ou de diversifier leurs placements, le savoir-faire de Thomas & Piron permet de concrétiser tous types de projets, du plus conventionnel au plus audacieux.  

Cette exigence requiert une recherche constante de profils qualifiés pour pouvoir assurer le suivi de nos projets et la croissance de l’entreprise. L’étendue de nos métiers est telle qu’elle permet une grande perspective d’évolution en adéquation avec un secteur qui évolue de manière constante et avec des techniques de travail innovantes. 

Reconnue «Best Managed Company» 4 années consécutive pour sa stratégie explicite, sa solide expertise, sa motivation et ses excellentes performances financières, Thomas & Piron est aujourd’hui devenu un groupe international incontournable.

Name: Urban Development Institute of Australia (WA)
Description:
ABOUT UDIA

The Urban Development Institute of Australia (UDIA) is the peak body representing the development industry across the nation.  The WA division has a strong membership across a broad range of developers, consultants, state and local government departments and agencies and is a recognisable and highly respected brand in this state.

UDIA Member activities include master planned communities, medium/high density development, urban renewal, land sub-division, multi-level developments, attached & detached housing, commercial, retail & industrial developments, retirement living and hotels & resorts.

Our target audience is made up of over 4,500 industry contacts from the urban development sector and our luncheons attract up to 650 attendees.

Name: Banks Group
Description:
The Banks Group is a family business based in County Durham, employing people across the north of England. It was established in 1976 and develops land for a variety of uses including residential and commercial property and mineral extraction.

We adopt an approach to our business that we call development with care, which ensures that our developments have a positive long term effect on the environment, the economy and the local communities that host our developments.

The Banks Community Fund was established in 1997 to provide support to community groups, voluntary organisations and environmental projects that are located within close proximity of a current or proposed Banks Group development and provide benefit to the local community.

Name: New Story
Description: We empower families to transform their lives through land and home ownership.

Name: Aveo
Description:
As Australia’s leading and most innovative retirement living provider, our diverse national portfolio offers something for everyone. For over 40 years, we’ve been dedicated to helping retired Australians be their best selves across every stage of their later life journey.

We’re committed to delivering the best retirement living options, continually listening and learning about what people want. It’s how we make changes for the better. Taking the time to understand their unique needs, we help retirees find the perfect place to call home. And with a 94% resident satisfaction rate, we’re confident that 10,000+ residents agree.

Name: Land, Planning and Development Federation
Description:
The Land, Planning & Development Federation (LPDF) represents the UK’s leading land promotion, housebuilding and development businesses.

LPDF members specialise in the promotion and development of predominantly greenfield strategic land across the UK.

LPDF members support the housebuilding and commercial development sectors by providing “oven ready” land with a planning permission and can facilitate the delivery of serviced parcels. 

The LPDF seeks to represent its members’ views and to influence Government planning, housing and commercial development policy.

Name: Experience Senior Living
Description: Experience Senior Living, a division of NexCore Group, builds and manages senior living communities. Driven by resident experience, world-class healthcare, and providing the best quality of life possible, we are here to break the stigma of traditional senior living experiences.

Name: Zimaya Properties
Description: A boutique real estate developer committed to excellence, focusing on delivering high-quality developments with thoughtful layouts and meticulous craftsmanship.

Name: Housing Choices Australia
Description: The Housing Choices Group of Companies (Housing Choices) is a national, not-for-profit housing provider. Housing Choices exists to create safe, quality and affordable housing for people struggling to find a home in Australia’s challenging private rental market; working with partners to create resilient and inclusive communities. Managing over 7,000 social and affordable homes for 8,500 residents across Australia, Housing Choices has an asset base of $1 billion, revenue of more than $80million and almost 300 employees across five states. Housing Choices has won numerous architectural and other awards for the quality of its housing and services for people living on low incomes, many of whom live with disability.

Name: Advance Northumberland
Description:
Advance Northumberland is a diverse property investment & development business, delivering opportunities to improve prosperity, tackle inequalities and drive economic growth in support of Northumberland County Council,

Wholly owned by Northumberland County Council and with a board made up of public and private sectors, Advance Northumberland is the Council’s primary regeneration delivery vehicle. It employs a wide range of investment, development and regeneration capabilities to shape places and promote economic growth and prosperity in Northumberland.

Name: Assemble
Description:
Australia needs fresh housing solutions to tackle our entrenched housing crisis. Backed by our majority owners, AustralianSuper and HESTA – two of Australia’s largest superannuation funds - we are delivering new homes and providing better and more diverse housing choices for renters and homebuyers right across the country.  

Our approach sees us design new housing investment models which will create a better, fairer housing system – and one that cares for the people and places within it. As a Certified B Corp, we’re part of a global community of businesses that meet high standards of social and environmental impact. 

We’ve introduced innovative ways to support people to achieve home ownership.Backed by AustralianSuper, our purchase pathway (known as build-to-rent-to-own) fixes the rent and purchase price of our homes for five years, enabling our residents to experience living in their homes whilst they save to buy - without the housing market racing away from them.We have completed two projects under our purchase pathway – 38 Albermarle Street, and 15 Thompson Street in Kensington – with further projects currently in construction.  

We also know the Australian housing rental market also needs innovation. Backed by HESTA, we’re also delivering social, affordable, Specialist Disability Accommodation (SDA) and mid-market-rate rental housing (build-to-rent), where residents can start putting down roots. Our first project, 402 Macaulay Road in Kensington, is due for completion in 2025.

Name: Groupe Al Omrane
Description:
Entreprise publique née en 2007 à la suite de la fusion de quatre établissements publics (ERAC-ANHI-SNEC-ATTACHOURK) et devient l’opérateur-aménageur chargé de la mise en œuvre de la politique de l’Etat en matière d’habitat et de développement urbain.

Contributeur de premier ordre à l’effort national de production de logements et principalement ceux destinés aux populations les plus démunies, et particulièrement dans les zones et les segments en déficit. Le 𝐆𝐫𝐨𝐮𝐩𝐞 𝐀𝐥 𝐎𝐦𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐞 contribue ainsi à travers son positionnement social à renforcer l’offre d’habitats pour les couches sociales à faible revenu et classe moyenne et à améliorer leur cadre de vie.  

Opérateur privilégié de l’État et des collectivités territoriales dans la résorption de l’habitat insalubre et clandestin ainsi que dans la prévention de sa reconstitution.  
Aménageur de référence à l’échelle nationale qui accompagne la politique de développement des villes nouvelles et des grands projets urbains intégrés. 

Le Groupe assure ces différentes missions grâce à une présence sur l’ensemble du territoire national à travers ses 70 représentations (14 filiales et 56 agences). Il dispose aussi d’une représentation à Paris qui lui donne une présence et une visibilité à l’international. 

L’atteinte de ces objectifs se base sur un partenariat renforcé avec l’ensemble des acteurs centraux et locaux (élus et autorités locales), publics et privés, et sur un renforcement du positionnement du Groupe en tant qu’entreprise citoyenne, moderne et performante.

Name: Vico Homes

A home is more than four walls and a roof.
It should be a safe place. A place to start things. A place of opportunities. A place to grow. A place you feel you belong. These are the places we provide for today and for the future. 

Our people are approachable and professional. And we’re passionate about supporting customers. We're always looking for great people to join us, to work with us and to join forces to offer the very best for our customers and our communities.

Name: Aga Khan Agency for Habitat India

Description:

Name: National Council of State Housing Agencies (NCSHA)
Description:
For more than 50 years, state Housing Finance Agencies (HFAs) have played a central role in the nation’s affordable housing system, delivering financing to make possible the purchase, development, and rehabilitation of affordable homes and rental apartments for low- and middle-income households.

The National Council of State Housing Agencies (NCSHA) is a nonprofit, nonpartisan organization created to advance, through advocacy and education, the efforts of the nation’s state HFAs and their partners to provide affordable housing to those who need it.

NCSHA’s members are:
• the state HFAs, as well as the HFAs of the District of Columbia, New York City, Puerto Rico, and the U.S. Virgin Islands;
• the agencies that allocate the Low Income Housing Tax Credit in the states where an HFA does not; and
• more than 350 affiliate members in the affordable housing field.

NCSHA’s Mission
To advance through advocacy and education the nation’s state Housing Finance Agencies’ efforts to provide affordable housing to those who need it.

NCSHA’s Vision
An affordably housed nation.

Name: Strijp-T
Description:
Strijp-T -  Een duurzaam bedrijvenpark met karakter, waar wij vol energie ondernemen! En waar regelmatig door en voor de community verschillende events worden georganiseerd om de ontmoeting en verbinding te versterken en vooral erg leuk zijn.

Strijp-T is de herontwikkeling van een 25 hectare voormalig Philips industrieterrein naar een modern hoogwaardig groen duurzaam bedrijvenpark. De karakteristieke bedrijfsgebouwen, waaronder een voormalige kolencentrale en een pomphuis, worden met behoud van de industriële look en respect voor het historisch waardevol karakter gerenoveerd en geoptimaliseerd om innovatieve hightech maakindustrie te huisvesten.

Om als innovatief bedrijf succesvol te zijn moet je de beste mensen in huis hebben en klanten de juiste indruk van het bedrijf geven. Daarnaast wil je duurzaam ondernemen in een inspirerende en dynamische omgeving. Op een locatie met karakter, dichtbij de binnenstad en met een uitstekende bereikbaarheid. In een gebied met interessante partijen om je heen en volop mogelijkheden om je verder te ontwikkelen. Het in Eindhoven gelegen Strijp-T is zo’n locatie, hier vind je het allemaal.

In deze snel veranderende wereld weet je nooit precies wat de toekomst brengt. Op Strijp-T, met haar spraakmakende historie, duurzame kwaliteit en hoogwaardig comfort ben je klaar voor iedere uitdaging. Wil je meer of minder vierkante meters? Met één partij, die het gebied herontwikkelt, de panden transformeert, verhuurt en begrijpt dat flexibiliteit belangrijk is, ben je op Strijp-T altijd zeker van passende huisvesting voor nu en in de toekomst.

Op Strijp-T onderneem je vol energie en worden ideeën werkelijkheid.

Name: Just Build Homes
Description:
Just Build Homes is the only service to connect supporters of housebuilding with the planning process - mobilising supportive voices, generating supportive responses on planning portals and thereby helping to get thousands of new homes approved.

We are a brand set up by Shared Voice, a research-led digital communications agency. 

When new homes are proposed, it seems like everyone objects. 

But there is a silent majority who are over-looked in the planning system. 

A whole generation who can’t even afford to rent, let alone buy, because of the lack of supply. 

Their stories are not told, their voices are not heard. 

We balance the right of objectors to be heard, with the right of supporters to be heard too – increasing the chances of new homes being approved. 

So that decision makers have the full picture of what local people think.

Name: Professional Development
Description:
Certainly! Providing comprehensive information and guidance is crucial in any development process, whether it's personal growth, skill development, or professional advancement. Here are some key principles to ensure people have the information they need for development:

Clarity of Objectives: Clearly define the goals and objectives of the development process. This helps individuals understand what they are striving towards and why it's important.

Structured Learning Paths: Outline clear learning paths or frameworks that individuals can follow. This could include step-by-step guides, recommended resources, and milestones to track progress.

Access to Resources: Ensure easy access to relevant resources such as articles, books, online courses, tutorials, and tools that support learning and skill development.

Expert Guidance: Provide opportunities for mentorship, coaching, or access to subject matter experts who can offer insights, feedback, and personalized advice.

Feedback Mechanisms: Establish feedback mechanisms where individuals can receive constructive feedback on their progress and areas for improvement.

Supportive Environment: Foster a supportive environment where individuals feel encouraged to ask questions, experiment, and learn from both successes and failures.

Regular Updates and Communication: Keep individuals informed about updates, changes, and new opportunities related to their development journey.

Recognition and Rewards: Recognize and celebrate milestones and achievements along the development path to encourage motivation and continued progress.

Continuous Improvement: Regularly review and update development plans and resources to ensure they remain relevant and effective.

Encourage Self-Directed Learning: Empower individuals to take ownership of their development by promoting self-directed learning initiatives and providing autonomy in choosing learning activities.

Name: Citizens' Housing and Planning Association (CHAPA)
Description:
Citizens' Housing and Planning Association (CHAPA) is the non-profit umbrella organization for affordable housing and community development activities throughout Massachusetts. Established in 1967, CHAPA is the only statewide group which represents all interests in the housing and community development field, including non-profit and for-profit developers, municipal officials, local housing providers and advocates, tenants, homeowners, lenders, consultants, property managers, architects, and others.

CHAPA's mission is to encourage the production and preservation of housing that is affordable to low-income families and individuals. CHAPA pursues its goals through: advocacy with local, state, and federal officials; research on affordable housing issues; education and training for organizations and individuals; and coalition- and consensus-building among broad interests in the field.

Name: London & Middlesex Community Housing
Description:
London and Middlesex Community Housing (LMCH) provides subsidized housing for the City of London and the County of Middlesex. We are the largest provider of rent-geared-to-income housing in the London-Middlesex area.

We believe that housing is the foundation of a better tomorrow. London & Middlesex Community Housing is an ally for those who are looking for stability, security, and a sense of community through housing.

Name: Berg Development Group

Name: Human Nature
Description:
Human Nature creates remarkable places where living is affordable and inclusive, people thrive by sharing and communities grow strong. We help shape a healthier and more optimistic social imagination of what it is to live well in the 21st century and how, together, we might get there. 

The places we create, and associated services, support a step change in the quality of life for all in ways that regenerate nature and point the way to the essential actions needed to stabilise global climate. We hope to empower and support people in our communities to demonstrate how new ways of living and working can overcome barriers and catch on at an extraordinary pace.

Description:
Native Community Development Financial Institutions (NCDFIs) are transforming the Indigenous finance landscape by driving systems-level change, expanding access to capital, and redistributing wealth to Native communities. The Mountain Plains Community Development Corporation (MPCDC) plays a vital role in this movement as a purpose-built organization created to support the growth and resilience of the Mountain Plains Regional Native CDFI Coalition.

Unlike conventional professional service providers, MPCDC is grounded in a mission-driven model that centers Indigenous priorities and community-led development. Our work is dedicated to building capacity, expanding culturally relevant resources, and fortifying the infrastructure necessary to support sustainable economic health in Native communities across Wyoming, Montana, South Dakota, and North Dakota.

Our mission is to empower and strengthen the Coalition’s member NCDFIs, enabling them to create lasting economic opportunities and generational wealth for the communities they serve. Guided by our core values—equity, sovereignty, collaboration, and respect—we are committed to making meaningful contributions that reflect the unique needs and strengths of Indigenous peoples in the Mountain Plains region.

Together with our Coalition partners, MPCDC is advancing a shared vision of economic self-determination and financial justice for Native communities.

Hoe creëer je een leefomgeving die mensen met een zorgvraag écht ondersteunt - die het leven lichter, aangenamer en zelfstandiger maakt? Bij Olea begrijpen we dat elk project unieke wensen en eisen kent. Wij combineren technische expertise met een persoonlijke, warme aanpak.

We begeleiden zowel zorgorganisaties als particulieren bij nieuwbouw, verbouw, inrichting en facilitaire vraagstukken. Ook zijn wij gespecialiseerd in herstelgerichte projecten binnen de letselschade, waarbij een veilige en passende woonomgeving bijdraagt aan het herstel en welzijn van de cliënt.

ZORG EN WONEN VERANDERT - EN WIJ BEWEGEN MEE!
Er is een groeiende behoefte aan woonvormen waarin mensen met een zorgvraag zelfstandig kunnen wonen met zorg op maat. Van zorgstudio’s en -appartementen tot clusterwoningen met 24-uurszorg: Olea helpt deze leefomgevingen mogelijk te maken.

Ook bij particuliere woonoplossingen, zoals het (ver)bouwen of inrichten van een woning na een ongeval of ziekte, zijn wij een betrokken en deskundige partner. Samen met cliënten, belangenbehartigers en verzekeraars realiseren we duurzame oplossingen die het dagelijks leven direct verbeteren.

SCHAKEL TUSSEN TECHNIEK EN ZORG
Olea verbindt alle betrokken partijen: van aannemer tot zorgorganisatie, cliënten en verzekeraars. Wij spreken de taal van techniek én zorg, en zorgen ervoor dat iedereen op één lijn komt – met de gebruiker altijd centraal.

Onze dienstverlening rust op drie pijlers:

• Bouw & verbouw - van initiatief tot realisatie
• Interieur & design - gericht op comfort, functionaliteit en herstel
• Beheer & onderhoud - voor duurzame ondersteuning van het zorgproces

We kunnen een partner zijn in iedere fase. Of het nu gaat om een zorginstelling die uitbreidt, een woning die aangepast moet worden na letsel, of een facilitair proces dat beter moet presteren: we denken graag mee, nemen zorgen uit handen en realiseren omgevingen waarin mensen zich echt thuis voelen.

MEER WETEN?
We maken graag kennis!

Name: Houston Community Land Trust
Description:
Houston Community Land Trust (HCLT) was established in 2018 and is an affordable housing and community development 501c3 non-profit organization serving Houston and parts of Harris County. There are currently over 200 single-family homeownership and rentals units across Harris County in HCLT. In 2025 we expanded our programming to include our rehab program for those that already own their home and in need of repairs and long-term affordability.

HCLT is a TRUE Community Land Trust that utilizes the traditional CLT model which was created by African American farmers during the Civil Rights Movement. As a traditional CLT we are a community-controlled organization that has a tripartite board structure which is comprised of 1/3 CLT residents, a voting community membership and post-purchase Stewardship that engages and supports residents through various activities such as workshops, estate planning, home maintenance, educational scholarships and events.

HCLT is not just about getting someone into a home. We want to make sure they can maintain the home which we do by creating permanent affordability. The average HCLT mortgage is $107,000 with a monthly payment including PITI, HOA and CLT fees of under $1100. We also support activities to ensure that communities are developed without the displacement of historic residents, businesses and resources.

To learn more about HCLT and  Community Land Trust work nationally and internationally,  please visit our website at www.houstonclt.org , Grounded Solutions Network,  https://groundedsolutions.org/strengthening-neighborhoods/community-land-trusts/  and the International Center for Community Land Trusts  https://www.cltweb.org/

GRENZÜBERGREIFENDE KLIMAANPASSUNGSMAßNAHMEN UND -POLITIK

Hoyo Claro Developing Group, Inc. Fundada con el objetivo de transformar la manera en que las personas viven, nuestra compañía se especializa en la planificación, construcción y comercialización de proyectos residenciales innovadores que ofrecen a los clientes una experiencia única de confort y seguridad.

En Hoyo Claro Developing Group, Inc. nos distinguimos por nuestra capacidad para crear comunidades exclusivas que van más allá de la simple construcción de viviendas. Trabajamos con un enfoque integral que abarca cada fase del proceso: desde la adquisición del terreno hasta la entrega final de las propiedades, pasando por el diseño arquitectónico, la ingeniería, la urbanización y la promoción. Nuestro compromiso con la excelencia nos permite ofrecer proyectos que no solo cumplen con los más altos estándares de calidad, sino que también se adaptan a las necesidades y deseos de nuestros clientes.
Nuestra Misión es desarrollar productos inmobiliarios con un impacto positivo en la comunidad, de manera sostenible y rentable, con una visión de ser referente en el desarrollo integral de comunidades en la República Dominicana.

Name: ForAffordable.ca - Canadian Rental Housing Providers for Affordable Housing
Description: We are Canadian rental housing providers for affordable housing. We are made up of Canada’s five largest publicly traded residential REITs: Canadian Apartment Properties REIT (CAPREIT), Boardwalk REIT, Killam Apartment REIT, InterRent REIT and Minto Apartment REIT. We deeply understand that housing is about people. We know that safe, affordable, quality homes are the safe harbour of Canadian family life in all of its diversity.

Name: Rocky Mountain Communities
Description:
Rocky Mountain Communities builds brighter futures by investing in resident-focused affordable housing and services, empowering individuals and families to thrive.  RMC currently provides stable affordable housing and support services for more than 2,500 people in 10 communities throughout Colorado.

RMC was awarded the Organizational Health Tracking System (OHTS) Exemplary rating for Production and Program Services, Resource and Financial Management, and Organizational Management.

Men det är du som gör huset till ett hem, ett ställe som fungerar för det liv du lever och där man får en härlig känsla redan när man kliver in genom dörren.

Name: Canadian Network of Community Land Trusts
Description:
The Canadian Network of Community Land Trusts (CNCLT) is a network of over 35 emerging and established community land trusts and allied organizations working together to ensure the success and growth of the Community Land Trust (CLT) model throughout Canada. The goal of the CNCLT is to support the growth of community land trusts with the primary purposes of providing permanently affordable housing and other community assets that serve low-to-moderate income people. 

CNCLT was established in July 2017 in response to renewed interest and emerging challenges associated with the CLTs in Canada. Since establishment, the CNCLT has organized regular peer-to-peer networking meetings, knowledge exchanges, and webinars on CLTs. In 2021 CNCLT formally incorporated as a non-profit corporation after receiving funding from the CMHC Demonstrations Initiatives, under the Scaling Stream, to develop a Technical Assistance Program to support the growth of Canadian CLTs. 

We recognize that the Canadian Network of Community Land Trusts and its members are working on the traditional territories of First Nations, Inuit, and Métis people.

Centuries of colonialism have dispossessed Indigenous people of lands they have stewarded since time immemorial. Operating within a landscape of stolen land and broken treaties, it is imperative that the Network and its members support Indigenous self-determination and land  restitution through tangible actions.

Name: Slagteriet.dk
Description:
Slagteriet er et produktionshus for kulturelt og kreativt entreprenørskab.

Slagteriet S/I er en selvejende institution med base i de tidligere slagteribygninger midt i Holstebro.

Slagteriets formål er at skabe og være et ambitiøst produktionshus for kunstnere, producenter og borgere.

I bygningerne har vi næsten 13.000 historiske kvadratmeter til rådighed, som en række aktører og frivillige fylder med nyt liv døgnet rundt, året rundt.

I dag er Slagteriet hjemsted for en række faste aktørgrupper, og vi har plads til flere. Vi udlejer også værksteder, atelier, eventrum og meget mere til enkeltstående eller tilbagevendende arrangementer.

Power From Within Clean Energy Society (PFWCES) is a not-for-profit organization made up of 30 and growing volunteers from 8 plus countries, who are forward thinkers and collaborated together to make and bring changes to todays economy in a health and clean way that brings diversity together and breaks the age gap

Our hybrid sustainable 4Ever Homes, will save your family more than $6000.00 yearly with securities in energy usage & agriculture needs all year and more.  PFWCES we didn't just design home, we designed a life change that gives you and your family options like, homes tailored to your needs, education, skill training trade with community employment opportunity and more.

Our gated community without fences that has from seed to table agriculture security intergraded into our "Forever Homes".

 Book a video meeting for Q&A's, we always welcome them or if you're someone who would like to volunteer and join a great family, are arms are open, just contact us at info@pfwces.org subject Q&A on Homes or Volunteering.

Blessings
Isaac

Al Qattara is one of the leading real estate development and investment companies based in Al Ain, UAE. It was founded on the desire to build iconic and exceptional mixed-use communities. Al Qattara develops residential, commercial, and communal spaces of unparalleled quality. Every day, Al Qattara devotes all energy and knowledge in developing immersive spaces and integrating communities within them. It is Al Qattara’s calling to make you feel welcome.

Al Qattara prioritizes human connections and believes that all people deserve an exceptional experience in their living environment. Al Qattara fosters a culture of partnership and community. Al Qattara brings people together and nurtures tradition and culture by creating integrated communities.

Description: TerraBona Developments Ltd. is a private development firm that understands the urgent need to expand the rental housing supply in communities across Ontario. With strong investor backing and a highly experienced and committed technical team of development and management professionals, we have put together a business plan to expand the supply of purpose-built rental housing throughout the province. The intent is to develop up to 1,000 purpose-built rental units per year over the next 10 years.

Name: Habitat for Humanity East Central Ohio
Description: Serving Stark, Carroll, Tuscarawas, Harrison, and Jefferson Counties, Habitat for Humanity East Central Ohio is driven by the vision that everyone needs a decent place to live. People partner with Habitat for Humanity to build or improve a place they can call home. Habitat homeowners help build their own homes alongside volunteers and pay an affordable mortgage. Through financial support, volunteering, or adding a voice to support affordable housing, everyone can help families achieve the strength, stability, and self-reliance they need to build better lives for themselves. Through shelter, Habitat empowers. To learn more, visit habitateco.org.

Name: Mighty Hero Homes, Inc.
Description:
Mighty Hero Homes exists for the purpose of creating affordable, permanent homes for the more than 37,000 homeless US veterans. We will eradicate veteran homelessness by constructing over 450 Mighty Hero Home Bases across the country. Each Base will house 84 veterans in patented Mighty Hero Homes (tiny homes, kit homes and modular homes reimagined), will provide a Training & Career Center, a Health Services Center, a Commissary, Veterans Park (with "Flag Hill) and Commercial Gardens (to provide "Base to Table" fresh vegetables for local restaurants and residents).

Working closely with the VA, HUD and the local PHA, we will construct our Alpha site (Base-000) in Georgia. After a series of After Action Reviews, we will commence expansion across the United States.

For more information and to determine how you can be a part of rectifying this current stain on our country, please visit www.MigthyHeroHomes.org or contact us at info@mightyherohomes.org.

Name: North East Housing Partnership
Description:
Since the devolution deal was announced at the end of 2022, the members of the partnership have come together to collaborate on a prospectus for change.

Our prospectus sets out our record in four key areas: 
1- Regeneration, development and placemaking
2- Net zero and sustainability
3- Employability and social inclusion
4- Health, care and homelessness

It highlights some of the work we are already doing, our offer to the region and how we could work with the North East Mayoral Combined Authority to amplify our work, and our asks of the new Combined Authority going forward. Link: https://www.karbonhomes.co.uk/north-east-housing-partnership/our-prospectus/. 

We have been working on those workstreams over the past six months and will be continuing to make progress together.

Name: Frolic Community
Description:
Founded by MIT Center for Real Estate alumni, Frolic is revolutionizing affordable homeownership through our cooperative housing model. We partner with homeowners facing displacement in high-cost markets to transform single-family lots into vibrant pocket communities of 6-20 homes, making homeownership accessible with just $5K-$30K down payments.

Each Frolic cooperative features thoughtfully designed spaces that balance privacy with community connection, with sustainable design elements and financing structures that build equity while maintaining affordability.

As a certified B Corporation, we're committed to inclusive neighborhood development that supports first-time homebuyers. After the successful completion of our flagship project in Seattle, we're now expanding across Washington and California with 5 active projects and 48+ in our development pipeline.

Partner with us to transform property ownership into community building – whether you're a homeowner looking to build community and unlock your property's potential, a developer seeking our turnkey cooperative solutions, or a first-time homebuyer ready to build equity in a supportive community.

Description:

Vårt löfte till våra hyresgäster är att vi alltid strävar efter att skapa den sköna känslan att komma hem. Det hjälper oss att fatta kloka beslut.

Name: Minnesota Housing Partnership
Description:
Minnesota Housing Partnership (MHP) drives innovative housing solutions with original research, builds community capacity through collaborative development support nationwide, and advocates for policies that ensure everyone has a place to call home. 

Our mission is to expand housing and community development opportunity for those most impacted by economic and racial disparities by leading collaborative work to promote systems change and grow equitable development capacity. In partnership, we bring your housing and community development goals to fruition.

Name: Homes for America, Inc.
Description:
Homes for America (HFA) is a non-profit organization dedicated to creating and preserving quality, affordable housing communities that foster resident success and well-being. Since its founding in 1994, HFA has been a pivotal force in the Mid-Atlantic region, focusing primarily on Maryland and expanding efforts in Virginia, Pennsylvania, and Delaware. With a mission to provide stable, service-enriched housing, HFA connects residents with essential services and opportunities, ensuring they live with dignity and respect.

Our approach is rooted in a holistic understanding of housing as more than just a physical space. At HFA, we believe a home should support health, education, and economic stability. This philosophy drives us to offer a range of resident services, including health and wellness programs, financial assistance, educational opportunities, and community connectivity initiatives. Our Service-Enhanced Housing model ensures that residents are active participants in shaping the programs and services available in their communities.

HFA is committed to creating inclusive communities where every resident feels safe, respected, and supported. We strive to create inclusive communities where every resident feels safe, respected, and supported. Our development projects prioritize sustainability and environmental stewardship, aligning with our goals to reduce our ecological footprint and build climate-resilient communities. By leveraging partnerships with public, private, and community-based agencies, we provide comprehensive support that enhances residents' quality of life.

As we continue to grow and evolve, HFA remains focused on its core values: resident-centered decision-making, high-quality services, dismantling structural racism in housing, and fiscal responsibility. Our dedication to these principles ensures that we not only meet the housing needs of today but also pave the way for a brighter, more equitable future for all our residents.

Name: Maine Affordable Housing Coalition
Description:
The Maine Affordable Housing Coalition (MAHC) is a membership organization representing public, private and non-profit groups. MAHC has more than 140 private and public sector organizations, including developers, architects, syndicators, banks, engineers, builders, investors, Community Action agencies, public housing authorities, housing and service providers, advocates and others committed to ensuring that all Mainers are adequately and affordably housed.

MAHC commissions reports to inform the development and advocacy for safe, affordable housing in Maine. These reports include an analysis of the housing needs of Maine’s older population, a comprehensive assessment of housing affordability in Maine, the effectiveness of the Housing Choice Voucher Program in Maine communities, the economic impacts of developing affordable housing, and the governance structure of Maine State Housing Authority, and also convenes public forums on the affordable housing crisis.

MAHC is Maine’s lead agency for the New England Housing Network, a coalition of housing and community development organizations joined together to provide a regional response to changes in federal housing and community development programs, a member of Up For Growth, and the National Low Income Housing Coalition’s state coalition partner in Maine.

Join MAHC and Become a Member
MAHC welcomes new members and is supported by membership dues and grants. Regularly scheduled membership meetings are held in varying locations every other month around the state. MAHC sends weekly email round ups policy, funding, and news regarding affordable housing, and holds an every other year Housing Policy Conference. For more information or if you have any questions, please contact MAHC at info@mainehousingcoalition.org.

Elle vise les chantiers d’une certaine envergure (100 logements, au-delà de 500 K€ par commande).  
 
M2E a pour clients les entreprises générales, les maîtres d’ouvrage avec l’interface éventuelle des maîtres d’ouvrage délégués et/ou des maîtres d’œuvre.

Name: La foncière solidaire du Grand Lyon
Description:
La foncière solidaire du Grand Lyon est un Organisme de Foncier Solidaire (OFS) agréé par l’État.

Elle achète la part du foncier concerné par les logements en BRS dans les résidences proposées par les opérateurs immobiliers partenaires (promoteurs, bailleurs…).
L’opérateur construit son programme et vous vend seulement les murs à un taux de TVA réduit à 5,5%. La foncière solidaire du Grand Lyon reste propriétaire de la part du terrain de votre logement qui représente en moyenne 20% de son prix. Elle vous le loue à moins de 2€ le m2 habitable par mois. Ce loyer ou «redevance» fait l’objet d’un Bail Réel Solidaire contracté entre vous et la foncière solidaire, pour une durée maximum de 99 ans.

Name: ADAPT Housing
Description:
The Adapt team come from a variety of backgrounds and bring a wealth of experience in developing innovative and highly specialised solutions.
Our expertise in adhering to government-set guidelines in the housing sector is evidenced by the success of Affordable Management Corporation (AMC), which was founded by the Directors of Adapt Housing.
AMC manages over 2,800 dwellings across 160 locations, providing participation and access to tenants, property developers and investors in the Federal and State Governments’ National Rental Affordability Scheme (NRAS).
The Adapt team draw on the highly specialised solutions developed by AMC in managing government grant applications, administration, legislative compliance, and ongoing management requirements for participation in the NRAS, applying that knowledge and experience to the requirements of the NDIA’s National Disability Insurance Scheme.
Having already forged strong relationships within the government and housing sectors, Adapt is positioned to successfully collaborate with key stakeholders within the community to achieve ideal outcomes for individual participants, families, service providers and investors alike.
Underwriting the experience and connections of the Adapt team is a strong background in business services and finance, with services also provided to long-term investors seeking to capitalise on the opportunities presented through the provision of specialist disability accommodation.

Name: HomeSight
Description: We are a non-profit Community Development Corporation that wants to help make homeownership a reality for you! For more than 20 years, our highly trained staff has worked with families like yours, helping to create more than 1,700 home owners. Our programs help fulfill our mission of building strong, vibrant communities through homeownership, economic development and neighborhood revitalization. We offer first-time homebuyer education, financial counseling, purchase assistance, new home development, and 1st mortgage originations. HomeSight believes homeownership is one of the best investments you can make in your future. Owning your home gives you the freedom to create a living environment that suits you. It provides stability and a sense of place. And ownership allows you to build equity and financial security over time for you and your family. But we know that buying your first home can be challenging. That’s why we’re committed to creating affordable homeownership opportunities for first-time buyers. We currently serve King, Pierce, and Snohomish counties in Washington State.

WE are not just anyone!

Digitalization and technological innovations have long been part of our work. We don't shy away from new tasks and we also break new ground. We are guided by our long, successful history, but we don't let it restrict our ideas. We take care of housing, educational infrastructure, neighborhood development and digital services. 
We think ahead so that people in Mannheim can continue to live well in the future. To this end, we continue to develop and rely on every single employee. We are building the future together. Sounds exciting, doesn't it?
