Name: Housing Now!
Description:
An unlikely alliance of business, unions, universities and interest groups have come together to help solve the state’s housing crisis and support the delivery of more than 300,000 new homes.

Housing Now! is a bold initiative to stop the housing shortage tearing at the social fabric of our state.

Name: Cambridge Investment Partnership
Description:
Formed in 2017 as an equal partnership between Cambridge City Council and The Hill Group, CIP is delivering on its mission to regenerate ageing council estates, raise living standards and deliver the next generation of sustainable council housing alongside market sale homes.

All new homes developed through CIP meet or exceed national space standards, with many built to Passivhaus or the council’s CamStandard equivalent. The partnership also delivers social value through jobs, apprenticeships, and community investment, ensuring each regeneration benefits the wider neighbourhood.
 
With a pipeline of over 2,000 homes, including at least 1,000 new council homes, CIP continues to play a vital role in addressing Cambridge’s housing needs while contributing to national efforts to improve the standard of council housing across the UK.

Name: Veluwonen
Description:
Veluwonen biedt mensen met een bescheiden inkomen een huis dat zij tot een thuis kunnen maken, op een eenvoudige en prettige manier. Wij beseffen ons dat wij te maken hebben met vierduizend individuele, bijzondere huishoudens. Wij willen dat u als huurder ook écht het gevoel heeft: dit is mijn huis!

Deze huizen staan in drie gemeenten: Brummen (ongeveer 3.000 woningen), Apeldoorn (ongeveer 1.000 woningen) en Rheden (ongeveer 50 woningen). Ze staan verspreid over 16 dorpen. Deze dorpen zijn kleinschalig, overzichtelijk en saamhorig. Tegelijkertijd is ieder dorp anders. In grootte, ligging, de mensen die er wonen, sociale netwerken en in de voorzieningen. Wij vinden het belangrijk om het unieke karakter van de dorpen te behouden.

Name: CITYBUILDER
Description: CITYBUILDER believes that growth is good, and even better when many people build together. We are building a central North Carolina grassroots pro-housing coalition as a member-based, local news outlet and advocacy organization.

Name: Hedyn
Description:
At Hedyn, our vision is to have connected communities where everyone can live well. We use our expertise, scale and local knowledge to deliver excellent customer services that meet the needs of our residents, communities and future generations. 

Hedyn was born following the merger between Melin Homes and Newport City Homes in April 2025. Hedyn is Welsh for seed. It symbolises growth, new beginnings, and the potential for positive change. By connecting people across five local authority areas in Wales, Hedyn is committed to building communities where everyone can thrive.

And it’s not just about a name. Hedyn is a values-led organisation, and these values will underpin everything we do

Do the right thing
Make it happen
Be the difference

Follow us to find out about what’s it’s like to work at Hedyn, our current job opportunities and more about our talented, skilled and passionate people who make Hedyn a place:

• To grow, make a positive impact and drive change.
• Where like-minded people are empowered to do the right thing for our customers and residents.
• That sparks curiosity and energises team spirit.
• Where we nurture an inclusive environment, diversity is celebrated, community volunteering is championed, and social connection is welcomed.

Name: FPI Nouvelle Aquitaine - Federation des Promoteurs Immobiliers Nouvelle Aquitaine
Description: Fédération des Promoteurs de Nouvelle-Aquitaine

Name: Self-Sustainable City
Description:
Self-Sustainable City is a revolutionary real estate development designed for energy independence, food security, and eco-friendly living. Our mission is to create a self-sufficient community where homes generate their own power, grow their own food, and minimize environmental impact.  

Why Choose a Self-Sustainable Home?  
 Sustainable Housing – Eco-friendly homes built with renewable materials  
 Off-Grid Living – Solar, wind, and alternative energy solutions  
 Permaculture & Food Forests – Ready-made, maintenance-free food sources  
 Water Conservation – Rainwater harvesting & wastewater recycling  
 Freedom & Resilience – Live independently from centralized infrastructure  

Urban & Rural Living Solutions  
We offer both sustainable urban housing and self-sufficient rural retreats, providing options for those who want to embrace net-zero living without sacrificing modern comforts.  

Our Vision  
To build a future where sustainable cities and self-sufficient communities become the norm, reducing reliance on unsustainable systems and empowering homeowners with true autonomy.  

Join the Sustainable Living Movement  
Be part of the future of green housing, off-grid communities, and sustainable real estate. Whether you want to buy a home, invest in sustainable property, or collaborate, we welcome you to explore our vision.  

 Visit our website to learn more.
https://ramakrishnasurathu.blogspot.com

Name: Innascore Developments Inc.
Description: At Innascore Developements, we advocate for the client by ensuring that the project exceeds their expectations. Our Team believes in building thought provoking, profitable and quality projects that will last in the Okanagan Valley.

Name: Damaan Group
Description:
Damaan Group has become synonymous with iconic projects setting new standards for design, craftsmanship and inspired lifestyles. Today, Damaan Group enjoys a position of trust, established over years of uncompromising quality. We have consistently stuck to a mantra of sound design and attention to the small details that make a home special. I am proud that the Damaan as brand is now recognised in International markets and stretching to Pakistan.
I am also incredibly grateful to our community of employees, partners and shareholders. Thank you for supporting us in creating exceptional lifestyle experiences, and for believing in our ability to create flourishing communities. Our successes would not be possible without your commitment.
Finally, and most importantly, I’d like to express my appreciation to our customers, without whom we could never have achieved so much. Quite simply, you inspire us to do even better.

Name: Summit Housing Group
Description:
Summit Housing Group (SHG) is a dynamic development company based in beautiful Western Montana. Since our inception in 2002, we have specialized in creating high-quality, affordable housing solutions across multiple states, including Montana, South Dakota, Utah, Colorado, and Wyoming.
Our Expertise:
•	Market Rate Housing: We develop and manage market-rate housing that meets the needs of diverse communities.
•	Low-Income Housing Tax Credit (LIHTC) Developments: Our focus on LIHTC developments ensures that we provide affordable housing options for low-income families.
•	Build to Rent and Townhomes: We offer a variety of housing options, including build-to-rent properties and townhomes, catering to different lifestyle needs.
Our Commitment: At SHG, we are committed to quality craftsmanship and long-term sustainability. Our projects are designed to exceed zoning and inspection compliances, ensuring safe and durable housing for our residents.
Our Impact: To date, we have built, been awarded, or have under construction 40 properties, totaling approximately 2,000 units. Our continued growth is driven by our dedicated team and strong partnerships with institutions and organizations that share our vision of providing quality housing.
Meet Our Team:
•	Paul Capps: Chief Executive Officer & President
•	Scott Long: Owner and Chairman
•	Sam Long: Director of Acquisitions
•	John Franzese: Construction Manager
•	Sydney Sandefur: Development Analyst
•	Sharayah Ballas: Controller & Senior Construction Accountant
•	Karen Martin: Regional Manager and Compliance Officer
•	Kim Roth: Director of Human Resources & Accounts Payable Manager
Join Us: Follow our page to stay updated on our latest projects, industry insights, and career opportunities. Together, we can build a better future, one home at a time.

Name: South East Florida Apartment Association
Description: The South East Florida Apartment Association is the trusted provider of education, networking, and legislative support as the leading advocate, resource, and community partner throughout the multifamily industry.

Name: MADE Partnership
Description:
MADE Partnership focuses on the master development of large sites, to help deliver thousands of much needed new homes across the country.

MADE Partnership will act as master developer for multiple large scale, residential-led developments from 1,000 to more than 10,000 homes along with a variety of community facilities and employment uses. 

Potential development opportunities will include large brownfield developments, as well as new garden village style communities.

The partnership brings together Barratt, the UK’s leading national sustainable housebuilder, Homes England, the Government body responsible for housebuilding and regeneration in England, and Lloyds Banking Group, one of the largest funders of the UK housing sector.

Name: Wingspan Development Group
Description:
Wingspan Development Group is a full-service real estate development and investment firm that delivers high-quality, experiential commercial, mixed-use, and multifamily projects. 

The company has realized exponential growth by seeking to develop properties that others forego. Their approach yields exceptional value as well as the resurgence and regentrification of communities across Florida, Illinois, and Wisconsin. 

Whether it is our fund investors, employees, or residents, at Wingspan Development Group, we start by laying the foundation for positive, long-term relationships and build from there. Our team succeeds because we are hands-on, experienced, well-respected, and invested at every level. With more than 100 years of combined experience across multiple real estate segments, we build for longevity from the ground up.

Wingspan is a thriving entity within the Nicholas Family of Companies.

Name: EKIDOM
Description:
EKIDOM, l’Office Public de l’Habitat de GRAND POITIERS loue et vend des logements  sur Poitiers et sur une quarantaine de communes du département.
Son patrimoine est  de 12790 logements locatifs. 

1er bailleur de Grand Poitiers, EKIDOM renouvelle son patrimoine avec de nombreux logements neufs en chantier ou en projet. Son patrimoine ancien bénéficie également d’un plan de rénovation permanent pour maintenir sa qualité et son attractivité.   EKIDOM s’inscrit aussi dans la recherche constante d’une plus grande qualité de service pour les locataires et un niveau exemplaire de performance dans les logements.
Ekidom, c’est une équipe pluridisciplinaire de 205 collaborateurs repartie entre un siège social et trois Directions Territoriales situées dans les principaux quartiers de Poitiers. Plus de 50% de son effectif est dédié à la proximité et à l'accompagnement des locataires.
Pour contacter EKIDOM, postuler sur un logement ou s'informer : www.ekidom.fr

Name: Specht Group Italia
Description: Our project was created in order to develop in Italy the Central European concept of an active lifestyle mainly dedicated to boomers; people full of energy, desire to live, travel and share culture and experiences in unique locations without having to deal with daily commitments.

Name: THE IVEAGH TRUST
Description:
Founded by Edward Cecil Guinness in 1890,
The Iveagh Trust is the oldest housing charity in Ireland.

We are a statutory trust established under the Iveagh Trust Acts 1899–1961 and a registered housing charity. The Trust is an Approved Housing Body and operates on a not-for-profit basis.

Today, the Trust provides over 1640 homes around Dublin, offering a range of housing options including general needs housing for families, supported housing for older people and accommodation for men experiencing homelessness at the Iveagh Hostel.

Our Mission:

The core mission of the Iveagh Trust is the provision and management of quality housing and everything we do is directed towards furthering this aim. Our mission as set down in our founding deed is:
“The amelioration of the conditions of the working population of Dublin and their modes and manner of living, by the provision of improved dwellings”.

Building Communities:
Our aim is not just to provide homes, but to put in place the supports and services to help our communities thrive. A team of dedicated estate managers and caretakers are on hand to help make our communities great places to live.

Many staff also live in our communities and we strive to provide an on-site estate management service, seven-days, year-round.

Name: INSPIRING YOU TO GREATNESS NON-PROFIT COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION
Description:
INSPIRING YOU TO GREATNESS NON-PROFIT COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION serving: California, Colorado, New Mexico, Texas, Colorado, North Carolina, Oklahoma Washington State and beyond.

Nonprofit Developer-Builder Community Development Corporation specializing in build-to-rent affordable housing, pathway to homeownership educational platform, entrepreneurship education, workforce development and CRA community reinvestment eligible programs to assist low-to-moderate to mid-to-moderate income families with self-sufficiency and homeownership.

2001-2004 Conducted "Living Your Dreams" Debt Free Seminars Sponsored By Local Banks

2003-2005 Conducted Credit Restoration Seminars to Smith County Residents at Tyler Jr College

2005-2008 Conducted "Home Buying 101" Home Buying & Home Financing Seminars w/ Realtors

2011-2017 Key-Note Speaker at "Effective Renters Educational Seminars" in 9 cities in Texas

2015-2017 Workforce Development Grant Resource for The City of Dallas Economic Development

2016-2017 Junior Achievement JA Success Story “Empowering To Own Their Economic Success”

2017 Grant Recipient with MidSouth Bank, American First National Bank, Texas Governor’s Office

2017 Key-Note Speaker for Woodforest National Bank for Small Business Workforce Development

2017 Instructor for Collin College Entrepreneurship Program & Workforce Development Education

2017 Workforce Development Keynote Speaker for Woodforest National Bank Small Businesses

2015-2019 Collin College Youth Entrepreneurship Workshops

2020-2022 E3 - Educate. Empower. Employ Job Readiness Training

2020-2022 B3 - Better. Business. Banking Community Lunch & Learns

2020-2022 M3 - Pathway To Home - Mortgage Readiness Education

Inspiring You To Greatness CDC is a bootstraps back bone organization dedicated to creating a visual roadmap to success through education, entrepreneurship, workforce development, and affordable housing stability developments to stabilize housing while creating self-sufficiency.

Name: Hoffman Development Group, LLC
Description: 'One Point of Contact' .. from Project Visualization to Facility Completion.

Name: weVLC
Description: We find undervalued properties across the city’s most vibrant neighborhoods and bring them back to life. Through thoughtful design and careful renovation, we turn overlooked spaces into homes that people are proud to live in. And through our private investment platform, we invite others to share in this value — building wealth while building something real.

Name: NICHI - National Indigenous Collaborative Housing Inc / Logement coopératif national Autochtone Inc
Description: The National Indigenous Collaborative Housing Inc. (NICHI) exists to uphold and advance housing as a human right for all Indigenous Peoples living in urban, rural and northern communities from coast-to-coast-to-coast in Canada.

Name: Roost
Description: Building tools for a million co-ops

Name: Housing Assessment Resource Tools (HART)
Description: HART is working with governments, housing providers, and advocates across Canada to improve how we measure and address housing need, towards the national goal of “progressively realizing the right to adequate housing”.

Name: National American Indian Housing Council
Description:
Since 1974, the only national organization representing the housing and community development interests of Native Americans, Alaska Natives, and Native Hawaiians.

Our Mission: To effectively and efficiently promote and support American Indians, Alaska Native and native Hawaiians in their self-determined goals to provide culturally relevant and quality affordable housing for native people.

A 501(c)3 corporation, NAIHC is the only national organization representing housing interests of native people who reside in Indian communities, Alaska Native Villages and on native Hawaiian Home Lands. With over 250 members representing 463 tribes and housing organizations, NAIHC also has associate organizations and individual members who support our mission

Name: Cambridge Housing Authority
Description:
Established under state law on December 9, 1935, Cambridge Housing Authority (CHA) provides long-term rental housing and rental assistance to more than 10,000 low-income families, elders and disabled individuals through its Public Housing and Housing Choice Voucher (HCV) Programs.

CHA invests in Cambridge families and provides enhanced support to almost 10% of the city population.

By focusing on policy innovation and family economic opportunities, CHA meets its mission: to develop and manage safe, good quality, affordable housing for low-income individuals and families in a manner that promotes citizenship, community, and self-reliance in one of the most expensive housing markets in the country.

The Affordable Housing Accreditation Board (AHAB) has awarded national affordable housing accreditation to Cambridge Housing Authority in Cambridge, Massachusetts. The Cambridge Housing Authority became the seventh affordable housing provider to receive AHAB accreditation. The designation of “Accredited Affordable Housing Organization” reflects an organization’s commitment to meeting high performance standards established by the affordable housing industry and to providing quality, well-managed housing.

Name: COLEGIO ADMINISTRADORES DE FINCAS VALENCIA-CASTELLON (CAFVC)
Description:
Es una Corporación de Derecho Público con personalidad jurídica propia y plena capacidad para el cumplimiento de sus fines, que se rige por sus propios Estatutos y Reglamentos de régimen interno.

Agrupa a los profesionales que se dedican a la administración del patrimonio inmobiliario. Su actividad comprende las provincias de Valencia y Castellón y actualmente hay colegiados cerda de 1000 compañeros que administran más del 80% de las viviendas de la zona de influencia.

FUNCIONES DEL COLEGIO:

Vela por el buen ejercicio de la profesión para que la actividad profesional, en base a principios éticos, sea adecuada a los intereses y derechos de los ciudadanos, siendo uno de los puntales principales la preservación del Derecho fundamental a la Vivienda. También vela para garantizar la conservación y administración de los bienes y del patrimonio inmobiliario.

Defiende los intereses profesionales de sus colegiados evitando la mala praxis y la competencia desleal.

Organiza actividades con contenido social y cultural para sus colegiados.

Arbitra y media en los conflictos que se susciten entre colegiados, entre colegiados y sus clientes y entre los clientes de los colegiados.

Emite informes y dictámenes sobre cuestiones que entren dentro del ámbito profesional de sus colegiados.

Representa los intereses generales de la profesión ante la Administración y ante otras instituciones representativas de ámbito autonómico y estatal.

Fomenta acuerdos con otras entidades, organizaciones o colegios dentro de su ámbito de influencia.

Forma organizando periódicamente cursos para actualizar y ampliar los conocimientos técnicos y legislativos de los colegiados, de sus empleados y colaboradores, así como la posibilidad de obtener un título propio online a través de la Universitat de València con la obtención del Diploma de extensión universitaria en Estudios Inmobiliarios.

Informa al consumidor y usuario de sus derechos y obligaciones.

Name: Kindred Works
Description:
Kindred Works develops and manages purpose-built rental housing that isbeautiful, low-carbon, and desirable to live in. As a certified B Corp, our projects are responsible investments and contribute to livable cities, creating long-term stable returns for our investors and lasting benefits to the communities in which we work.Working with multiple landowners across Canada allows Kindred Worksto operate at scale, including systems-basedthinking, smart technology, innovation,and continuous learning to enhance the projects we build. 

With 17 active residential projects, we plan to deliver 20,000 rental homes by 2037 and pledge to fulfil the goals of the Paris Agreement to combat climate change by developing for a low-carbon future, ensuring that all developments are carbon neutral by 2030. 

We believe that rental housing can provide the kind of lasting security and stability that individuals and communities need so they can realize their potential. Since we develop and manage each of our projects, we can make long-term impacts that drive positive change in the industry and community.

Name: L&Q Estates
Description:
L&Q Estates has over 60 years of expertise in unlocking and delivering strategic development sites. Our portfolio contains developments ranging from 25 to 6,500 houses, together with a variety of commercial sites.

For every opportunity, our approach is tailored to meet the unique requirements of the site, but always grounded in our underlying philosophy; to minimise risk, maximise returns and deliver exceptional quality developments.

Name: Oslo Bolig- og byplanforening
Description:
Norsk BOBY er et tverrfaglig møtested og forum for boligspørsmål og planlegging i Norge. 

Bli medlem via denne nettsiden: https://boby.no/bli-medlem/ og bli en del av vårt faglige nettverk - og motta PLAN til medlemspris!

Oslo Bolig- og Byplanforening (Oslo BOBY) er en avdeling i Norsk Bolig- og Byplanforening (Norsk BOBY). Oslo BOBY har månedlige fagmøter på Kulturhuset i Oslo — sjekk ut hele programmet på nettsiden vår!

Name: Markanvisning.se
Description: Markanvisning.se är plattformen för Sveriges kommuner och företag inom samhällsbyggnadsbranschen. Plattformen syftar till att förbättra matchningen av kommunal mark med aktörer och samtidigt bidra till ett mer hållbart stadsbyggande.

Name: Ruchira Projects
Description:
Welcome to Ruchira, where every home tells a story, your story. It’s not just about walls and roofs; it’s about creating a space that resonates with you and is aligned with the life you aspire to live.

The journey of Ruchira began with Mr. Ram Goniguntla, an IIM Bangalore alumnus, whose vision was rooted in a deep, instinctive understanding of what makes a home truly profound. For him, building a home is not just about constructing walls; it’s about crafting a space that mirrors your values and supports your growth. It’s about creating a narrative that starts with you and evolves with you.

With every project, we blend decades of expertise with a spark of innovation, always with one focus, you. Each corner of our designs is thoughtfully crafted to encourage connection, harmony, and sustainability, all while being rooted in your needs, your desires, and your aspirations.

Here, you won’t just live; you’ll thrive, and your home will continue to grow and evolve alongside you.

Name: Fincamex Hogares Fuertes
Description:
En Fincamex acompañamos a las familias mexicanas en la construcción de su patrimonio, brindando soluciones de vivienda, seguras y de calidad.

A través de desarrollos bien planeados y programas de financiamiento flexibles, acercamos a más personas la oportunidad de tener un hogar propio y mejorar su calidad de vida.

Con presencia en distintas regiones del país, somos parte de un grupo con más de 36 años de experiencia en el sector inmobiliario y financiero, comprometidos con el bienestar y el crecimiento de México.

Name: Hexagon Housing Association
Description:
Hexagon is a housing association based in South East London. Hexagon was founded in 1990.

We work closely with local authorities to ensure that we complement and help to deliver their strategic housing needs.  We are regulated by the Regulator for Social Housing (RSH) which is a Government agency responsible for registering and monitoring.

We own over 4,000 homes across five London Boroughs and provide for a range of different needs:

the majority of the properties are for general needs tenants and are managed directly by Hexagon;
we employ 120 staff.

We have a development programme in excess of 400 new homes over a four year period, utilising grant from the Government (the Greater London Authority or GLA) and a moderate Shared Ownership programme.

We provide housing and care for people in the greatest need who cannot afford other ways of getting a home and have been nominated by their local Council housing. We are governed by a remunerated Board of 12 people which currently meets six times a year. There are also three sub-committees: Audit & Risk; Remuneration; and Performance Management.

Name: SEED Homes
Description:
The SEED Homes Story

The SEED Homes concept was born out of a conversation around transitional housing at a director’s meeting of a charity focused on giving a hand up to those in need.

It took six months to get to the development idea of the home design.  It had to be fast to install, affordable, and fill the gap between the tiny home market (under 400 square feet) and the traditional build that has pushed many people away from the dream, and some might say the right, to own a home.

“SEED Homes is not simply unfolding a house. It is lifestyle freedom.”

Sustainable | Environmental | Economic | Dwellings

Name: Redcon International
Description:
REDCON Construction

Celebrating 30 years of excellence, REDCON Construction is a leading EPC company in Egypt. Our diversified portfolio includes investments in construction, IT, GRC fabrication, steel, ready-mix production, and real estate development. 

We are committed to sustainability, social responsibility, and high standards across all our projects. 
Expanding into Saudi Arabia, we're excited to contribute to Vision 2030 by partnering with RYADA HOLDING INVESTMENT GROUP. This collaboration aims to deliver large-scale projects, including residential, hospitality, retail, and office spaces.

Our vision is to be listed among the top 250 global construction companies by 2025, with a strong presence in the electrical, water, civil, telecommunication, oil and gas, and real estate sectors in the Middle East and beyond.

Join us as we build a sustainable future and shape the construction industry's next chapter.

Name: Contender America
Description:
Contender America is a diversified real estate development company, dedicated to creating long-term value across multiple asset classes—including land development and infrastructure, hospitality, storage, and more. With the capability to manage every phase of land asset development—from acquisition and infrastructure planning to community design and residential construction—Contender America now offers end-to-end solutions that transform raw land into thriving and valuable real estate assets.
As a trusted partner to investors, business leaders, and communities, we combine deep industry expertise with a commitment to transparency, quality, and growth. Our proven track record includes collaborations with leading national builders and the successful development of high-impact projects throughout the Southeastern United States. With a consistent brand and a focus on innovation, Contender America delivers strategic solutions and sustainable results for all stakeholders.

Name: GEBALIS
Description:
Criada em 1995, é uma empresa local de promoção do desenvolvimento local que tem como objetivo a promoção e gestão de imóveis de habitação municipal.

Assegura uma política de gestão integrada, que visa a administração dos bairros, a qualidade de vida das populações residentes e a conservação do património. 

A GEBALIS tem como missão gerir eficazmente os Bairros com uma forte perspetiva de desenvolvimento e integração social, educação ambiental, conservação do património e integração profissional da população.

Name: DHIC
Description: Established in 1974, DHIC is the Research Triangle’s market leader in affordable housing development. Since its founding 50 years ago, DHIC has developed over 3,500 affordable apartments in 49 communities across 9 counties, providing homes for more than 4,000 individuals, children, and seniors. DHIC’s Homeownership Center has helped more than 2,000 families purchase their first homes. DHIC has received numerous awards in recognition of its excellence in design, community service, and management. DHIC is one of four NeighborWorks America organizations in North Carolina; a HUD-approved counseling agency; a member of the Housing Partnership Network; and a CORES Certified organization.

Name: Desire an Irresistible Circular Society
Description:
How do we take the green transition to the next level? In the Desire project - Designing the irresistible circular society - we believe that making the green transition desirable and irresistible is key when it comes to changing how we build and live in cities. 

Desire is part of the European Commission's New European Bauhaus initiative to bridge the worlds of art, culture, and education with science and technology in the green transition – and to get a new generation of green-social builders and artists involved. Desire is one of six lighthouse demonstrator projects financed by the EU.

From the start, we have developed a Desire manifesto with five Desire design principles for the green transition. Keywords: Circularity, Belonging, Biodiversity, Movement, and Aesthetics. 

We have tested the principles at eight existing demonstration sites, three in Denmark (Herlev, Høje-Taastrup, and Kalundborg), two in Italy (Milan and Turin), and one in each of the other countries: The Netherlands (Amsterdam), Slovenia (Ljubljana), and Latvia (Riga). 

In November 2024 at the end of the project, Desire's website was transformed into a Digital Learning Hub, where we share results and tools and give recommendations for making the green transition desirable based on values of sustainability, inclusion and aesthetics to help policymakers make the right decisions, companies develop their businesses and citizens to be engaged in the green transition. 

Visit our homepage to learn more.
Location: Copenhagen, The Capital Region of Denmark

Name: Rental Protection Fund
Description:
The Rental Protection Fund enables non-profit, Indigenous-led and cooperative housing providers to acquire and manage residential buildings to secure their affordability and sustainability for the long-term.

We put people first, and our impact is centered around tenant protection. We hope to stablize affordability, keep families in their homes and continue to grow the community housing sector.

--
We gratefully acknowledge the financial support of the Province of British Columbia through the Ministry of Housing.

Name: Sydhavnens Festival
Description:
Sydhavnens festival er en bydelsfestival, hvor kvarterets aktører åbner op for hele byen og for hinanden i fællesskab.

Festivalen finder sted den 3. weekend i august, hvor det gamle industrikvarter i Sydhavnen danner rammerne og musikere, kunstnere, iværksættere, foreninger, væresteder, institutioner og virksomheder skaber indholdet og inviterer dig indenfor til to fantastiske dage.

Bag initiativet er foreningen Sydhavnen, som samler alle aktører i området og fungerer som bindeled til dette kvarter.

El nostre treball combina:

·Formació i capacitació per a grups, entitats i administracions que volen conèixer i impulsar aquest model.

·Recerca i incidència política, generant coneixement i promovent polítiques públiques per al dret a l’habitatge.

·Gestió de sòl i promocions col·lectives, facilitant l’accés a espais i fent viables noves iniciatives.

·Xarxes i comunitats col·laboratives, treballant conjuntament amb moviments socials, administracions i cooperatives per enfortir l’ecosistema de l’habitatge cooperatiu.

A La Dinamo volem contribuir a transformar el sistema d’accés a l’habitatge a Catalunya, obrint camins per a una societat més justa, solidària i inclusiva.

Name: Grandmothers' Village Project
Description:
We are a nonprofit intentional community org. to house our grandmothers/fathers) in an environmentally safe and ecologically sound “Grandmothers’ Village.”

Our mission:
To foster a healthy and sustainable environment; promote social, economic, and spiritual justice, and strengthen democracy for our BIPOC Elder Women/Grandmothers.

Our Vision
The Grandmothers Village Project will be a not-for-profit organization founded to uphold and provide respect, honor, and care for our Elders, centering on our Grandmothers. By providing living spaces for a growing community of like-minded, spirit-centered, and health-minded Elders, we will provide the means for our Elders to live independently and sustainably, with ceremony and spirit at the core.

The Wisdom Tree Village will be our flagship community of Grandmothers (and some grandfathers) that live a spiritually-based life, located in Massachusetts. Our community will build self-sustaining/eco-based homes, and be conscientious and considerate of environmental impact and health within our infrastructure. We will work toward being an off-grid community, as well as, growing our own organic food and medicine. Ceremony will be at the heart of this community. This will be a place where Elders are honored for their wisdom and will have ceremonial space to share their wisdom both within and outside the community. Diversity of traditions will be honored and welcomed here. At the center, and common ground will be a deep respect and love for the land and nature, a knowing that we are all connected: Humans, Animals, Plants, the Earth, the Waters, the Air, and all they contain; and a dedication to live in a manner that is good medicine for all involved.

Our Values
The highest integrity in every endeavor and interaction.
Honor and respect for our elders, the land, and all of nature.
Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion of all BIPOC identified people.
Commitment to the land, good health, zero carbon emissions
Spiritual Animism philosophy

Wij onderscheiden ons van andere maatschappijen door een extra element te integreren in onze projecten: duurzaam bouwen. Door in te zetten op duurzaamheid creëren we een duidelijke meerwaarde, zowel voor de huurder (die een comfortabele woning met lage energierekening krijgt) als voor de gemeenschap (door minder natuurlijke bronnen te verbruiken en minder schadelijke stoffen uit te stoten). Niet alleen door initiatieven te nemen om de sociale cohesie te versterken (door o.a. buurtfeesten), maar ook door huurders te begeleiden in het vinden van een duurzame levensstijl.

Description:

Jubilee works toward our mission through the revitalization of housing and community assets, including the Midtown Center, a historic building owned by Jubilee.  The Midtown Center is a community hub currently home to 11 non-profit organizations, all local and serving the Holland community.

Our current projects include the development of two mixed-income pocket communities, Park Vista Place and Vista Green. In partnership with the Lakeshore Habitat for Humanity, Park Vista Place consists of 10 newly built houses whose buyers range from households of 30%-120% of the area median income.  Vista Green is a progressing development that will consist of 42 single-family houses and townhouses with the same goal as Park Vista Place of becoming a mixed-income, affordable housing development. 

Jubilee's target population are buyers who range from 80%-120% of the area median income and the Lakeshore Habitat for Humanity's target population ranges from 30%-80% of the area median income.  We are working together on the Park Vista Place and Vista Green developments to create mixed-income, affordable housing pocket communities in Holland.

Met behulp van een netwerk van partners faciliteren en ontzorgen wij het volledige proces. Samen met projectontwikkelaars, gemeenten en woningcorporaties brengen wij vraag en aanbod bij elkaar om tot succesvolle bouwprojecten te komen. Door beheer en onderhoud gestuurd te werken bieden wij de bewoner lange garanties en lage kosten. Alle woningen die wij met onze partners aanbieden voldoen aan de norm van Passief Bouwen en hebben een ruim 75% lagere energiebehoefte dan traditioneel gebouwde woningen. In het buitenland is, zoals bijvoorbeeld in Schotland en Vancouver, Passief Bouwen verplicht. Het wordt tijd dat in Nederland het aanbod veranderd en zorgen voor goede woningen, zorgeloze energierekeningen en klimaat positief worden.
