Perennial Management a deep history in property management. We concentrate our services in the greater Minneapolis/St. Paul areas as well as communities within 50 miles of the downtown areas.

Our management portfolio encompasses affordable housing developments, market rate apartments, and apartment buildings that also have commercial spaces. In the affordable housing arena, Perennial is recognized for its knowledge and compliance management.

Perennial Management preceded by Perennial Properties, Inc., has been involved in property management for over 30 years. The firm remains strong in its commitment to meet owner goals and the needs of residents in the buildings in its management portfolio.

Name: Virginia Association of Housing and Community Development Officials
Description:
The Virginia Association of Housing and Community Development Officials (VAHCDO) is dedicated to preserving and maintaining Virginia’s existing housing resources; improving the health and living standards of the people of Virginia; and developing new housing opportunities throughout the Commonwealth. VAHCDO represents agencies and localities that use federal, state and local funds for the development, construction and maintenance of housing. 
VAHCDO plays an important role in the formulation of housing and redevelopment policy in the Commonwealth of Virginia. Such policy influence results from the strength and support of our member agencies and the effective leadership of Board of Directors. VAHCDO representatives interact regularly with the Virginia Housing Development Authority (VHDA), Virginia Department of Housing and Community Development (VDHCD), Virginia Housing Study Commission, and the Virginia Office of the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD). VAHCDO has also joined with the Virginia Housing Coalition (VHC) in addressing policy issues of mutual concern such as the Low-Income Housing Tax Credit Program.
Since 1971, VAHCDO has assisted Virginia's redevelopment and housing authorities and local government housing and community development departments in promoting affordable housing opportunities and vibrant communities. 
VAHCDO is organized as a non-profit organization under Section 501(c)(3) of the IRS Code with a mission designed to support its members' efforts to improve the health and living standards of Virginia's citizens, encourage community development, eliminate substandard and inadequate housing, eradicate slums, blighted or deteriorating areas, and promote other such actions to help achieve the goal of providing a decent home and suitable living environment for Virginia's families.

Name: NeighborWorks Sacramento
Description:
NeighborWorks Sacramento is part of a national non-profit that has been committed to partnering with underrepresented communities to build healthy, sustainable, and equitable neighborhoods through education, housing development, empowerment, and advocacy since 1987. 

We offer housing counseling, workshops, loan services, self-help housing, down payment assistance, new construction, and community events that have been pivotal in bridging the gap between the desire to be a homeowner and receiving a new set of keys. 

We turn dreams into reality.
⠀⠀

Name: Langston Hughes Affordable Housing, Inc.
Description: Langston Hughes Affordable Housing, Inc. (LHAH) is a 501(c)(3) owner and developer of Affordable and Attainable Housing in the Southeast and Southwest United States.

Name: Hornsey Housing Trust
Description:
Hornsey Housing Trust provides affordable housing and support services in the London Borough of Haringey that enable older people to live full, independent lives in the community of their choice. Our aim is to meet the current and future needs of older and vulnerable people in Haringey by providing quality homes, excellent, value for money services and placing tenants at the centre of everything we do. We provide both housing and some level of support to a wide variety of people of different ages and from different cultures and ethnic backgrounds, mostly in the Hornsey, Crouch End, Tottenham and Highgate areas of the borough.

The Trust was established to support old age pensioners and we have retained our specialism of housing older people: the current minimum age at which someone can be allocated a Hornsey Housing Trust home is 45 and the average age of a Trust resident is 70. A third of our housing stock consists of sheltered housing, offering scheme-based accommodation with on-site support staff and additional services to our more vulnerable residents.

Name: wonderlife
Description:
wonderlife – Exploring the Future of Living

wonderlife is an experimental platform exploring what the future of living could look like. As the way we live, work, and connect continues to shift, we’re asking bold questions about how our neighbourhoods, homes, and communities need to evolve to meet new social, economic, and environmental realities.

We see neighbourhoods not just as places we live, but as living systems that shape how we relate to each other, to work, to nature, and to purpose. Wonderlife brings together deep experience in building large-scale coworking and innovation communities and applies that thinking to housing, urban design, and shared infrastructure.

Through concept development, prototyping, and thought leadership, we explore ideas such as intergenerational living, circular neighbourhood models, localised economies, and life-centred planning. We look at how technology, design, and human values can intersect to build more resilient, inclusive, and connected places.

Wonderlife is not a commercial venture. It’s a sandbox for ideas, a space for asking better questions, and a home for white papers, frameworks, and collaborative experiments focused on the future of how we live — and how we might live better, together.

Name: Chartered Institute of Housing (CIH) South East and London Regional Group
Description:
We are a collective group of housing professionals based in the South East, providing a space for professional CIH peer support and promotion of social housing as a career of choice. 

Our group consists of members from Local Authorities, Housing Associations, Contractors and Consultants who are passionate about the housing sector and professional development.

Our group is open to any CIH member, so if you’re interested in getting involved or would like any further information please get in touch.

Name: Vital Haus
Description: Vital Haus specialises in the development and management of high end mix-use residential projects across Victoria.

Name: TIC Mould Control
Description:
TIC Mould Control offers the only self-deliver mould control system in the UK and Ireland that kills surface mould and spores making properties safe.

Our innovative system eradicates airborne and surface mould in under one hour. Its user-friendly design and traceable treatment process offers a cost-effective solution. By enhancing property health and safety, TIC Mould Control represents a significant leap in property maintenance technology, ensuring a safer living environment for occupants and a safer working environment for staff.
 
Verified and certified, the system demonstrates its reliability and effectiveness, significantly contributing to occupant welfare by maintaining a safe and healthy living environment. TIC makes safe the treatment area enabling staff to work, free of breathing in harmful spores.

Our bespoke mobile app measures and records treatment times, generating certification for auditing and traceability. TIC uses a premixed chemical, which is safer for operator handling, making the process user-friendly and cost-effective. This approach improves indoor air quality by reducing spore count and enhancing the occupants experience by minimising the time that they are unable to enter the area being treated. 

Our goal is to create safe living environments, prevent mould recurrence, enhance lives, and support organisations in mitigating litigation risks.

Name: Jessie My Love Foundation
Description:
Jessie My Love Foundation is a regenerative rural development initiative centering Black and Indigenous women and their families through land, healing, housing, and dignified work.

We are building an intergenerational model of rural renewal that integrates ecological farming, food production, trauma-informed education, land stewardship, and economic equity, all grounded in cultural safety and respect for ancestral knowledge. From seed to soil, from childcare to income generation, we offer a place where women can rise, rebuild, and reclaim their futures on their own terms.

Founded in 2024, Jessie My Love Foundation is redefining what rural can be. We’re not a charity, we are a living blueprint for what justice looks like when it’s rooted in land and led by those who’ve been systematically denied it.

What we do:
•Provide housing and land-based apprenticeships for Black and Indigenous women and families
•Operate a regenerative farm with wraparound services including childcare, healing supports, and dignified work 
•Design and deliver programming that supports cultural reclamation, financial literacy, and self-governance
•Partner with government, philanthropy, and community leaders to reimagine sustainable rural infrastructure

We are currently in the early stages of land acquisition and community mobilization, focused on Ontario, with long-term plans to scale across Canada and beyond. This is more than a project, it’s a promise.

If you’re interested in being part of something sacred, strategic, and revolutionary, we invite you to follow our journey.

Name: SociallyHomes
Description:
We connect people and resources working to resolve homelessness across the public, private, third and voluntary sectors.We deliver accessible framework, strategic guidance and operational toolkits, alongside professional community support networks and our digital events calendar.
We promote ethical, compassionate and sustainable social and supported housing solutions and we ensure they safely reach vulnerable individuals, all over the UK.

Name: Parklane Foundation
Description: The Parklane Foundation helps to build better futures within local communities through fundraising initiatives, community-inspired projects and national charity partnerships.

Name: LeSar Holdings Inc.
Description:
Advancing Solutions to Intractable Societal Challenges

With a diverse range of subject matter expertise and an unwavering commitment to equity, access, and excellence, the LeSar portfolio of companies is uniquely positioned to drive the systemic changes and capacity building necessary to address our housing affordability and homelessness crises. 

We partner with clients across public, private, non-profit, and philanthropic sectors to solve complex problems and implement solutions, enabling them to deliver positive change in their communities and for their stakeholders.

Comprised of LeSar Development Consultants, the Global Policy Leadership Academy, and LeSar Support Services, our teams leverage expertise, experience, and creativity across disciplines to support clients in solving complex, multi-disciplinary challenges that result  in physically, economically, and environmentally resilient and inclusive communities.

Led by market-facing leaders Jennifer LeSar (Founder/CEO), Christina Mun (Chief Strategy Officer), and Rachel Ralston (Chief of Staff), we cultivate collaboration and drive innovation to present holistic solutions to the market.

Name: Méti-Cité
Description:
Méti-Cité, le Cabinet d’Urbanisme spécialisé pour le Logement Accompagné.

Chez nous, l'urbanisme rencontre l'innovation pour façonner le logement et l'urbain de demain de manière sociale, inclusive et durable.  En tant qu'experts passionnés, nous mettons notre savoir-faire au service des territoires et des acteurs clés tels que les bailleurs de logements sociaux, les collectivités territoriales, et les entreprises du secteur médico-social et de l'économie sociale et solidaire.

 Notre expertise au service des acteurs des territoires 

 Projet sur mesure, impact maximal ! 
 Logement social et accompagné
 Habitat spécifique
 Proximité
 Structure médico-sociale

 Nos services pour optimiser vos projets :
1 Étude et diagnostic
2 Assistance à Maîtrise d'Ouvrage (AMO)
3 Évaluation de dispositifs
4 Recherche de financements dédiés
5 Formation

 Prêt à concrétiser votre vision ? Ensemble, construisons un avenir où chaque individu trouve un chez-soi adapté à ses besoins. En unissant nos forces, nous pouvons non seulement façonner des espaces urbains innovants et durables, mais aussi créer des communautés inclusives où chacun peut s'épanouir pleinement.

Suivez nous pour rester au courant de nos projets et de notre expertise !

Name: Outside The Box Consulting (OTBC, LLC)
Description:
Our professional experience has allowed us to work alongside the nation’s top developers to open numerous award-winning communities. With 14+ years of experience in master-planned communities, OTBC, LLC brings expert industry knowledge in community operations from conception to transition. We specialize in amenity and community management operations, agrihoods, grand openings, award-winning lifestyle programming, 5K runs, virtual events, COVID-19 health and safety protocols, and alcohol licensing.
We help both developers and HOA Management Companies or self-managed communities. Our services help ease the burden on your company, community, either temporarily or permanently.  We understand the market today, and we also know that we hold a unique skill set that can assist. We provide a fresh perspective.

Name: Great States Development
Description:
At Great States Development, our dedication, determination, and teamwork drive our passion for shaping and revitalizing communities across the Midwest. Based in Fargo, North Dakota, trust and hard work form the bedrock of every project we undertake. Our commitment to excellence guides us in every interaction with our partners and clients.

As investors first, we approach each project with a deep understanding of every opportunity and a determination to maximize its impact on the community. Whether collaborating with investors, communities, or local businesses, we are dedicated to creating opportunities for shared success in the heartland.

From development to management, from financing to marketing, our team possesses the expertise to handle every aspect of the real estate lifecycle. Our mission goes beyond real estate. We are passionate about creating spaces that foster thriving communities, enriching lives, and leaving a lasting impact. Together, let's build not just buildings but legacies that stand the test of time.

Name: Neighborhood Finance Corporation (NFC)
Description:
NFC provides unique lending programs and other services to facilitate neighborhood revitalization in Polk County and Cedar Rapids, Iowa through partnerships with residents, governments, community based organizations and the business community. 

To date, NFC has provided more than $250,000,000 in loans and grants in its lending area.

Name: Northaven Senior Living
Description:
Northaven is a community that seniors can call “home.”

Northaven Senior Housing provides low-income and affordable housing for people seeking an independent living and or an assisted living community. Located near Northgate Shopping Center, seniors have access to shops, restaurants and many other activities. Seniors have apartments that are spacious and private with easy accessibility.

Northaven's community-based housing and supportive services are meant for seniors to enjoy every day. With activities like brain fitness, exercise classes, educational events and bus trips, seniors are sure to meet new friends and keep active. There are also places to have a quiet moment to read a good book in the library or visiting with friends in the community spaces.

Northaven's staff is committed to providing great service. Many members of the team have worked at Northaven for more than 10 years. They enjoy what they do and provide continuity of care for all of our residents. As one of our team members said, “We come to work every day in our residents’ home. They do not live in our facility.”

Our community is primarily made up of seniors of modest means. We are unpretentious, caring and full of life. Our residents participate in an array of activities and participate in the world around them through drives for the local foodbank, collecting supplies for school children and knitting caps and more for programs that distribute clothing in the neighborhood.

Name: London Ebor Developments
Description:
London Ebor Developments is a York based property development Company also with offices in Central London, dealing in the sale of residential, as well as commercial and industrial property.

Our residential homes offer modern design and are built using the most appropriate methods of construction incorporating many of the latest features.

Our range of homes starts from studio flats for first time buyers to spacious large residences for families primarily in and around the York and Harrogate areas, but covering other areas of Yorkshire and extensively now in prime Central and West London.

Our commercial and industrial property developments are mainly offices and warehousing - mostly available to buy but also available for rent

The Company was formed in the 1987 by Fred Brown who is still heavily involved and oversees a dynamic team of executives who run the company day to day.

As active developers we are always seeking new sites and if you are aware of any suitable sites or buildings in London or Yorkshire, we would be pleased to hear from you. info@londonebor.com

Name: HESTIA BUREAU D'ÉTUDES TECHNIQUES
Description: Conception et Réalisation de plans, Réalisation de plans et suivi Permis de Construire, Plans DCE, CCTP, Chiffrage TCE spécialisé dans la Réhabilitation de l'Habitat, dossier de consultation des fournisseurs et des sous-traitants, Assistance.

Name: PED-ACT
Description:
PED-ACT: Auto characterization of PEDs for digital references towards iterative process optimisation

Positive Energy District (PED) is important for city development. PED-ACT focuses on process optimization for enhanced stakeholder cooperation and reinforced decision-making. PED-ACT contribute to the standardisation of the database of existing PEDs (planned, designed or implemented) with a focus on Sweden, Turkey and Austria. PED-ACT further learns from the database and creates digital PED references by mapping stakeholders’ needs and priorities in the cities of Borlänge (Sweden), Umeå (Sweden), Ankara (Turkey), Karşıyaka (Turkey) and the county of Lower Austria (Austria). PED-ACT pursues bilateral learning and co-design strategies among different cities for wide replication of PEDs towards ‘climate-neutrality and smart cities’ goals.

Name: Pop-Up Housing Innovation
Description:
Mission: Kickstarting bottom up infrastructure in low income, marginalized communities.
Slotted Angle Architecture
0 waste construction system 
Building Light on the Planet. Democratising Self-Build Housing using the $100 House Kit System. A modular industrial shelving framing system a.k.a Slotted Angle Iron Frames adapted to create habitable spaces and New age housing Solutions for Emerging needs.
Tiny House, Green House, Solar Dryer, Micro Housing, rooftop Housing, Micro Sheds Et Al.

Name: Laraix Developers
Description: Laraix Developers, a division of the esteemed Laraix Group, has a rich legacy of excellence across diverse industries, including pure drinking water, food production, and plastic manufacturing. Established in 2016, the Laraix brand has become a trusted name in the UAE, known for its commitment to quality and innovation. Building upon this solid foundation, Laraix Developers is now poised to make a significant impact in Dubai's real estate sector. Our inaugural project, Zyra Vista Residences, is strategically located in the vibrant community of Al Warsan. This development offers modern, meticulously designed homes that cater to professionals and families seeking a harmonious blend of luxury and affordability. With a focus on delivering exceptional living experiences, Laraix Developers continues to uphold the group's tradition of trust, quality, and customer satisfaction.

Name: Dōmad
Description:
En Dômad, estamos redefiniendo el futuro de la vivienda.
Somos una empresa de tecnología especializada en la fabricación de viviendas modulares de lujo, listas para habitar en solo 7 días, sin arquitectos ni procesos constructivos tradicionales.
Dos líneas de producto, un mismo ADN disruptivo:
Casas de lujo todo terreno: móviles, removibles, resistentes y perfectas para uso recreativo en cualquier entorno. Diseñadas con interiores de alta gama y mínimo mantenimiento.
Casas residenciales de lujo: diseño vanguardista, confort superior y acabados premium para quienes buscan estilo y funcionalidad en su día a día.
Construcción inteligente y sostenible
Utilizamos materiales reciclados y tecnologías como sistemas solares y biodigestores. Apostamos por un modelo más limpio, eficiente y respetuoso con el entorno. Desde la península de Yucatán para todo México, en Dômad facilitamos “estar donde quieres estar”, con un hogar elegante, funcional y listo para el futuro.

Name: earthē
Description:
Better Home. Better Health. Better Planet.
Your everyday getaway! 

Earthē & Co is a multifamily, resort-like hybrid that addresses the fundamental shifts in today's market. 

Flexible. Accessible. Eco-Luxury Living +  Members Club

Earthē is a great place to land, designed to simplify and enhance life for our members though community-driven, tech-enabled, eco-conscious living spaces. 

Our branded members club offerings provide our members with the opportunity to reset and refresh their mind, body and wellbeing, anywhere, anytime. Members can live, visit, work and play with flexible leases to designer-quality accommodations , welcoming workstations and an itinerary full of rejuvenating activities. We invite you to move more, play more and create healthier habits.

Our urban eco-living locations are in inspiring places, connecting you to nature-rich environments while still being near dining, shopping, fitness, entertainment and employment options.
 
You'll enjoy an impactful and playful way to live, work and connect, when you stay, earthē .
 
be well live mindful, stay earthē

Name: BE WEST
Description: We believe that buying a new home doesn't need to be that stressful, we want to cut the jargon and pave the way for an effortless experience. We offer truly impartial advice and support from genuine experts, who put your needs above all to help you get you settled in your new home and onwards to a brighter future.

Name: PHX Hills Equestrian Estate
Description:
To be known in the Greater Kansas City Community and beyond as a best-in-class equine therapy, recreational facility, and event space that provides patients, members, and guests unforgettable experiences utilizing amenities and services in a peaceful, natural setting that will ultimately enrich the lives of individuals. 

Our vision is to be a guiding light to inspire and heal the minds and hearts of individuals and communities.

Our mission is to provide unforgettable experiences utilizing amenities and services in a peaceful, natural setting that will ultimately enrich the lives of individuals and whole communities.

Name: Northern Circle Indian Housing Authority (NCIHA)
Description:
Northern Circle Indian Housing Authority (NCIHA) is a Tribally Designated Housing Entity that administers affordable housing, community development, and social service programs on behalf of nine federally recognized Tribes in California through leveraged funding and resources from federal, state, and philanthropic sources to maximize impact and expand housing and community opportunities across Tribal communities. As a TDHE, NCIHA also provides technical assistance to other tribes and tribal entities that support the successful development of communities and programs that promote wellness, preserve culture, foster self-sufficiency, and create lasting resources for families. Guided by its vision to create security and end homelessness for all Native people through hope, healing, and housing, NCIHA serves as a catalyst for building strong, healthy, and self-sufficient Tribal communities.

Learn more about us by visiting: https://nciha.org/ 
Follow us on Facebook & LinkedIn

Name: Self Organised Architecture
Description:
SOA is an action research collaborative working to establish Community-Led Housing in Ireland. We are independent, mission-driven and not-for-profit.

We believe that communities of all sizes should have the right, and the ability, to be instigators and co-creators of secure, affordable homes and that this can benefit individuals, communities and even entire cities. We want people in Ireland to realise this potential.

Name: Heritage Living Group
Description:
Heritage Living unites our expanded capabilities under one elevated brand dedicated to modern, high-quality living environments.

From luxury lodges and glamping accommodation to garden studios, residential SIP-built homes and specialist sports spaces, we deliver design-led modular buildings built around lifestyle, performance and sustainability.

Driven by client feedback and evolving market demand, Heritage Living replaces multiple previous labels with a single, stronger identity. Using advanced Structural Insulated Panel (SIP) construction, curated interiors and a commitment to efficiency and beauty, we create spaces that inspire and endure.

We partner with luxury park operators, homeowners, developers and performance-driven organisations to deliver highly considered, future-proof living and leisure environments.

Name: ARC4Justice: Anti-Racism Center for Justice and Transformative Change
Description: The work of ARC4Justice is to advance a vision of society where the systemic roots of inequities in housing and community systems have been addressed, where power and resources are held and allocated by those who have been most marginalized, where all cultures are appreciated and wholeness, rather than scarcity, is the guiding principle.

Name: Southwest Fair Housing Council
Description: Southwest Fair Housing Council (SWFHC) seeks to achieve and preserve equal access to housing and housing services through the elimination of all forms of illegal discrimination or practices that can discourage, impede, or deny access to housing and housing services for any person or persons.

Name: NPG INDONESIA (ex. PT Nuanu Property Group)
Description:
About NPG Indonesia:
NPG is a residential and commercial real estate developer specializing in the construction of apartments and townhouses on Bali.

We create extraordinary housing complexes that offer modern living in harmony with nature, while generating stable income for investors.

Our focus is on quality, service, and creating long-term value through sustainable real estate developments.

Name: Willow Tree Housing Partnership
Description:
South Western Housing Society and Tamar Housing Society merged on 31st March 2023.

In 2020 two local based social housing providers, South Western Housing and Tamar Housing,  started working together in partnership, as two like-minded organisations looking to harness collective expertise, knowledge and passion for social housing, in order to benefit existing and future tenants.  The merger between the organisations means that there is capability to increase the ability to provide new homes and invest in existing homes.

CEO, Donna Johnson says “As we enter a new phase of our evolution, our focus is clear, to deliver more and for the benefit of the people that we provide services to both now and in the future.  Our vision remains to Deliver & Grow.  Our priorities for the next three years will be centred around four key areas:

People – this includes our current and future tenants, our colleagues and all of the partners we work with in collaboration as a smaller enterprise across the South West.

Property – we will work to ensure we maintain and invest in the homes we own and manage, keeping them safe, and also working to improve their fabric to deliver more sustainable homes for the future.

Place – we want people to enjoy where they live and for the new homes we build to be where people can access services and benefit from green spaces as much as possible.

Environment – we aim to be an organisation that puts sustainability at the heart of what we do, whether that is in the building of new homes, retrofitting existing ones, or limiting our impact on the biodiversity that is so important in supporting the efforts being made to manage climate change.”

Name: Meridian Enclave
Description: Welcome to Meridian Enclave, an upcoming and visionary housing project nestled in the heart of Faisalabad, Pakistan. With a mission to redefine modern living, Meridian Enclave is set to be the epitome of comfort, luxury, and sustainability, offering a harmonious balance between urban living and natural tranquility. Our commitment to excellence and innovation drives us to create a community that surpasses all expectations, delivering a lifestyle that reflects the aspirations of the modern Pakistani family.

Name: VvE Curaçao
Description: VvE Curaçao deelt al haar kennis over VvE’s. VvE’s en hun besturen hebben profijt van deze informatie, voor een onbezorgd woongenot op Curaçao.

Name: Ray Livin
Description: Ray Livin empowers individuals to chase their dreams and foster genuine connections within a community where homeownership is not actual the priority. We redesigned the suburban to ignite supportive connections inviting all to celebrate life's journey together.

Name: Bellamare Development
Description:
Starting as a family office in 2011, Bellamare Development, located in Ridgeland, MS, has grown to a full-service development, management, and Investment Vehicle for accredited investors seeking alternative asset returns in Real Estate. 

Bellamare has consistently achieved 30%+ IRRs across $400MM+ of projects for the last 10+ years. With momentum growing, we have $1.1B of development planned over the next 5 years.

Name: Norfolk Environmental Credits
Description:
NEC provides a streamlined and simplified solution for property developers in Norfolk to meet legally mandated nutrient neutrality standards. Through the purchase of credits developers are able to offset the environmental impact of their projects, protecting water quality in local rivers and catchments.

NEC also invests in nutrient-positive initiatives such as waste water management solutions and environmental projects.

Name: COLAS DEVELOPMENT GROUP
Description: CDG selects unique projects to develop in alignment with the COLAS mission to build legacies and strengthen communities.

Name: Chrysalis Group
Description:
The Chrysalis group is made up of a number of commercial and not for profit organisations with the common mission to safeguard and promote the wellbeing of our service users and communities across the UK, providing high quality, good value, sustainable services and homes, demonstrating that commercial opportunity and activities through the group can underpin our commitment to deliver for social good.
 
The group includes a housing association providing supported living accommodation, a charity supporting women and children escaping domestic abuse, a charity providing support and work skills to clients recovering from drug and alcohol abuse, a commercial maintenance services company and the parent company that provides external consultancy services and support services to the rest of the group.

Name: SPL Loyauté Habitat
Description: Opérateur public en charge du déploiement du programme d'Habitat Social de la Province des Iles Loyauté.

Name: Eastern Affordable Housing Alliance
Description:
The Eastern Affordable Housing Alliance (EAHA) was formed in 2009, uniting six local government authorities in Melbourne’s eastern metropolitan region:
Knox City Council, Manningham City Council, Maroondah City Council, City of Monash, Whitehorse City Council, and Yarra Ranges Council.

Name: Parry Jones Homes
Description: Welcome to Parry Jones Homes, a Shropshire-based property company specialising in small new build developments and the revitalisation of outdated buildings for the rental sector. With a deep connection to our local roots, we take pride in seamlessly blending modern design with the charm of Shropshire's landscapes. Our mission is to create unique living experiences, preserving the region's heritage while providing comfortable and stylish homes for our community. Join us in reimagining the future of Shropshire's properties at Parry Jones Homes.

Name: Lotta Homes - Woningnood oplossing
Description:
Ontwikkelaar van woningconcepten in volkshuisvesting.

Lotta Homes helpt aan het oplossen van de Nederlandse woningnood door snel, effectief en grootschalig duurzame, permanente huisvesting te creëren voor (semi-)overheid. LOTTA is een massief houten eengezinswoning die voldoet aan het Nederlandse bouwbesluit, BENG eisen en is de enige CO2 positief fabrieksmatig geproduceerde eengezinswoning met een MPG score van 0.45.

Name: City of West Des Moines Community & Economic Development
Description:
Housing: The City of West Des Moines works with two housing programs in an administrative capacity: the Dallas County Local Housing Trust Fund and the Metro Home Improvement Program. The department works with the boards of directors or management committees for the different funds to set policy and oversee the operations of the programs. The department also serves as the administrator for the Community Development Block Grant Program funds the City receives through the United State Department of Housing and Urban Development.

Economic Development: The department has the leading responsibility for economic development in the City. Activities include assisting small and large businesses with expansion and growth, business recruitment, marketing the city at a regional and national level, negotiating development agreements, enabling the creation of a diverse economy and employment base for the City, and expanding and diversifying the City’s tax base.

Name: Nempresa
Description:
Nuestro propósito: Desarrollamos hogares que se adaptan a tu estilo de vida.

Nuestra ambición: Que nos conozcan como el desarrollador más adaptado, al comportamiento y las necesidades del momento.

Nuestros Valores:
1. Orden
2. Pasión por lo nuestro
3. A tiempo
4. Resuelve rápido
5.Conexión
6. Compromiso
7. Competitividad

Name: Asociación Civil Cireneos
Description:
Cireneos es una Asociación Civil que trabaja con familias que viven en extrema precariedad a través de los pilares de vivienda digna, educación y fe. 

Trabajamos con misioneros jóvenes que llevan adelante todas las actividades y también con profesionales de corazón que vuelcan su profesión para el servicio de los demás.

Actualmente estamos en Montevideo (Barrio Santa Eugenia y Proyecto Colmena), Canelones (Barrio Costanera), San José (Barrio Viejo Berral y Roberto Mariano) y Melo (Barrio Humberto Pica). 

Con la misión de que todos puedan tener una vida digna, hemos brindado soluciones que abarcan la vivienda transitoria y permanente, así como también otro tipo de propuestas de caracter educativo como apoyo a nivel escolar, liceal y UTU, talleres de capacitación en oficios, Escuelita de Fútbol y Tenis para niños/as, acompañamiento a adultos y jóvenes en necesidades varias y apoyo a quienes se están insertando en el mercado laboral. 

Seguiremos trabajando con la mirada firme en nuestro mayor objetivo: construir un futuro donde todos vivan con dignidad. Juntos hacemos posible lo imposible!

Name: Genesis LA
Description:
Genesis LA’s mission is to deliver financial solutions that advance economic and social opportunities in underserved communities. We work with nonprofits, small businesses, and women and minority-owned enterprises, by delivering capital which builds capacity and transforms communities. 

We believe that community development finance requires close collaboration between lenders and the organizations working on the ground in underserved communities. Therefore, we work hands-on with nonprofits, small businesses, and women and minority-owned enterprises, by blending our financial products with capacity building efforts so that we can more effectively transform underserved communities. This strategic approach is designed to grow and strengthen organizations that serve our Los Angeles market, yet struggle to access the capital they need to expand their delivery of goods and services. Our business model focuses on providing innovative financial solutions for these types of borrowers and their projects, with the goal of growing the next generation of community development infrastructure within Los Angeles County and providing opportunity for new community development models to be tested and hopefully expanded.

Name: Black Housing Policy Network
Description: The Black Housing Policy Network (BHPN) is a leading not for profit organization focused on assessing and analyzing housing and homeownership policies, trends, programs and projects.

Name: Brique par brique
Description:
Brique par brique (BxB), founded in 2016 by community organizers, addresses the need for affordable housing in the gentrifying, multicultural Parc-Extension neighborhood through a social justice lens. Beyond housing, the organization operates a cultural center that fosters solidarity and empowers diverse low-income residents, newcomers, and immigrants, by providing a welcoming space for expression, resource-sharing, and community-building. Through strategic collaborations and community economic development, Brique par brique actively challenges systemic barriers, building a neighborhood where all residents feel seen, valued, and supported.

—

Brique par brique est un organisme à but non lucratif fondée en 2016 par des organisateur.ices communautaires  afin de répondre de manière innovante et dans une perspective de justice sociale au besoin de logements abordables dans le quartier multiculturel de Parc-Extension.
En plus du développement de logements abordables, notre centre culturel est un lieu où nous cultivons la solidarité, les atouts et le potentiel de notre communauté dans le but d'améliorer les milieux de vie des divers résident.e.s à faible revenu. Grâce à nos activités communautaires et nos programmes culturels, nous offrons un espace d'expression accueillant et accessible, dans le but de créer des liens entre les personnes et les organisations, d'encourager l’entraide et de soutenir les talents et les aspirations des résident.e.s de Parc-Extension et d'ailleurs.

Name: People's Housing+
Description:
People's Housing+ is being created through a strategic partnership of three New Orleans based community development organizations: Home by Hand, Crescent City Community Land Trust, Tulane Canal Neighborhood Development Corporation. 

We believe everyone deserves a pathway to home. With more than 40 years combined experience among our merging partners, we envision People's Housing+ as your one-stop affordable shop.

We are Better Together.

Fostering multi-generational wealth through affordable housing and stewardship.

Name: Used Modulars Canada Ltd.
Description:
Used Modulars Canada Ltd., together with Used Modulars USA and Modular Man Camps, is North America’s leading platform for buying, selling, and brokering used industrial modular buildings. 

Founded in 2014 to address a critical gap in the modular resale space, we’ve grown into a trusted partner for clients across industries including energy, construction, mining, hospitality, agriculture, and remote operations. From dormitories and kitchens to recreation units, offices, and full camp systems, we support clients with solutions tailored to the lifecycle of modular infrastructure.

Our business model is fully independent—we don’t manufacture, lease, or operate modular assets. Instead, we act as a strategic connector, helping clients navigate transactions, evaluate inventory, and unlock value through resale, redeployment, or acquisition. With over 30 years of experience in the modular sector, we bring deep market insight and an unmatched network to every engagement.

Our core services include:

Full-service brokerage for used modular and remote assets

Verified third-party evaluations through the Used Modulars Certified Program

Strategic resale and disposition planning

Cross-border visibility across Canada and the U.S.

In special cases, assistance with the custom and/or functional design of retrofit products through our partner vendor network

We work with site operators, EPCs, First Nations, developers, and end-users to meet urgent housing needs, improve asset utilization, and reduce capital waste. 

Whether liquidating surplus buildings or sourcing modular accommodations for a growing workforce, we are committed to delivering practical, value-driven outcomes.

Used Modulars Canada Ltd: Independent. Experienced. Built for your bottom line.

Name: OX Place
Description:
Oxford needs homes and Oxford City Council is committed to building them.

In 2016, Oxford City Council established OX Place as its own housing company to create high-quality, genuinely affordable homes in the City.

At OX Place, we’re responsible for building the homes local people need. Our profits are invested back into the city, to provide essential services for local people, and into building more homes for people who need them.

We build homes you can buy outright, as well as homes you can buy a percentage of, known as shared ownership homes. This scheme gives people the chance to get on to the property ladder by buying a share of a home and renting the rest. With shared ownership you can buy more shares of your home in future. Sometimes you will be able to own it outright.

And we build council-owned rented homes for those who need a safe, high-quality, affordable rental home in the city. In some cases, these are for people who can’t afford to buy, in others, it’s so we can provide affordable homes for key workers, such as nurses, doctors, university researchers and local school teachers who may need to live in the city while they’re training, but then move away.

Name: Uplyfted
Description:
Renewed modular carpet.
We are Uplyfted, your go-to partner for renewed modular carpet. Our flooring also happens to be ultra low-carbon. We specialise in the supply and fit of renewed modular carpet, specifically tailored for social housing providers throughout the UK.

Our story...
Uplyfted by name and nature.
Uplyfted was co-founded by David Catton and Agustina Memoli, two dynamic flooring industry experts with a shared vision for sustainability and social value. Their journey began with a mutual mission to renew modular carpet that has been uplifted after first use and to create environmental and social value for those in social housing and other community initiatives.

Name: BYond Homes Ltd
Description: We build select developments across the South East, in well-connected and desirable locations with a real community feel. Name: One Ontario
Description:
One Ontario is a non-profit organization dedicated to modernizing real estate development processes in Ontario. 

By fostering collaboration among government, industry, and academia, we aim to streamline development approvals, enhance data exchange, and address housing challenges. Leveraging advanced technology, evidence-based research, and strategic partnerships, One Ontario provides practical solutions to create a transparent, efficient, and sustainable development landscape. Our initiatives focus on breaking down silos, simplifying approvals, and promoting sustainable growth to meet the province's housing needs.

Name: Mosaic Land Development
Description:
Mosaic Land Development provides full-service land development to assist our clients in developing their property. We utilize strategic partnerships with engineers, land planners, and market researchers to provide innovative approaches tailored to our clients' needs. We work collaboratively with each client to identify and develop unique, cost-effective solutions that meet both present and future needs.

We offer a full range of services to help developers and home builders manage their development needs.
 
We partner with landowners to help navigate through the development process to maximize and preserve both value and flexibility.

Name: Native Learning Center
Description:
The Native Learning Center (NLC) offers free Training and Technical Assistance to Native Americans and those working within Indian Country. Our training promotes the essentials of The Native American Housing Assistance and Self Determination Act of 1996 (NAHASDA) for Tribal members and their communities. The NLC is a model activity program of the Seminole Tribe of Florida (STOF) Housing Department, and was established in cooperation with the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) and Eastern Woodlands Office of Native American Programs (EWONAP) in 2008.

The Native Learning Center develops curriculum that achieves the primary objectives of NAHASDA supports the EWONAP’s mission of promoting the development of Native housing and housing related activities. The NLC’s housing related training opportunities and resources support the critical growth and improvement of quality of life for Tribal communities while providing participants with best practices that can be shared with their communities.

Name: RoseVilla Senior Living
Description:
RoseVilla Senior Living is a nonprofit, single-site Continuing Care Retirement Community (CCRC), also known as a Life Plan Community. Founded in 1960, we are located on 22 lush acres overlooking the Willamette River in Portland, OR, and bring that natural Pacific Northwest inspiration into our community, both in the vibrant, forward-thinking way our community is built and in the way residents choose to live life.

We have been breaking the mold of traditional senior living since we were founded in 1960. Today, our focus remains as ambitious as ever: We live to improve the lives of our entire community. Not just those who call us home. Not just those inside our walls. Our whole community. We believe that being part of a community isn’t just important—it’s critical. 

We’re singularly inspired by the power of connection and what it can do for those both within and beyond our campus. That inspiration powers a community where self-purpose, independence, and autonomy come standard. 

For millennia, humanity’s very survival relied on community. Life came from sharing. Combining. Doing life together. We believe that even today, it still does. Human connection has the power to transform lives. And we exist to help older adults transform theirs.

Brisbane is more than just a location; it's a vibrant, growing metropolis brimming with opportunities and unique character. Our vision is to contribute to the city's evolution by creating spaces that not only meet the demands of today but also inspire the possibilities of tomorrow.

Name: Minnesota Consortium of Community Developers
Description:
MCCD is an association of nonprofit community development organizations committed to expanding the wealth and resources of neighborhoods through housing and economic development initiatives. For close to thirty years, MCCD members have worked together to increase opportunities for the development of quality, community-based projects through collaborative action on public policy issues, loan fund development, public education efforts, and long-term strategic planning. Through these efforts, the organization has demonstrated the effectiveness and efficiencies gained by a shared vision of cooperation.

The staff of MCCD work to facilitate cooperation and communication among our members and advocate on behalf of the work they do.  A core function of MCCD is to manage a micro-loan program designed to improve capital access for new and growing neighborhood-based businesses.

Our goal is to create 1,000 affordable housing opportunities in Idaho by 2026, including offering homeownership and rental options, as well as preserving existing homes, all tailored to match local wages.

Name: Affordable Housing Solutions
Description:
AHS is an advisory firm specialising in the development of social and affordable housing.

We have a deep understanding of social and affordable housing and the societal and economic influences that contribute to an inequitable housing system.

We bring together participants from private industry, government and the community housing sector to deliver wonderful places to live.

AHS delivers Consulting Services, Capital Advisory and Development Management that are integral to developing and operating social and affordable housing.

We begin with the premise that the success of any affordable housing initiative depends on the level of collaboration between public, private and community sectors and the capacity of the partners to translate their shared social and environmental objectives into commercially viable projects.

We offer an ‘open architecture’ approach, revealing to partners and stakeholders how we solve problems. This facilitates the sharing of information and the opportunity to develop skills and strive for better outcomes.

We work with clients and partners to deliver medium and large scale affordable and mixed-use housing projects that create lasting positive change in our community. We have delivered thousands of social and affordable housing dwellings in Australia.

The AHS team has executive management and board-level experience in the housing industry with complementary skills in property development, property finance and affordable housing operations. Above all, we share a passionate commitment to innovation in the provision of affordable housing.

Name: Carbon Neutral Homes
Description:
At Carbon Neutral Homes Ltd, we believe a home should be more than just a place to live — it should give you:

•	Freedom from energy bills
•	Peace of mind
•	A smaller environmental footprint

Our mission is bold yet simple: to empower residents with homes that eliminate energy bills, reduce carbon emissions, and promote long-term well-being - setting new benchmarks for energy independence, affordability, and environmental impact

The Problem with Today’s ‘Green Homes’

•	Most UK ‘green’ homes are grid-tied and generate less than 30% of the energy they use
•	EPC ratings reward paperwork over real-world performance
•	Dependent on the grid in winter, when renewable generation drops and heating demand spikes
•	Marketed with unverified sustainability claims, contributing to widespread greenwashing
•	Are vulnerable to outages and energy price hikes

The result? A housing sector that talks about net zero but remains locked into the very infrastructure and costs it claims to avoid

Our Approach

We design and deliver homes that:

•	Consistently generate surplus clean energy, 24/7
•	Minimise reliance on the grid through our proprietary dual renewable technology strategy
•	Support healthier living through thoughtful design
•	Deliver verified, measurable performance

Two Critical Market Needs We Address

•	Eradicating fuel poverty by eliminating household energy bills
•	Future-proofing assets against net-zero regulations, rising energy prices, and grid dependency

Your home becomes your own power station—covering all your needs and more

Why Carbon Neutral Homes

With growing demand for sustainable housing and ESG-aligned investments, we offer a profitable and purpose-driven opportunity to build the next generation of energy-independent, resilient homes

Partner With Us

Together, we can scale this model across the UK — delivering environmental impact, social value, and attractive returns

Sustainability built in, not added on. Let’s build the future — Living Beyond Zero

Name: Crosbie Homes
Description:
Crosbie Homes is the private residential arm of the Morris & Spottiswood Group – specialising in the creation of bespoke, high quality homes in desirable environments. 

Hand selecting each of our sites, our team pride themselves in evoking a lifestyle choice within our clients, as well as presenting them with their new property. 

Currently on site within Bearsden, Blanefield and Sandal, our spacious and energy efficient properties all share exceptional standards of build quality and design, set in prime locations with excellent amenities and established communities. 

We understand that a residential property isn’t just a building – it’s a home and we go the extra mile to make it one.

To get in touch with our team, please contact us at https://www.crosbiehomes.co.uk

Name: The Convivia Group
Description:
Influenced by the Latin ‘Convivo’, meaning to ‘live at the same time’, Convivia was established in 2017 by CEO, Roy Ledgister, with a vision to create an inspirational property and tech portfolio that delivers experiential living and brings communities to life.
A private group of companies operating primarily within the public housing sector, Convivia is a new entrant to the market and is committed to inspiring change and creating fun communities, while equalising standards and opportunities across the social and private sector.
Focused on revolutionising the public housing sector through its concept of ‘Convivial Housing’ - very high-quality housing developments that support the physical, mental and educational needs of all tenants.
Convivia’s core focus is people, not just numbers. 
In making luxury accessible to individuals currently living in sub-standard conditions, Convivia plan to extend its philosophy into the wider community and dedicate time to marrying commercial benefit with delivering life changing spaces.
Having already attracted significant investment, Convivia is now looking to scale the business and governance to match our strategy, which will double the size of our property portfolio in each of the next three years.
