Name: Advantage Frost NPO
Description:
Objectives of Sport with our Ambassador, David Frost, South Africa’s own true example and successful 3-time winner of the Nedbank Million Dollar and current Champions Tour Player at the young age of 64.
Using sport is a core vehicle for economic and social development for those marginalised in South Africa through the use of Sport, Technology and bringing the youth and senior generation closer together to offload life experience in many fields of expertise.
Achieving our social development goals through sport as it’s a unifying force and a vehicle for inclusion which contributes to peace, the empowerment of women, marginalised young people and the active senior. 
Connecting the world of sport with the world of development funding is critical. We have a great need to bring the youth and senior generations together using life experience from our retired and active seniors who have a wealth of knowledge they want to share.
This means youth gain mentorship through successes of our active seniors’ life experiences and in turn we need to move away from closed communities where youth do not have access to grow and learn especially in the retirement industry in South Africa.
As a South African platform based in Cape Town, The Advant-Age Frost Foundation aims to promote the support and funding of projects promoting development through sport and social activity between young and active seniors in South Africa.
By taking existing solutions supplemented by a range of tailor-made services, our platform is positioned as a one-stop shop and pursues four main goals:
•	Facilitate and fast-track funding for projects of all sizes (micro, medium-sized and macro)
•	Promote and accelerate support for projects through Legend Sport Coaching and Development
•	The use of technology to streamline development in a more cost-effective way
•	Ensure promotion of inclusivity amongst youth and the active seniors and encourage the sharing of life experiences, knowledge and feedback

Name: STEP UP ACADIANA
Description: STEP UP ACADIANA is a non-profit Housing Counseling agency based in Lafayette, Louisiana. We offer the following services free of charge to local individuals and families: first time homebuyer workshops; financial literacy, home maintenance, and disaster preparedness classes; pre-purchase counseling and foreclosure prevention counseling. We only charge for current Tri-Merge credit reports.

Name: Magers Management Company
Description: Magers Management Company combines form and function with a creative vision and value-added touch to design properties that maximize business value for the user, foster a sense of community and enhance the physical experience and environment for our tenants, their employees and customers. Knowing how powerfully the marketplace rewards new ideas, we approach each project as an opportunity to create imaginative and customer-identified space that is essential to the location, the market, and the community.

Name: SSHCH (CBS)
Description:
Saif Social and Health Care Homes Limited has been providing high-quality, person-centered care since 2014. We specialize in supporting individuals with disabilities, mental health conditions, and complex needs through a variety of services, including supported living, domiciliary care, and community outreach.

Our dedicated and compassionate team works closely with each individual to develop personalized care plans that focus on promoting independence, dignity, and overall well-being. We are committed to creating safe, inclusive, and supportive environments where individuals can thrive.

Our Services Include:
 Supported Living – Empowering individuals to live independently in safe, structured settings.
 Domiciliary Care – Offering personalized care in the comfort of clients' own homes.
 Community Outreach – Providing support that fosters social engagement and inclusion.

At Saif Social, we understand the importance of building strong relationships and creating environments where individuals feel valued and supported. With a focus on compassion, professionalism, and social inclusion, we are dedicated to enhancing the quality of life for everyone we serve.

We are passionate about making a positive impact and ensuring that those in our care have the resources and support they need to live their lives to the fullest.

Name: Studio People
Description:
Studio People solves the polycrisis of 6,6 million unhoused people, unsustainable building materials and 59% youth unemployment in South Africa by offering circular, net-zero and open-source furniture boards and prefabricated housing panels named, TexiBoards.

The very people who suffer from living in shack dwellings and unfurnished homes in flood-stricken areas are the centre of our creative problem-solving. Just having a roof over your head is not enough. A home must shield you from weather, be functional for daily living and never compromise on durability, no matter where you live or how much you earn. However, building materials contribute 37% to greenhouse carbon emissions and are the biggest cost barrier causing unsafe and undignified living conditions.

We have designed a recyclable and multiple-usage building material that can be produced using small-scale manufacturing and repurposes fashion waste. It aims to replace wood-based boards in walls, floors and furniture. Acting as a unifier in all essential stakeholders to solve the housing crises and bring sustainable innovation to revive township businesses in construction and furniture-making.

Our goal is to create an egalitarian and sustainable urban environment, helping shack dwellers and youth out of poverty. We empower the underdog. Including people and businesses, to co-create solutions in our fight against the housing crisis, unsustainable building materials, and unemployment - one board at a time.

Name: Mark Development, Inc.
Description:
Mark Development, Inc. is a real estate development and property management company founded in 1977. Mark Development was formed by Mr. Mark Y. Watase with the sole intent of building affordable housing for the people of Hawaii.

Today, Mark Development, Inc. manages and/or owns 16 properties in Hawaii with 1000+ units.  Our staff is certified with over 40 years of experience in the affordable housing industry.

Name: OnPoint Capital
Description:
OnPoint started as a development company in Boston and has grown into a private real estate investment and asset management firm that acquires and stabilizes large Multi-Family properties in a few key markets throughout the United States. 

We focus on acquiring properties with over 100 units and a value add component that are in markets that show strong fundamentals as well as indicators of long term lasting growth. We create value and force appreciation by making capital improvements, enhancing management and reducing operational inefficiencies. Our goal is to create communities that our residents are proud to call home while generating attractive risk adjusted returns for investors that are supplemented with rental cash flow. In addition to multifamily assets we take on select ground up or infill development projects locally in Boston. 

We believe that investing in multifamily real estate presents one of the most powerful strategies for creating long term wealth. While there are many asset classes in real estate, we choose to focus on value-add and re-development. We work with our investors to align their investment goals with the appropriate asset class.

Name: Hull City Council Warm Homes Team
Description:

Phone - 01482 612908
Email - warmhomesteam@hullcc.gov.uk

Name: Mustard Seed Property
Description:
Mustard Seed Property (MSP) exists purely to serve its beneficiaries: adults who are otherwise at risk of homelessness. This happens by leasing property to highly respected organisations that deliver the services direct to the individual tenants.

Since 2007, more than 30 individuals have been helped in this way, who would otherwise be in homelessness hostels, or other unsuitable housing, or even in some cases, rough sleeping. The positive impact this has on those around them is also very felt, whether as respite for parents coping with grown-up children with learning disabilities or the friends and families of those who would otherwise be in desperate need.

If you’re interested in our share offer and would like to invest please find more information on the Ethex website on the link below.

Name: Woodbury
Description:
Welcome to Woodbury, a residential neighborhood and mixed-use community in the heart of Idaho’s beautiful Palouse region. Woodbury has been developed to be a walkable, bikeable, and liveable neighborhood that is designed with a human-centric approach and is able to cater to the simple things in life. 

Built with timeless design, Woodbury uses the best of what we have learned through American and European architecture and neighborhood design. When you buy in Woodbury, you buy so much more than a home – you are buying a life designed with you in mind.

Name: People First of Santa Cruz County
Description:
Our Belief
Here at People First, our core belief is simple: everyone deserves a place to call home. It's not just a goal; it's our unwavering commitment.

We stand for the dignity of every person facing homelessness, seeing their inherent worth beyond labels. Our doors and hearts are wide open, welcoming collaboration and transparency.

We're breaking boundaries, and nurturing a community where flexibility and creativity drive progress. Our aspirations are grand, but we're rooted in the real world, acknowledging its imperfections.

Setbacks? They're stepping stones. Challenges? Opportunities to fine-tune our strategies. We're relentless, resilient, and dedicated to ensuring housing for all.

Mission
To ensure housing for all by uniting the community, forming partnerships, inspiring advocacy and providing services.

Vision
We envision a future where people are safe, valued and supported.

Values
Transparency, Creativity, Thriving, Compassion, and Accountability for our staff, participants and community as a whole.

Diversity, Equity & Inclusion
We at People First know that diversity, equity, and inclusion must be grounded in creating spaces for everyone, especially the most marginalized and vulnerable among us, to thrive. Our work is centered on the fact that everyone has immense power within themselves, and we all deserve the environment and relationships to be able to recognize and access that power. We value people of all backgrounds, identities, and lived experiences- in fact, embracing that variety is essential to fostering healthy, supportive communities.

Name: Enhance Technical Consulting Ltd.
Description: Development Technical Consultancy that provides a multi product service for all aspects of build and planning within the homebuilding industry. Covering all aspects tailored to landowners, developers and homeowners who need support and guidance from the experts.

Name: Management Plus, AAMC
Description:
Management Plus is a privately owned community management firm.  Its partners are passionate about ensuring the company is meeting the needs of its clients and not simply accepting the status quo. 

Our managers are not property managers.  While this term is commonly used to describe community managers, as it applies to the State of Ohio, a property manager must have a broker’s license and is given decision making authority based on an approved budget by the property’s owner.  A community manager must focus on people, property, and amenities as well as building community.  Community managers take their direction from the Board of Directors.

Management Plus’s strength is in its team!

    We have over 100 years of collective management experience
    Our management team supports one another while servicing individual portfolios
    Our team provides transparent reports on all services
    Our managers provide written manager’s reports at each board meeting
    We have loyal teams!  Several of our team members have been with Management Plus for many years
    We endeavor to meet our clients' needs by providing professional and friendly customer service.

Name: Life Care Assist
Description:
Creating inclusive, supported communities through innovation, integrity, and collaboration.

About Us
At Life Care Assist, we are passionate about changing lives. As a leading NDIS-registered provider operating across Victoria and expanding nationally, our core mission is to empower individuals living with disabilities through thoughtfully designed accommodation, and responsive services.

We believe that everyone deserves to live with dignity, independence, and purpose. Our approach is participant-first, future-focused, and grounded in strong ethical values. Whether we’re developing state-of-the-art homes, everything we do is built on the foundation of choice, inclusion, and respect.

With a team of highly trained professionals, trusted partners, and a strong growth trajectory, Life Care Assist is proud to be a provider of choice in the disability, aged care, and specialist housing sectors.

Name: CarePatrol Twin Cities East & St. Paul
Description:
Senior Housing options can be overwhelming, let us navigate you thru this journey, step by step in person.  Whether being discharged from a hospital or care setting, or if extra care support is needed due to a recent change in a health condition, our 3-step process ensures safety, quality, and peace of mind. CarePatrol Local Senior Care Advisors understand the aging process and how health, social and financial concerns work together and influence outcomes.  Every senior care community we recommend is pre- screened by your local CarePatrol Senior Advisor.   We understand your patient’s or client’s current needs and anticipate future ones before recommending a community.
We stay supportive long after move-in day with regular check-ins to ensure your loved one’s needs are being met even when health conditions change.

Name: Dream of Detroit
Description:
Dream of Detroit is combining community organizing with strategic housing and land development to build a healthy community and empower our neighborhood on the Westside of Detroit. 

We believe that If we can mobilize our residents and allies, improve the existing and build the next generation of the neighborhood’s housing stock, and create an economic corridor along Woodrow Wilson street, we can revitalize the entire upper-east Dexter-Linwood neighborhood. Further, we’ll use a people- and partner-centric strategy that makes sure our current neighbors are leaders and beneficiaries of the area’s transformation even as we bring in new growth and opportunities.

“What’s happening now is we have to come together and build one here in Detroit. That’s how I see the DREAM of Detroit. The fulfillment of A BIG DREAM by a lot of people, the fulfilment of the prayers of a lot of people, with many different histories, and many different roots.” Imam Zaid Shakir, Building a Prophetic Hood (March 4, 2018)

We’ve rehabbed homes throughout our neighborhood and plan to develop our first new residential and commercial properties beginning in 2022. We’re also a founder of the Longfellow Block Club, a member of the Coalition for Property Tax Justice, and we’re allied with partners throughout the neighborhood and across the country. Our work has been featured in local and national media: The Nation, The Detroit Free Press, Crain’s Detroit Business, The Guardian, WDET Detroit 101.9, WBEZ Chicago 91.5, WJR NewsTalk, Sapelo Square, AltMuslim, Mad Mamluks, Diffused Congruence.

We envision a city anchored in its sustainable neighborhoods; a city where homeownership, small business development and cooperative economics, local food sourcing, and access to education and the arts are priorities of every neighborhood; and a city where neighborliness is a shared value.

Name: El Shams for Housing and Development
Description:
شركة الشمس للإسكان والتعمير ش . م . م خاضعة للقانون رقم 159 لسنة 1981م
تم إنشاء الشركة وفقاً للمرسوم الملكي عام 1946 تحت اسم الشركة المصرية  للمباني الحديثة
الشركـة هي حصيلة دمج أربعة شركات عريقة :

1. شركة الإيموبيليا والتي كان يرأسهـا إسماعيل باشا صدقي رئيس وزراء مصر في ذلك الوقت 

2. الشركة المصريـة للمباني الحديثـة 

3. الشركـة المساهمة المصرية لأراضى البناء ( حدائق الأهرام ) والتي كان يرأسها البرنس عباس حليم 

4. شركة المباني الممتازة بالإسكندرية .

بدأت الشركة نشاطها العقاري بممارسة نشاط تأجير العقارات التي تمتلكها الشركة المنتشرة بالأحياء المتميزة في القاهرة الكبرى والإسكندرية .

انطلقت الشركة في التوسع في مجالات الاستثمار العقاري الأخرى متمثلة في الإسكان والتعمير والمشاركة في مجال الاستثمار العقاري والفندقي  و تمتلك الشركه حاليا مجموعه من المشروعات في مدينه السادس من أكتوبر و العاصمه الاداريه الجديده بجانب محفظه مميزه من العقارات التاريخيه في القاهره علي رأسها عمارة الايموبيليا.

Name: Rising Tide Residences
Description: Rising Tide is a shared supportive housing initiative dedicated to helping those who need a second chance. We provide housing for people facing the harsh realities of income instability, credit issues, prior evictions, or criminal records. Our doors are open to those who are often turned away—those who just need someone to believe in them.

Name: Association Réseau HPN
Description:

Name: The LGBTQ+ Housing Pledge
Description:
What is the LGBTQ+ Housing Pledge?

The LGBTQ+ Housing Pledge scheme, by HouseProud and delivered by Stonewall Housing,  provides a comprehensive framework for social landlords to demonstrate their commitment to creating inclusive and safe environments for LGBTQ+ residents. By adhering to this pledge, organisations show their dedication to eliminating discrimination and supporting LGBTQ+ individuals in their communities.

It is a scheme that all social housing landlords in the UK can sign up to and was engineered to work flexibly for all social housing providers, regardless of their size and geographical location. It has been developed by HouseProud and the University of Surrey in association with residents, staff members and sector leaders to address the issues raised by the findings of ‘No Place Like Home?’, a study undertaken to understand LGBTQ+ residents experiences of social housing.

Why is it needed?


The home is of central importance to LGBTQIA+ residents, because it should be a place where people can freely express their sexual orientation and/ or gender identity without fear or prejudice.  Sadly, despite recent changes in equality laws, LGBTQIA+ residents continue to experience discrimination in their everyday.

Are you interested in signing up to the pledge scheme? 

A staff member or resident of a housing provider (such as a housing association, ALMO, or local authority) can express interest in signing up to the pledge.

To express an interest, or simply to find out more, please email our team at HouseProudPledge@stonewallhousing.org

Name: Home As Power Stations (HAPS)

The HAPS approach will adopt energy-efficient systems. It will create homes that are better for the environment and more cost effective to run, using:
	• PV solar panels
	• heat pumps
	• batteries
	• mechanical ventilation heat recovery systems

HAPS' main objective is to reduce a home’s reliance on existing energy networks.

The HAPS project aims to:
	• move thousands of households away from fossil fuel heating systems
	• reduce energy bills
	• provide cleaner and greener alternatives
	• have a positive impact on people’s health, in particular respiratory wellbeing

Over the past decade, through austerity, a pandemic and a cost-of-living crisis, MTVH has renewed its commitment to making a positive difference in our communities. So far, £50 million has been invested in providing homes and improving lives, through MTVH funding and partnerships.

Our ambition over the next ten years is to raise community investment from MTVH funds, fundraising and partnerships to £75 million through the Molly Huggins Foundation. We will prioritise places that lack funding, and work with local people to connect their experiences with external investment.

In 1957, we committed to working together to provide homes and communities. In 2024 and beyond, we are more committed to that vision than ever.

Name: Community Land Trust Association of West Marin (CLAM)
Description:
The mission of the Community Land Trust of West Marin (CLAM) is to provide stable and permanently affordable homes in an environmentally responsible way in the communities surrounding Tomales Bay in Marin County, CA.

As a community land trust, CLAM takes land and housing out of the speculative real estate market and holds it “in trust” to serve as a long-term community asset for housing people.  In historically segregated Marin, with one of the most expensive housing markets in the country, CLAM works to advance racial and economic equity by enabling families to purchase and rent homes affordably, whereby preventing displacement and empowering families and individuals to advance economically and thrive within their communities.

CLAM creates and stewards stable and permanently affordable homes in an environmentally responsible way in the communities surrounding Tomales Bay. Our work crosses issues of economic equity, racial justice, issues related to aging and seniors, affordable housing, and environmental sustainability. By creating stable and affordable places for people to live, we enable individuals and families to build wealth, contribute to their community, reduce carbon emissions (by decreasing commutes for local workers), and reduce stress and other negative health outcomes related to unstable or unsuitable housing.

Established in October 2020, the partnership’s maiden development is a new low-carbon neighbourhood on land north of Lower Cambourne. The partnership is dedicated to the development of high-quality, sustainable homes and communities in the South Cambridgeshire area.

We are excited to share project updates and partnership news.

Name: VIVO²
Description:
Bij VIVO² Development streven we naar projecten voor een wereld waarin we niet met ingehouden adem leven op kruissnelheid, maar een wereld vol zuurstof die óns tempo bijhoudt in plaats van omgekeerd. Een wereld die evolueert van overlevingsmodus naar genietmodus. Waar ademruimte is om te leven, wonen en (samen)werken in een inspirerende en groene omgeving.

Voor die wereld van morgen ontwikkelen wij vandaag.

We ontwikkelen met mensen in gedachten voor een leven vol zuurstof. Woningen waar écht in geleefd wordt en die klaar zijn voor de toekomst, wat die ook zal brengen. Zo streven we naar een duurzaam woonmodel waar iedereen een thuis heeft en dat altijd meegroeit in een continu evoluerende wereld. Hoe we dat doen? Met ons hoofd in de wolken, maar met onze voeten stevig op de grond.

We beseffen immers dat wij als ontwikkelaar mee het stadsleven en de skyline bepalen. Nu, maar ook morgen en de komende decennia. Een verpletterende verantwoordelijkheid waar we bij VIVO² zorgvuldig mee omgaan.

Name: Aunt Leah's Properties
Description:
Spanning over thirty years, Aunt Leah’s has been a steadfast force in supporting youth and young mothers transitioning from foster care. Their comprehensive assistance spans a diverse range of services, including facilitated housing, vocational training, and vital mentoring on life skills.

Recognizing the pressing demand for expanded housing options, Aunt Leah’s took a significant stride in 2013 by establishing Aunt Leah’s Foundation, now operating under the name Aunt Leah’s Properties. This entity was entrusted with the mission of nurturing and overseeing an array of affordable housing choices, tailor-made for new mothers and young people navigating their way out of the foster care system.

The majority of the properties within Aunt Leah’s Properties’ portfolio are leased back to Aunt Leah’s itself. This strategic leasing arrangement empowers Aunt Leah’s to offer comprehensive programs and comprehensive housing support services, ensuring a holistic and impactful approach to uplifting the lives of those in our care.

Name: AZRA Signature Solutions, LLC
Description:
At Azra Signature Solutions LLC, we are committed to empowering vulnerable youth and justice-impacted individuals by providing access to safe, stable, and supportive housing. While based in Maryland, our services are available nationwide to organizations, group homes, and reentry programs seeking trusted housing and supportive services.

We prioritize solutions that restores dignity, promotes independence, and helps individuals build a strong foundation for long-term success. Whether transitioning from foster care or reentering society, our goal is to build confidence, restore hope, and lay a strong foundation for lasting independence.

Name: Amagaya
Description: Amagaya is an eco-systemic design company, focused on optimizing human health and happiness as it relates to the person, community, and planet. Our master-planned conscious communities are built to endure via a conservationist approach to land stewardship and our mission to develop for future generations.

Name: Middlemarch Community Led Housing CIC
Description: Middlemarch Community Led Housing CIC is a social enterprise providing technical support to community land trusts (CLTs) and services to the wider community led housing sector. Middlemarch specialises in supporting partnerships between CLTs and housing associations to build affordable homes for local people.

Name: Stark Metropolitan Housing Authority
Description:
Our Mission: To provide eligible residents of Stark County with decent, safe, and affordable housing and contribute to nourishing neighborhoods by working in partnership with the public and private sectors. 

The Stark Metropolitan Housing Authority (SMHA) is a political subdivision of the state of Ohio and funded in part by the United States Department of Housing & Urban Development to provide subsidized housing and self-sufficiency opportunities for eligible citizens of Stark County. SMHA operates per policies established by its five-member Board of Commissioners, the Ohio Revised Code, and regulations and statutes administered by the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD). As an agency, SMHA provides decent, safe, and sanitary housing and resident programs to eligible low-and moderate-income families of Stark County. SMHA was founded in 1939 and assists over 4,000 families in Stark County.

Name: Public Voice CIC
Description:
Public Voice is a Community Interest Company (CIC) with a mission to improve neighbourhoods, the lives of the people who live in them and the public services they use.

Through our work, we ensure people in the community are heard – bringing together diverse voices and including those who find themselves marginalised or are rarely reached by service providers. We take a user-centred, co-production approach to understanding individuals’ and communities’ needs, and translate that into meaningful insights for service providers in government, public health, and housing. The results are better outcomes for residents and service users, more effective and efficient services for providers, and stronger and healthier neighbourhoods.

Name: Ebeltoft Havnefront
Description:
Nyt eksklusivt boligkompleks i flotte omgivelser, til Ebeltoft Vig

Med placeringen i første række og med udsigt til Ebeltoft vig, Ahl Hage og Mols Bjerge, får de nye boliger ved på Ebeltoft Havnefront den bedst tænkelige placering.

Boligerne indrettes i fem huse med varierede facadeforløb og højder. Det muliggør en unik kontakt til omgivelserne og et mangfoldigt og levende miljø. Friarealerne mellem husene indrettes med opholdsmuligheder og belagte flader, der gradvist fletter sig sammen med kystens naturlige landskab og vegetation – her mødes by, hav-kultur og natur.

De forskudte placeringer og planløsninger understreger også karakteren af varierede og individuelle boliger. Her er god plads til udeophold med lys og udsigt i fokus, og er der er indarbejdet tagterrasser med adgang for beboerne fra hver trappe eller elevator. Tagterrasserne indrettes og møbleres så der er ekstra plads til at nyde de gode sommerdage, de lune aftener eller alle de fantastiske udsigter stedet byder på.

Boligerne indrettes overvejende som 2, 3 og 4 værelseslejligheder. Variationen i boligernes størrelser og indretning imødekommer beboere med forskellige behov og ønsker, ikke mindst for beboersegmentet 50+. Der er lagt vægt på at skabe gode solfyldte og lyse boliger, hvor alle har udsigt til vand, by og natur.

Er du på udkig efter en bolig i et attraktivt område? 

I Ebeltoft har du mulighed for at udleve dine boligdrømme i et område som byder på alt fra spændende naturoplevelser, et rigt kultur- & byliv, store attraktioner i nærområdet og en nem tilgængelighed fra hele landet. Kombineret med et stærkt lokalsamfund og foreningsliv.

Boligerne indrettes i fem huse med varierede facadeforløb og højder. Det muliggør en unik kontakt til omgivelserne og et mangfoldigt og levende miljø.

We then take responsibility to unlock the value through our town planning specialist practice producing viable planning applications to deliver our promised design concept for value generation.

Our specialist project and cost management then take charge to coordinate the delivery of all professional services involved in the development till we finally hand over the project to the institution at or higher than the value promised.

Name: Terra Global Developments Inc.
Description:
Terra Global Developments isn’t simply building homes. We are rebuilding the sense of community that has been lost. At a time when neighborhoods feel disconnected and homebuilding has become less personal and more transactional, we focus on what matters most: places where people know each other, look out for each other, and feel they truly belong.

We design neighborhoods that feel real, rooted, and alive. Our work brings back the everyday moments that once defined community life: porch conversations, familiar faces, shared spaces, and the comfort of knowing you’re part of something larger than yourself.

The Terra Global Difference is simple. Our communities are intentionally shaped so people can actually know their neighbors. Shared spaces spark natural interaction.  We create places where children can play freely, families feel at home, and neighbors look out for one another. That sense of belonging is built right into the blueprint.

Our approach blends thoughtful design, renewable technology, regenerative systems, and modern lifestyle tools to create sustainable communities that endure. Every development is built around one guiding belief: if it doesn’t build community, we don’t build it.

We’re restoring connection one neighborhood at a time - and proving that community isn’t a thing of the past. It’s how can we live today.

Our first location is Vulcan, Alberta - the Star Trek Capital of Canada, where residents and visitors will experience how to 'Live Long & Prosper' - not as science fiction, but for real. The first build/story will inspire property owners & municipalities to build their own custom health and longevity village. We'll guide them through visioning, municipal planning & approvals, marketing & sales, accessing investment capital, programming, and network connections, then provide resources on the ground from development project idea to completion. Our vision is to add 10+ healthy years onto 20 million people's lives by making it easier to access healthier homes, products, and services "close to home" in local communities. 

Our target measures of success will be extended human and environmental healthy lifespans.   

Are you leading the development of a Health and Longevity village in your community - as a legacy, catalyst, and showcase of best practices for what's possible?

Name: Community Housing Improvement Program - CHIP NYC

CHIP supports its members in their mission to provide excellent and affordable housing, build a sense of community in their properties, and employ thousands of New Yorkers with their small businesses.

CHIP’s educational programming includes an annual seminar series, online resources, the monthly publication of the New York Housing Journal, and frequent informative email blasts. In addition to educational programs, CHIP offers a telephone helpline to answer owners’ questions about City and State agencies and to help them navigate the bureaucracy of property ownership. CHIP’s membership network also allows for the exchange of vital knowledge between property owners.

Freedom Villages gives you more choices for living independently and affordably in your own home – while having the time of your life.
Our village facilities include designer village centre buildings, which are the hub of social activity and good times at each village. Our residents love the community spirit and active social calendar in our villages. Regular activity and friendship allow for a village support network, enabling residents to remain independent while also part of an engaged neighbourhood. Enjoy as much or as little social contact as you wish!

Freedom residents are the beneficial owners of their homes. This means enjoying a share in capital gain if you need to move on to a new location, subject to market increases and length of time in the village. Name: MEVIR
Description:
MEVIR es persona pública no estatal, con una larga y fecunda trayectoria, creada por Ley N.º 13.640 en 1967 a impulsos del Dr. Alberto Gallinal Heber con el objetivo de erradicar la vivienda insalubre del asalariado rural. Con los años este objetivo inicial de la Institución se fue incrementando, atendiendo múltiples demandas de la sociedad en todo el interior del país, tanto en áreas rurales muy apartadas, como en parajes y localidades urbanas. Todo ello se tradujo en ajustes sucesivos al marco legal original.

Fue así como a la edificación de nuevas viviendas se le agregó la refacción de las ya existentes y se sumó la construcción de instalaciones para pequeños productores tales como salas de ordeñe, tanques de frío, galpones, pozos de agua, entre otros. Del mismo modo se incorporaron servicios comunitarios como escuelas, policlínicas, comisarías, centros CAIF y locales comunales, además de la provisión de infraestructura vial, agua, electricidad y saneamiento en los lugares más alejados del Uruguay profundo donde las políticas públicas llegaron o ampliaron y mejoraron su cobertura.

MEVIR implementa un sistema de acceso a la vivienda basado en un concepto integral de hábitat según el cual la vivienda es un elemento dentro de un sistema complejo donde interactúan equilibradamente varios factores: el territorio, la producción de bienes y servicios, el ser humano en comunidad, los servicios comunitarios y las infraestructuras físicas.

Por estas razones, el lema de MEVIR: “Construimos Comunidad”, sintetiza bien el espíritu de la obra que materializa la Institución.

Name: PathwayCommunities
Description:
Pathway Communities is a Public Benefit Company.

Mission:
Expand long-term, attainable homeownership that stays affordable. We use land grants, long ground leases, deed restrictions, and shared equity. We pair high-performance design with digital equity, financial literacy, and workforce opportunity so people can live well and move forward.

Program trails

1) Upward Mobility (civil servants) Attainable/Sustainable Community Development

2) Age-in-Place Community Development

3) Workforce Housing with Workforce Development

4) Faith-Based Housing Development

Who we serve
Public sector: city and town managers, councils, planning and zoning staff, housing and land bank leaders, state agencies.
Civic and community: faith leaders, community land trusts, neighborhood groups, tribal and veterans orgs, aging and disability advocates.
Education and health: universities, community colleges, K-12 districts, hospital systems.
Employers and workforce: public safety, municipal employers, chambers, trade schools, unions, small business networks.
Capital and utilities: CDFIs, credit unions, foundations, impact investors, utility co-ops and publics.

How we serve (toolkits)
Policy and zoning. Design standards. Public land to ownership. Faith-based development. Employer-assisted ownership. Workforce development. Finance and impact. Stewardship. Energy and water.

What we measure
Affordability retention at resale. Owner cost vs local wages. Ownership conversion rate. Equity accrued by year 3 and 5. Local hire share and apprentice hours. Energy use intensity and utility cost per home. Access to transit and response time for civil servants. Digital participation and financial literacy completions. Stewardship compliance.

Governance promise
We publish an annual public benefit report, keep affordability protections at each resale, align capital, design, and delivery with mission, and disclose our progress openly.

Our vision is “Making our House Your Home” and we plan to make this happen by delivering on our four strategic goals over the course of our Business Plan.  Our four strategic goals are:

 2. Investing in an environmentally sustainable way in tenants’ homes
3. Being a good employer that attracts and retains high quality staff
4. Working with partners to contribute to the wellbeing of communities throughout the Outer Hebrides
We have made a real impact on our communities in improving standards of rented housing, providing new homes and creating employment opportunities.

Name: MOFO BOSTAD
Description:
MOFO BOSTAD är ett professionellt bostadsutvecklingsbolag med ett driv att utveckla arkitektritade och hållbara hem som många har råd att bo i. 

Vårt största fokus är välbyggda bostadsrätter i Mellansverige. Vår filosofi är att erbjuda unika och hållbara hem för medvetna människor, som väljer ett modernt boende med omsorg. I ett MOFO-boende kan du därför vara säker på att vi tar ett helhetsansvar för att skapa hem där människor trivs och mår bra. 

Allt tar sin början i visioner och insikter om vad människor har för behov och önskemål idag såväl som i framtiden. När vi hittar ett passande område eller en tomt skapar vi en boendemiljö med människornas behov i centrum. Vi utvecklar hållbara, genomtänkta och miljösmarta boenden inom en attraktiv prisbild.

Vårt ansvar tar inte slut när köparen har flyttat in. Våra bostäder ska leva och utvecklas. Därför tar vi också ansvar för förvaltningen av huset, stöttar i arbetet med att bilda bostadsrättsförening för medlemmarna och så vidare.

Name: Grey & Ivy
Description:
Grey & Ivy develops economies through community-led housing solutions seeded by Grey & Ivy Knowledge Centres that integrate education & training in the entire process. 

Each Knowledge Centre is supported on an ongoing basis with 5+ years of intensive Research & Development behind us into housing, education & training, technology development, and business solutions that work. With over $2 million invested and over 100,000 hours of boots on the ground R&D combined with professional and academic support we have tried, tested, failed and then found success and more successes, we know what we're doing!

Each Knowledge Centre is connected to all other Knowledge Centres to allow for collective sharing of knowledge and expertise and integrated supply chain systems across the country that allow for self-determined and self-sustained solutions. Everything we do always weaves Indigenous and western ways of knowing and being to create ongoing and bilateral learning to create opportunities and growth for all with two-eyed seeing.

Grey & Ivy plants the Knowledge Centre seed with shovel-ready facilities and infrastructure supported similar to a fully customizable franchise that include all the tools, resources, programming, technology and professional guidance and support — all that is continually updated and advanced to keep pace with local innovation and market demands. 

Grey & Ivy puts the education and training and housing manufacturing facilities right in the spot their needed and connects them all with advanced technology to create a loop of knowledge and learnings that is applied back into the system. 

Grey & Ivy creates an upward spiral of growth through developments anchored in familiar and proven equity investment models that allow for more and more growth.

Grey & Ivy is a for-profit company to ensure what we say is economically and socially viable, nimble, self-sustaining and extraordinarily impactful without being a burden on the community or the government.

Name: HiveHoods
Description:
HiveHoods is a transformative platform designed to empower communities to take control of their housing and energy needs through sustainable, community-led development. At HiveHoods, we believe that the future of housing lies in the hands of the communities themselves—where local knowledge, collaborative efforts, and innovative solutions come together to create homes that are not only affordable but also environmentally sustainable.

Our platform provides the tools and resources needed for communities to drive their development projects from the ground up. Whether it’s accessing funding through crowdfunding, engaging community members in decision-making, or integrating renewable and decentralized energy solutions, HiveHoods supports every step of the journey. By placing the power of development into the hands of those who will live in these homes, we ensure that each project is tailored to the specific needs and values of the community.

Sustainability is at the core of everything we do. HiveHoods drives the use of low-carbon materials, energy-efficient designs, and renewable energy systems (solar and wind power). Our focus on decentralized energy solutions means that communities can generate their own power, reducing dependence on traditional energy grids and fostering long-term energy independence.

Through HiveHoods, we aim to create a ripple effect—where successful community-led projects inspire others to take similar initiatives, leading to a broader movement of sustainable, affordable housing across regions. Our platform is designed to scale, supporting communities of all sizes as they work towards a more sustainable and equitable future.

Join us at HiveHoods, where communities build their futures, homes are shaped by collective vision, and sustainability meets innovation. Together, we’re not just building homes; we’re building resilient, empowered communities.

Name: Whiteinch and Scotstoun Housing Association Limited
Description:
WSHA has its origins in two local community groups: the Whiteinch Housing Action Group and Scotfore.  The former was established in 1975 with the express aim of 'retention of the community' in the face of demolition of houses and shops brought about by the Clyde Tunnel and the clearance policy of that time.  The latter organisation (Scotfore) was set up round about the same time to coordinate a large environmental improvement project in Scotstoun.  These two bodies formed the basis of the original membership of the Housing Association which was formally constituted late in 1977.

Our Vision: Aiming for High Quality Homes in a Desirable Environment

Our Mission: At Whiteinch & Scotstoun Housing Association, we aim to provide and maintain the highest quality rented housing and environment.  We also aim to support this with quality services and associated activities for the betterment of our community.

We believe that everyone has the right to affordable housing. That is why we're going to build our own home in the Kolenkitbuurt in which our vision can be realized.

We no longer want to live anonymously in a neighborhood where neighbors don't greet one another. We want a home where there's always someone to drink coffee with, watch a movie with or do some grocery shopping for when you're sick.

Our group consists of multiple generations, people from all backgrounds, from families with or without children, people living alone, cohabiting, young people, older people and elderly people.

We strive to ensure that Eureka! will reflect the city in which we live together as much as possible and are still looking for new members to set this up together!

Name: The Ferry Project
Description:
Established in 1998, Ferry Project is an award-winning social enterprise and registered charity that helps homeless people in Fenland.

The aim of Ferry Project is not simply to provide accommodation, but to give people the skills they need to enable them to live independently.  These include life skills such as cooking and cleaning, education courses, vocational training, volunteering and employment opportunities.

We currently provide up to eighty-eight bed spaces in Wisbech and engage with over three hundred clients per year.  We are the largest provider for homelessness-related issues in the district, engaging with an estimated eighty percent of those who find themselves homeless in Fenland.

Our clients include people with financial problems, people fleeing domestic violence, people recovering from a family breakdown or from drug and alcohol abuse, young people leaving the care system, those who have been sexually abused, ex-offenders and those with simply nowhere else to go.

Counselling and basic skills training are performed at the project’s headquarters, Octavia View, with additional educational courses provided at the Queen Mary Centre in Wisbech.

Name: Havelock Housing Limited
Description:
Havelock Housing Limited is a dedicated community housing organisation that is committed to reducing homelessness. We do this by managing multiple property portfolios such as Canberra's iconic community housing development Havelock House, along with Gunghalin Singles Accommodation and Disability Housing to name a few. Overall we manage 300 properties and support over 370 tenants.  

Havelock House opened in 1951, and became home to public servants. There was less demand for hostel accommodation during the 1970s, and under The Hon Malcolm Fraser’s government in 1981, the sale of Havelock House was announced. The sale did not proceed, and a small section of the building became an office for the Australian Federal Police.

In 1983, the first Hawke Ministry handed over full control and use of the building to the AFP despite intense lobbying by various welfare groups. The decision, when made public, elicited a quick and powerful response from concerned community groups. By early July a picket line was in place outside Havelock House, which remained in place for 16 weeks through a Canberra winter. 

The Australian Social Welfare Union coordinated the picket with support from the ACT Council of Social Services. Numerous unions, community organisations and hundreds of individuals gave their time.

The picket ended in December 1983 with a settlement, which committed the government to:
•	Transfer Havelock House to low-cost housing use when alternative office accommodation was built for the AFP
•	Undertake an inquiry into homelessness in the ACT and surrounding regions.

The building was officially handed over from the Department of Territories to Havelock Housing Association on 10th April 1988, and the first residents moved in.

Since then we have ensured affordable accommodation for people on low incomes. Properties in other locations across Canberra gradually came under the Association’s management and today we are one of the largest Community Housing providers in the ACT.

- Franchise & Train: we license our technology and help build bamboo supply chains in regions across Latin America, Africa and Asia.

KuNa is a winner of the Architecture in Development 2022 Challenge, a semi-finalist of the MIT 2023 Solve Challenge and a winner of the Built by Nature 2024 Prize. 
Currently supported by the Impact Hub Accellerator program, KuNa is a growing impact venture with a mission to make home ownership affordable and sustainable for everyone.

Description:

During our exploratory period, we were in awe of the natural landscape; however, our local partners pointed out that owing to extractive farming processes over 20yrs more than 60% of the rainforests, flora, and fauna have been lost. 

We are designing, developing and delivering a world-leading sustainable village for residents and tourists that will serve as the green and gold standard worldwide for generations to come. Our community will restore over 60% of biodiversity by implementing regenerative farming processes, protecting land use, and sharing knowledge, resources and opportunities. Together, we will cultivate and preserve our utopia.  

Learn more about our parent company People & Planet (www.linkedin.com/company/people-and-planet-group) and our sister project: (www.linkedin.com/company/sumberkima-beach).

Homelessness is just the symptom. 

APAP’s mission is to break cycles of poverty and disadvantage and to tackle issues of homelessness as they present in Haringey and Enfield by:

Providing respite to those rough sleeping

Supporting individuals through and beyond housing crisis

Preventing people from experiencing the nightmare impact of rough sleeping

Artist Space Trust grew from the shared commitment of Vital Arts and the Northern California Community Land Trust (NCLT) to stem the displacement of artists economically vulnerable to rising real estate costs. We believe that community control and ownership are necessary to ensure that local artists have access to safe, affordable spaces to live, create, and share their work.  

Image: Bay Area artists Kelly Tunstall and Ferris Plock by Shaun Roberts

We emphasize accessible and supportive housing for former foster youth students, formerly incarcerated students, and other marginalized student populations who currently experience the highest rates of student homelessness and housing instability, according to consecutive California college surveys. Our team leading this initiative is driven by lived experience and personal connection to the communities we serve.

Name: UP by GOGO
Description:
UP by GOGO is making all the right moves for those in need

UP by GOGO is a new social enterprise initiative from the passionate team at GOGO Events. 

In August 2022 GOGO just announced UP by GOGO, an initiative designed to ease the stress of moving house, especially for those who might struggle under the strain, such as those in social housing. 

UP will ensure safe transport of treasured possessions as well as arranging to repurpose, rehome, or UPcycle all of those unwanted things left behind. UP will also create job opportunities for members of the community who have often struggled to find employment, but are eager to work.  

UP at a glance::
UP places the care of the person moving and their needs at the centre of the process.

UP manages the gifting, rehoming and upcycling of all unwanted items left from the move.

UP creates jobs for people who have been marginalised and have complex barriers to employment

All while ensuring that the moving between homes has a positive impact on the environment. 

UP by GOGO are currently running a crowd-funding campaign with the aim of raising enough money to expand the concept to those in need all over Australia. 

For interview requests or to find out more please contact UP by GOGO lead Sarah Gun via email at sarah@gogoevents.com.au.

Website: https://upbygogo.com.au/

Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/UPbyGOGO

Name: Gbg7000+
Description:
Gbg7000+ är en samverkansgrupp där deltagarna gemensamt verkar för byggandet av minst 7000 studentbostäder under perioden 2016-2026. Vi vill dessutom utveckla Göteborg som en kunskapsstad och tydliggöra studenternas betydelse för staden.

Göteborg har två starka lärosäten som lockar studenter från hela Sverige och resten av världen men bristen på boende påverkar stadens och lärosätenas attraktivitet negativt. Enligt en undersökning som Ungdomsbarometern gjort på uppdrag av Studentbostadsföretagen väljer hela 29 procent av studieintresserade unga bort Göteborgs universitet på grund av bostadssituationen. Motsvarande siffra för Chalmers tekniska högskola är 22 procent. 

Att unga studieintresserade väljer bort staden redan innan universitetstiden belyser ett bekymmersamt läge för staden och dess attraktionskraft samt kompetensförsörjning. För Göteborg är det därför av stor vikt att studenterna kommer hit men också att de stannar kvar efter utbildningsperioden. Ju mer etablerade studenterna är desto större är chansen att de stannar och blir yrkesverksamma i Göteborg efter studierna.

Name: Van Grond Los - Community Land Trust Leuven
Description:
Een Community Land Trust creëert warmere buurten waar betaalbare woningen aangeboden worden die bewoners versterken, zowel mentaal als financieel. Ze ijvert voor meer kwalitatieve buurten met een grotere sociale cohesie, zonder dat de verhoogde kwaliteit leidt tot de verdringing van huidige bewoners. Grond wordt bij een Community Land Trust niet gezien als een investering maar als een gemeenschappelijk goed dat gebruikt moet worden in het algemeen belang van de hele gemeenschap. CLT Leuven vindt dat het grondwettelijke recht op wonen moet waargemaakt worden voor alle bewoners van de stad  en dat de bewoners zelf mee vorm moeten kunnen geven aan hun buurt. Een goede woning – van goede kwaliteit, betaalbaar, duurzaam en met optimale woonzekerheid – is essentieel voor het welzijn, de gezondheid en de ontwikkelingskansen van haar bewoners.

Door een juridische scheiding van grond en woning blijft de grond steeds eigendom van de gemeenschap en kunnen enkel de woningen zonder de grond gekocht of gehuurd worden. De woningen zijn betaalbaar omdat de eigenaar de grond niet hoeft te betalen. Doorverkoop kan, maar steeds aan een vooraf bepaalde plafondprijs. Wanneer een bewoner wenst te verhuizen wordt de woning zonder de grond doorverkocht aan een volgende bewoner volgens een vast toewijzingsreglement. Hierdoor wordt langdurige betaalbaarheid gegarandeerd en kan een eenmalige investering over generaties heen ingezet worden. De prijs wordt niet langer door ‘de markt’ van vraag en aanbod bepaald, maar ligt buiten de speculatie van de markt.

Name: Sustain UK Ltd
Description:
Formed in 2009, Sustain UK Ltd is a not-for-profit company created in response to a growing need for quality social housing related support and accommodation for vulnerable homeless people in the West Midlands.

Now a registered provider, Sustain UK’s mission is to provide vulnerable and homeless people with the opportunity to live in housing of a decent standard, to be delivered by a trained local workforce.

Many of Sustain’s residents are experiencing mental health issues, learning difficulties and/or dual diagnoses, (including those who are recovering from alcohol/drug dependency.)

Name: Start Somewhere
Description:
You always have to start somewhere …   

Start Somewhere is a German-African social enterprise that offers innovative and affordable building solutions based on the TwistBlock.

More than just a stone, the TwistBlock building system brings with it several competitive advantages:

Expedited Construction & Early Profitability:
o Enables rapid construction, leading to quick project completion.
o Accelerates project profitability by allowing buildings to be put to profitable use earlier.

Utilization of Unskilled Labor:
o Enhances accessibility to construction processes through the primary employment of unskilled workers.
o Results in reduced labor costs.

Material Efficiency & Sustainability:
o Minimizes material consumption by eliminating the need for mortar, plaster, or formwork.
o Utilizes just one-third of the cement typically required in conventional construction techniques.

Adaptive & Appealing Design:
o Adapts seamlessly to various surroundings and environmental conditions.
o Maximizes floorplan utilization, allowing for efficient and versatile space management.
o Offers real aesthetic value in design.

Social Impact & Local Production:
o Builds value in Kenya by promoting local production and generating employment opportunities.
o Uplifts the community by improving access to innovative & affordable construction.

Opt for TwistBlock building solutions for unparalleled affordability, adaptability, and efficiency, complemented by an enticing aesthetic design.

 Unternehmen: Sichern Sie sich im Wettbewerb um die besten Talente einen unschlagbaren Vorteil. Mit maßgeschneiderten Mitarbeiterwohnungen steigern Sie Ihre Attraktivität als Arbeitgeber und fördern die Loyalität Ihrer Fachkräfte.

Name: ARCbuild
Description:
ARCbuild redefines home property building with technology and clean technology. 

Our mission is to provide affordable, prefabricated modular homes powered by a full suite of clean technology options globally. With ARCbuild's innovative building systems, we can build multiple disaster-resistant homes in 30-90 days, up to 50% cheaper, and 100% off-grid with independent solar power, advanced clean water supply, advanced greywater recycling, and advanced wastewater systems.

ARCbuild’s proprietary manufacturing and controlled global logistics processes utilize innovative, rapidly assembled, prefabricated steel modular building systems easily shipped anywhere in the world. These affordable homes are the most sustainable product available on the market today, which allows us to build homes in any location remote or otherwise.

With a team of experienced serial entrepreneurs and operators, ARCbuild (Affordable Resilient Communities) will [1] help close the housing affordability gap, making home ownership a reality once again, and [2] help build communities of affordable smart homes with clean technology. We have prototypes of affordable homes in the U.S., as well as affordable homes in Mexico, and are getting started to scale globally. We believe in building long-term partnerships with all our stakeholders. 

Real Estate, Construction, Clean Technology, Smart Cities, and PropTech redefined.

Name: First Place Global Leadership Institute
Description:
The First Place Global Leadership Institute is the nation's leading professional development organization addressing housing, healthcare, education, employment and public policy supporting adults with autism and/or intellectual/developmental disabilities (A/I/DD).

Developed by curious and unstoppable leaders in this specialized field, the Global Leadership Institute demonstrates extensive experience in the public, private, nonprofit and philanthropic sectors serving the greater community. We focus on the social determinants of health and multidisciplinary research driving a new generation of home and community options.

As a creator of resources, curricula and programs, connector of like-hearted leaders and catalyst for change, the Global Leadership Institute fosters positive, sustainable change in urban, suburban and rural areas across the country and around the world. We recognize the power of our example in the collaborations we foster, real estate we develop and communities we build.

For more information, visit firstplaceglobal.org, follow us on Instagram at instagram.com/firstplaceaz, like us on Facebook at facebook.com/FirstPlaceAZ or check out our blog at firstplaceglobal.org/about/blog.

Name: Lotus Living
Description:
Lotus Living has been shaping thriving communities across Melbourne’s West since 1988. With a deep-rooted passion for family, community, and sustainability, our team is dedicated to delivering residential developments that go beyond housing; they create a sense of belonging. 

From our early projects, such as Oakwood in Keysborough and Lotus Apartments, to our landmark developments like Jubilee in Wyndham Vale and Silverwoods in Yarrawonga, we have remained committed to building vibrant, well-connected neighbourhoods where families can flourish for generations to come.

Beyond property development, Lotus Living is a diverse and dynamic business with investments across multiple industries. We own and operate The Sebel Yarrawonga, a luxury 4.5-star resort located on the banks of Lake Mulwala, providing a premier hospitality experience in regional Victoria. 

Our portfolio also includes Prime Stockfeed and various farming enterprises, reflecting our deep agricultural heritage and commitment to supporting Australia's food production industry. 

This diversity strengthens our business and allows us to contribute meaningfully to multiple sectors, all while staying true to our core values of quality, community, and sustainability.

Our approach is guided by strong family values, a commitment to quality, and a deep appreciation for the communities we serve. We believe that great developments are more than just bricks and mortar, they are places where people build their futures. By prioritising sustainable design, open spaces, and essential amenities, we ensure that every Lotus Living community is designed to enhance the lives of its residents while contributing positively to the fabric of our suburbs.

Name: Rebuilding Together Richmond
Description:
Our mission: Repairing Homes, Revitalizing Communities, Rebuilding Lives

With the belief that everyone deserves to live in a safe and healthy home, our organization provides critical repairs, accessibility modifications and energy efficient upgrades to low-income homes and community centers at no cost to recipients. Our impact extends beyond the individuals served to revitalize and stabilize vulnerable neighborhoods and communities across the country. 

Since 1992, Rebuilding Together has aided in the revitalization of over 40 neighborhoods by impacting close to 1,900 homeowners and hundreds of neighbors throughout the Greater Richmond region. Rebuilding Together Richmond (RTR) consolidated operations with the Tri-Cities affiliate in July 2016 and expanded its geographic footprint to serve homeowners in Petersburg, Hopewell and surrounding communities.

Name: Rural Housing Coalition of New York

The Rural Housing Coalition of New York represents rural preservation companies, and other housing and community development professionals from government, non-profits and the private sector that share a common interest in serving all the residents of rural New York State.

Name: PREFIGURE EU project
Description:
PREFIGURE aims to create pathways towards an equitable green and digital transition laying the foundations for the development of energy-efficient, affordable and environmentally sustainable housing.

The project employs a mixed method transformative approach, identifying “prototypes of change” that address the housing-energy nexus. Co-creating knowledge with residents and involved stakeholders, PREFIGURE provides evidence-based solutions that inform EU, national, regional and local policies that tackle housing and energy vulnerabilities across local case studies and beyond.

Funded by the European Union. Views and opinions expressed are however those of the author(s) only and do not necessarily reflect those of the European Union or European Research Executive Agency (REA). Neither the European Union nor the granting authority can be held responsible for them.

Name: Garden Street Communities Southeast, LLC
Description:
Founded in 2016, Garden Street Communities Southeast, LLC is a privately-held residential lot development company focused primarily on land acquisitions and development to sell finished single-family and townhome lots to homebuilders. Garden Street Communities is headquartered in Pensacola, Florida and operates primarily in the Southeastern part of the United States, including Texas.
 
            Garden Street Communities manages a wide range of land acquisition, infrastructure development, and project planning activities related to residential lots for a specific community. Land acquisition and lot development activities are currently focused on three regions comprised of twenty individual markets across six Southeastern and Central states – Florida, Georgia, Alabama, North Carolina, South Carolina, and Texas, with expectation of expanding further into other attractive regions.    
 
            While our primary focus is developing for residential homebuilders, Garden Street has also developed real estate for investment purposes.  The lots we deliver in our communities are generally for entry-level, first-time move-up, and active adult homes.  Entry-level and first-time move-up homebuyers are the largest segment of our new home market.

Name: ديوان العمران - Diwan Alomran
Description: Diwan Alomran works on studying and analyzing urban development processes from an integrated perspective that takes into account social, economic, and environmental dimensions, with the aim of achieving sustainable and equitable urban growth. The Diwan’s vision is rooted in the understanding that the urban realm is not merely a physical space, but a dynamic system that reflects the social, economic, and political interactions within communities. Accordingly, the Diwan develops policies and strategies that promote sound urban governance, safeguard individuals’ rights to adequate housing, equitable development, and social inclusion, while protecting the urban fabric from unplanned or ill-considered interventions.

Name: Dos Ríos
Description:
Vivir en armonía con el entorno
Dos Ríos consta de una superficie de 73 hectáreas ubicadas sobre el bellísimo Delta del Paraná. Escondido dentro de dos pequeños arroyos, Las Malvinas y Tres Sargentos, Dos Ríos ofrece una ubicación impresionante para una casa vacacional o fin de semana, o aun para un hogar permanente. Es tranquilo, privado e idílico. Asimismo, tiene un espacio común maravilloso, constando de una gran piscina y un quincho. Dado que es elevado, dicho espacio goza de vistas espectaculares sobre la reserva común. Esta área es equipada con baños, duchas y reposeras.

Dos Ríos es el lugar idóneo para comprar un lote y construir su casa escondida. Es una combinación impresionante de tranquilidad, naturaleza, colores, flora y fauna. Se puede disfrutar las caminatas, la pesca, el canotaje, y la natación en los arroyos o la pileta. Socializar si quieres o si prefieres, mantenerte alejado del resto del mundo en tu casa aislada. Si hay un cielo sobre la tierra, es acá!

Name: KuNa
Description:
With over 1 billion people in the housing crisis and 40% of emissions coming from real estate, the world desperately needs a sustainable building revolution.   

KuNa is a bamboo social housing solution that is durable, cost effective, sustainable and simple to procure in any part of the world where bamboo grows. Thanks to our prefabricated panel and connection system, KuNa homes can be installed in 48 hours and can expand over time to meet the needs of families. KuNa has 3 deployment models: 
- Turnkey: we design, supply and install the home
- Self Build: we design the home and supply the panels. The family installs the home with supervision of our experts.
- Franchise: we license our technology, co-invest in infrastructure, provide advisory and community development services to help build bamboo supply chains 

KuNa won the Architecture in Development 2022 Challenge, made the semi-finals of the MIT 2023 Solve Challenge and won the Built by Nature 2024 Prize.
Our team is currently focused on scaling up the model in Mexico to make home ownership affordable and sustainable for an underserved market of over 1 million people / year.

Name: Scape Properties
Description:
Scape Properties is a privately owned development company located on the Central Coast NSW. The company’s vision is to create developments with a focus on green and environmentally centered communities. 

Currently Scape Properties is developing a 623-lot master planned estate at Crangan Bay- Central Coast. The development is ideally surrounded by National Parks and enjoying nearly 1 km of lakefront reserve on Lake Macquarie making it one of the most appealing land release estates the Central Coast currently has to offer. 
  
Scape Properties was established as the development arm of Scape Constructions by Director Andrew Smith.  

Scape Constructions is an award-winning civil landscape company that has been operating for over 25years. The company has largely been involved in local government infrastructure, sporting, and community projects. Scape has been fortunate enough to be involved in many iconic projects ranging from Olympic Park in Sydney to some of the Central Coast’s landmark projects including Ettalong Foreshore Redevelopment, Avoca Beach Foreshore Redevelopment, Central Coast Stormwater Harvesting Project, The Peninsula Recreation Precinct and many more. For more information on Scape Constructions projects please visit Scape Constructions website.

Name: WeBuild Concord
Description:
We are a premier agency providing and inspiring the development of equitable and permanently affordable housing options for all of Concord’s residents and the surrounding area. 

WeBuild was founded in 2019 under the original name, Concord Family Enrichment Association (CFEA). The purpose of CFEA was to serve as a nonprofit and enrichment arm for the City of Concord’s housing division. The organization’s mission expanded in 2021-2022 to provide opportunities through public and private partnerships for residents, including the building and developing of homes, a community land trust, and advocacy for equitable housing and community development. WeBuild is an inclusive and fluid model that responds to the specific needs of neighborhoods, economic and community development, and other intersectional factors that impact housing and talent development.

Organizational Statements

Vision Statement
WeBuild Concord will be a premier agency providing and inspiring the development of equitable and permanently affordable housing options for all of Concord’s residents.
 
Mission Statement
WeBuild Concord is a catalyst for building homes, partnerships, and opportunities for the stability and wealth of our residents. 

Core Values – S.C.O.R.E.

Stability
We enrich neighborhoods by providing affordable housing options for low to moderate-income households.

Connectivity
We foster relationships by listening, understanding, and empowering the voices of partners and residents.

Opportunity
We partner to ensure residents have quality homes that improve their ability to thrive and build wealth.

Respect
We promote pride and dignity in our community through high standards and thoughtful stewardship.

Equity
We affect change by building an inclusive future through housing.

Contact: Info@webuildconcord.org

Name: Exal Malaysia
Description:
Exal Group is a property developer and project delivery company based in Perth, Western Australia and Kuching, Sarawak.

With a current development pipeline of over GDV AUD$1 billion, our projects range from large scale subdivisions to purpose built student accommodation.  

In 2022, we are embarking on our maiden project in Malaysia with 269 units of aspirational homes in a gated guarded, masterplanned community in Kuching, Sarawak. 

We adopt a motto of “together we grow” as we strive for mutual gains with all stakeholders, constantly challenging ourselves to improve on the value we deliver to our partners based on our common vision.

Name: Church Development Agency Limited
Description:
The Church Development Agency (CDA) is a consultancy and development partner supporting dioceses to identify, plan, and deliver housing projects using Church land.

We are one of seven key partners in the Church Housing Project – inspired by the Coming Home report – that’s mobilising the Church to meet the need for more housing.

Our aim is to create affordable, high-quality social housing, and communities well-supported by the Church and its partners within the Church Housing network.

The majority of our projects are based in the West Midlands due to a three-year pilot programme, primarily funded by the Oak Foundation. Since starting in 2024 with 14 projects across Birmingham and Gloucester, the programme has grown to 30 projects delivering over 900 homes. We’re proud to be working closely with the Dioceses of Birmingham, Gloucester, Bristol, Lichfield, Derby, and Truro to make a real difference. 

Our goal is to demonstrate the success of this model locally, as a pilot for national scale.

As a key delivery partner of the Church Housing Association (CHA), we ensure CHA has first refusal on affordable homes developed through CDA projects, maximising long-term impact.

Name: Equally Crafted Management
Description:
Equally Crafted Management (ECM) was established in 2021 by industry veterans Gregory Knight and Natalie Levkovitz, combining over 20 years of experience in hospitality management and resident services. Our mission is to create lasting social and economic impacts within the communities we serve by providing exceptional resident services and tailored relocation management solutions. ECM has successfully impacted over 12,000 tenants through their resident service programs, relocation projects and tenant organization efforts.

At ECM, we pride ourselves on our innovative approach and unwavering commitment to enhancing the quality of life for our residents. We specialize in resident relations, tenant organization, and relocation management, ensuring that each project is handled with the utmost care and professionalism. Our goal is to foster strong community connections and drive meaningful change through our dedicated services.

If you’re interested in learning more about our services or exploring collaboration opportunities, we invite you to get in touch with us.

Name: HECKSOW
Description:
Hecksow is transforming the mortgage industry with AI and blockchain technology to make homeownership possible for everyone, everywhere.

By combining advanced AI and blockchain, we speed up loan approvals and ensure every transaction is secure and transparent. Our platform invites communities (through DAOs) to collaborate, ensuring solutions stay aligned with real-world needs.

With easy-to-use digital tokens, we’ve reimagined home financing—reaching regions traditional banks often overlook. Whether you’re buying your first home, investing, or part of an underserved community, Hecksow simplifies the process, making it fair and inclusive.

Join us in building a world where everyone can own a home, no matter where they are.

Name: Community Development Corporation of South Berkshire
Description: The mission of the CDCSB is to provide housing that is affordable and economic development opportunities for low- and moderate-income residents of the community. This mission is carried out in two focused areas of work: creating jobs by helping small local businesses begin, grow, and restructure to better compete in today’s complex economic environments; and building affordable homes, which includes both rental units and home ownership. Learn more at cdcsb.org.

Name: The Housing Alliance
Description: The Housing Alliance is a private, non-profit organization that serves as the hub in Collier County to bring together aspiring homeowners, renters, property developers, local government, funders, donors, and the community-at-large. It is envisioned to become the “go to” resource for housing in Collier County with a focus on workforce, lower income, and senior housing. Through structured coordination and collaboration, The Housing Alliance works with private and public partners with the goal to make much needed housing in Collier County a reality. Through a formal affiliation agreement, The Alliance will amplify and support the services provided by HELP which includes the housing navigator program and HUD-certified financial counseling and homebuyer education, as well as the Collier County Community Land Trust which provides land acquisition and partnership opportunities for affordable housing developments. Funding for the Alliance is provided by the Collier Community Foundation, Collier County Community and Human Services Division and private donations.

Name: Ecospheres
Description:
Ecospheres orchestrates new residential developments via our AI-driven new home buying platform, Nest-AI, which simplifies and accelerates the development of master planned communities. Nest-AI is a platform where new home buyers are empowered to be their own architect by working with an “AI-Architect,” as a sales funnel, automating the creation of the customers unique blueprint, incorporating the local building codes, community guidelines and the customers personal desires into the design. We then send the blueprints to partnered contractors and prefab manufacturers helping to manage the entire development of communities from conception-to-completion. Each home is engineered to be regenerative for the environment and technologically superior, creating the futuristic communities we all dream about in a fraction of the time. 

New homebuyers effortlessly select a lot in one of our communities, use conversational AI to architect their home and then design it in 3D or VR. Once content, they’re then able to seamlessly purchase their home, which we then manage the process from construction all the way to the move-in.

We promote the use of eco-conscious materials, supporting carbon-reducing and recycled options, alongside various renewable energy sources. By choosing Ecospheres, you contribute to climate restoration while achieving your dream home.

Simply by living the American Dream, you’re positively impacting the environment!

Join us on the sustainable building revolution today!
