Name: Noodopvang Nederland BV
Description:
Noodopvang Nederland
Menswaardige, kwalitatieve én efficiënte opvang voor vluchtelingen: dat is waar Noodopvang Nederland voor staat. Van A tot Z wordt u in het gehele traject ontlast rondom de keuze, realisatie en het beheer van een noodopvang locatie. Noodopvang Nederland heeft de flexibiliteit om snel te schakelen en staat met eigen professionals en samenwerkingspartners voor u klaar om deze taken op te pakken. Voor u een hele zorg minder. 

Over Noodopvang Nederland
Noodopvang Nederland is opgericht door 3 ervaren professionals met veel ervaring op het gebied van vastgoedmanagement, facilitaire dienstverlening, projectmanagement en natuurlijk noodopvang. 

De politieke uitgangspunten over de opvang van vluchtelingen veranderen regelmatig en daarmee de vragen die bij gemeenten worden neergelegd ook. Hoe kun je als gemeente hier adequaat op inspringen? Hoe zorg je voor een opvanglocatie die aan alle wetgeving voldoet? En op de juiste manier wordt beheerd?

Noodopvang Nederland is speciaal opgericht voor de volledige ontzorging bij het inrichten en beheren van noodopvanglocaties. Wij kennen de actuele wetgeving, brengen jarenlange facilitaire kennis en ervaring mee en daarnaast profiteert u van ons brede netwerk van professionals en samenwerkingspartners. Altijd met oog voor de mens. Kortom, u heeft er geen omkijken meer naar.

Facilitair én maatschappelijk
We combineren het beste van twee werelden: een facilitaire zakelijke blik maar wel met een maatschappelijke focus. We geloven er namelijk in dat de juiste aandacht voor de bewoner essentieel is voor een succesvolle noodopvanglocatie.

Dit draagt bij aan de gewenste stabiliteit op de locatie en vertaalt zich naar het draagvlak van de lokale samenleving. Het zorgt voor rust en regelmaat voor zowel de bewoner, de omgeving als de lokale politiek.

Name: South East Regional Delivery Alliance (SE-RDA)
Description:
The South East Regional Delivery Alliance (SE-RDA) brings together local authorities, RSLs and industry partners to develop new affordable homes through collaboration.

The SE-RDA is a partnership between The City of Edinburgh, East Lothian, Fife, Midlothian, Scottish Borders and West Lothian Councils, as a priority of the Edinburgh & South East Scotland City Region Deal (ESESCRD) with support provided by public sector infrastructure body, the Scottish Futures Trust.

It was established following the success of the Edinburgh Home Demonstrator (EHD) programme that was used to develop, test and implement a new delivery model for new affordable housing projects through three pilot projects across various sites in the South East of Scotland.

Based on pipeline coordination, regional typologies and design & performance standards, as well as collaborative procurement, the delivery model aims to facilitate the development of affordable housing projects with local authorities and Registered Social Landlords (RSLs) for greater use of modern methods of construction (MMC) to transform the performance of housing across the region and enable the move towards affordable homes built to net zero carbon standards.

Name: R.E.E.D CAPITAL GROUP LLC CDFI
Description:
Housing is a Human Right!
In a society that promotes basic human needs, R.E.E.D Capital Group joins forces to support and create the most viable solutions.

Who is R.E.E.D?

Byron K. Reed, CEO
The former Texas Capital Bank, SVP, Director, Community Development. As well as the former Vice President and Head of Community & Local Government Relations at CIT/OneWest Bank, which offered a wide range of banking products in Southern California. Under Reed’s direction, he was involved in all aspects of the Bank's community & local government relations efforts, given his vast experience with community and economic development. He also helped to identify new housing developments, assist with the rehabilitation of affordable housing, and economic development efforts, including workforce development/job creation, financial education, and core community services by partnering with key region elected officials, civic/community leaders, Faith-Based Communities, and other Not for Profit Partners. 

How we got started:
After residing in California for 15-plus years and relocating back to Dallas it was devastating to see that not much had changed. Individuals in the South Dallas areas experienced broken promises, corruption, and a loss of faith in mankind or those in positions to make a difference by building up the community and adding value where there was a lack. Byron knew at that moment that the vision given to him by God, the time, was now.

Philippians 4:13 "I can do all things through Christ which strengthened me.”

Birthing R.E.E.D. (Real Estate & Economic Development) Group, LLC. Having poured into other financial institutions for 38-plus years, Byron said “it was time to bring it HOME” literally. We believe that “housing is a basic human right”, and our purpose is to increase the opportunity for the preservation of high-quality, affordable, accessible, and attainable housing in South Dallas, targeting low and moderate-income individuals and families.

Name: Veterans 1st of NEW
Description:
Veterans 1st will help Northeast Wisconsin veterans by providing them with Transitional Housing and supportive services followed by the opportunity to move into Affordable Housing after completing counseling, job training, and job placement. We will give our area veterans a place to call home with wrap-around services to help them succeed with a purpose and a desire to become self-sufficient. 

Veterans’ 1st of NEW is a non-profit agency with an inspiring approach to addressing the immediate needs of our men and women who have served our country. First and foremost, our core belief is that every veteran needs a safe and supportive living environment.

Our vision is a three-tier construction project that will accommodate 21 tiny homes and one Community Center that forms a Veterans’ Village. The 21 tiny homes will be a combination of affordable and transitional homes. The Community Center will consist of an office, kitchen, and meeting room.  The Community Center will be the main focal area shared by veteran residents, area veterans, volunteers, and outside professional staff. It will offer programs, host meetings, and provide space for special events, but most importantly, offer the much-needed camaraderie between our veteran residents and veterans from the community.

Our team is mindful of other complex issues that veterans face today. One of those issues is the rising rate of veteran suicide, at over 22 per day. Then, add in the extra complexity that often comes along with suicide such as substance abuse and mental health challenges. We will be working with Brown County Community Treatment Center, the Vet Center, and the Green Bay VA Clinic to address those issues. With Brown County and VA Vouchers, their rents would be supplemented by up to 70%. They will pay up to 30% of their income for program fees and save 20% for up to 2 years to move into an affordable rental unit that is under Fair Market Value rent for another 3 years. Then they could get a VA Home Loan.

Name: Hays Farm
Description:
Just minutes from downtown and Redstone Arsenal this land has been farmed for well over a century. The 850 acres of forests, fields, springs, and ponds are revered as a place where a family made their home. From a blacksmith’s forge to rows of peach trees, this land has been cherished. Children have wandered in the woods, fished in the ponds, and have delighted in the splendor of the setting. Around this land grew a progressive city, marked by the spirit and innovation that carried man into space. As innovation remade this city, so too it allowed this land to yield a livelihood for its people.

Today, this land is being cultivated yet again. Nearly two-thirds will remain as protected parkland. That represents 540 acres of trails, woods, lakes, and open spaces that will transform this place an enduring playground for the families of a growing region.

Name: Maggie Walker Community Land Trust
Description:
The Maggie Walker Community Land Trust (MWCLT) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that seeks to develop and maintain permanently affordable homeownership opportunities for low and moderate-income households. The organization was formed in 2016 and is governed by a volunteer Board of Directors.

Using the Community Land Trust (CLT) model, the MWCLT creates homes that are sold to qualified buyers while retaining ownership of the land beneath the houses. These homes receive permanent subsidy that stays in the home and allows the home to be sold below market value. As an affordable alternative to renting, the CLT model allows families to build wealth through their monthly mortgage payments. In exchange for buying the house at a lower price, a CLT homeowner agrees to pass on the gift, and sell the home at a price that is affordable to someone in a similar financial situation. The new resale price is determined by the increase in the Richmond region’s Area Median Income (AMI).

The Maggie Walker CLT is committed to providing perpetually affordable homes to families with modest incomes throughout Richmond’s neighborhoods. In this way, we can keep our neighborhoods diverse and vibrant, even as housing prices rise. Without a program like the MWCLT, some neighborhoods in Richmond will be unaffordable to many of our citizens in the next decade.

The Housing App is built to support efficiency, accuracy, and better outcomes for both housing providers and tenants.

The organisation was founded in 2006 with the kind support of the MATRA Programme of the Netherlands Ministry of Foreign Affairs designed to help strengthen civil society organisations, democracy and the rule of law. The Centre for Sustainable Housing is the research and consultancy hub of CAC that aims to support the urban housing management and renovation process through a suite of consulting services, knowledge transfer and training programmes addressing the quality and sustainability of the built environment. It has a particular focus on improving energy performance and alleviating energy poverty during the entire building lifecycle. Built for efficiency and transparency, our platform helps mortgage professionals quickly understand where a buyer stands, reduce friction in early conversations, and confidently guide clients toward the right next steps.

With our easy-to-use readiness check, loan officers can assess buyer potential in minutes—no hard pull. No disclosures. No delays. Just a clear, actionable snapshot of financial readiness that supports more thoughtful conversations and more efficient workflows.

But readiness checking is only the beginning. QualGauge also strengthens your referral network by allowing real estate professionals to submit readiness checks directly to your account, giving loan officers visibility into buyer activity across their ecosystem even when they’re off the clock and turning your network into an extension of your team.

QualGauge is here to help mortgage professionals:
 Qualify leads faster
 Reduce unnecessary hard credit pulls
 Strengthen realtor partnerships
 Improve buyer experiences
 Save time on early-stage conversations
 Increase team efficiency
Save Money & Time for both the MLO & consumer

We believe in efficiency. We believe in transparency. And we believe loan officers deserve tools explicitly built for the way they work today.

Name: Ecologische Woonzorggemeenschap Puur Natuur
Description:
Ecologische woonzorggemeenschap Puur Natuur wil een veilige haven bieden voor zorgzame mensen die affiniteit hebben met duurzaam en ecologisch wonen én voor kinderen en (jong)volwassenen met een meervoudige beperking. Een groene plek waar we in harmonie met elkaar en met de natuur leven. Waar ruimte is voor ieders eigenheid en talenten, en voor stilte en reflectie.

Kinderen en (jong)volwassenen met een verstandelijke of meervoudige beperking hebben veel liefdevolle duidelijkheid nodig. Zij gedijen het best in een warme, zorgzame, overzichtelijke omgeving. Is die er niet, dan geeft dat stress en een onveilig gevoel. Ook zijn ze zich vaak helemaal niet bewust van mogelijke gevaren. Meer nog dan anderen hebben zij daarom een veilige haven nodig. Sommigen hebben zóveel zorg en nabijheid nodig, dat het bijna niet realistisch is te verwachten dat dit allemaal professioneel georganiseerd kan worden. Hoe gewenst die professionele betrokkenheid ook is. We zien ook een maatschappelijke ontwikkeling die ons zorgen baart: over tien jaar is er al een derde minder mensen beschikbaar voor de zorg. Tal van (ouder)initiatieven laten zien dat er niet alleen een wens is voor positieve verandering, maar ook een noodzaak. 

Daarnaast is klimaatverandering en uitputting van de aarde een groot maatschappelijk probleem. Naast duurzame relaties in de zorg voor extra kwetsbare kinderen en (jong)volwassenen, vinden we het van belang zorg te dragen voor de aarde en dat op een duurzame manier vorm te geven door onze woon- en leefwijze. 

Dit is dan ook waar we ons voor inzetten: een mooi en goed leven, met zorg voor de aarde en voor elkaar.

Ons doel: een woongemeenschap realiseren in een natuurlijke omgeving, waar mensen met én zonder beperking op een duurzame manier samen wonen en leven.

Doe jij mee? Of wil je meer weten? Zie onze website voor meer info en deelname aan online inspiratiesessies voor geïnteresseerden.

In 2020 two local based social housing providers, South Western Housing and Tamar Housing,  started working together in partnership, as two like-minded organisations looking to harness collective expertise, knowledge and passion for social housing, in order to benefit existing and future tenants.  The merger between the organisations means that there is capability to increase the ability to provide new homes and invest in existing homes.

CEO, Donna Johnson says “As we enter a new phase of our evolution, our focus is clear, to deliver more and for the benefit of the people that we provide services to both now and in the future.  Our vision remains to Deliver & Grow.  Our priorities for the next three years will be centred around four key areas:

People – this includes our current and future tenants, our colleagues and all of the partners we work with in collaboration as a smaller enterprise across the South West.

Property – we will work to ensure we maintain and invest in the homes we own and manage, keeping them safe, and also working to improve their fabric to deliver more sustainable homes for the future.

Place – we want people to enjoy where they live and for the new homes we build to be where people can access services and benefit from green spaces as much as possible.

Environment – we aim to be an organisation that puts sustainability at the heart of what we do, whether that is in the building of new homes, retrofitting existing ones, or limiting our impact on the biodiversity that is so important in supporting the efforts being made to manage climate change.”

We’ve created a space where residents can unwind, rejuvenate, and embrace a healthier, more mindful lifestyle.

Sustainable Living

Strategically located on the Narsapur Highway, SAGE ONE offers easy access to Hyderabad’s key areas while providing the peace and tranquility of rural living. You’re never far from the city’s conveniences, but you’re always close to nature.

At SAGE ONE, we believe in creating a better future by going back to the values of the past. Here, every day is a return to simplicity, sustainability, and luxury—all in perfect harmony with the natural world.

Ze verdienen te veel voor sociale huur maar hebben onvoldoende eigen geld voor het kopen van een woning. Hierdoor worden ze gedwongen om bij hun ouders te blijven wonen. Of om een onverantwoord groot deel van hun netto-inkomen (meer dan 40%, Nibud) te besteden aan huur. Een immens maatschappelijk probleem, waarvan bijna iedereen vindt dat het snel moet worden opgelost.
Cokopen is onze oplossing.

Name: Nærheten AS
Description:
Mange ulike faktorer er viktig for et godt og langt liv, men gode sosiale relasjoner står i en særstilling. Nærheten er en løsning på helsefremmende boform.

De store og sosialer rommene til leiligheter er i Nærheten lokalet. Løsningen bygger på vår kulturarv fra klyngetun og forskning som konkluderer med at gode relasjoner gjør at vi har bedre helse og lever lengre. Med sosiale boformer får vi uformell frivillighet. Mange tenker kollektiv når sosiale boformer nevnes, men det er mye mer enn det. Nærheten er en sosial boform, som er et supplement til etablerte løsninger. Det gjør vanlige boliger helsefremmende.

Bedre bokvalitet i nærheten av hjemmet, med Nærheten lokalet.  Det er forbeholdt alle generasjoner i nabolaget og er en "tredjeplass" som gir trygghet og tilhørighet! 

Eldrebølge ressursen er i nærheten og reduserer ensomheten.

Nærheten er din og nabolagets storstue - rommet og plassen du noen dager mangler hjemme i arbeidstid, fritid og høytid er i Nærheten.

Nærheten symbiosen er veldig bærekraftig og kan oppnå redusert klimaavtrykk og investering med mer enn 50%. Det kan økes med den eiendomsteknologien vi bruker, fordi vi kan bruke andre ledige lokaler i nabolaget til Nærkontoret.no, Nærværet.no og Nærvert.no som hovedsakelig har behov på ulike tidspunkter. Flere tilbud i Nærheten reduserer transportbehov og nabolag blir mer aktive og trygge. 

Nærheten AS skal levere komplette Nærheten lokaler med multifunksjonelt inventar og Nærheten PropTech kan drifte flere lokasjoner. 

Aktiv bruk av lokaler i nabolaget fra morgen til kveld og i helg, gir trygge nabolag. 

Godt naboskap og vennskap dyrkes best Innendørs, i vårt klima. 

Sammen kan vi forme nabolag i by og bygd, som former deg. 

Nærheten AS søker etter utviklingsprosjekter og samarbeidspartnere som bidra til å skape merverdi for utbyggere, nabolaget, boligkjøper og kommunen.  

For "Den nye normalen"  Mer mangfold og tilbud i nærheten av hjemmet.

Lev og del i Nærheten.

Name: Upper Hammonds Plains Community Land Trust
Description: Established in 2022, UHPCLT emerged as a response to the increasing concerns within our community, with a commitment to strengthening, protecting, and preserving the historical and sacred lands of Upper Hammonds Plains. Our mission is centered around providing affordable housing and preserving the African Nova Scotian culture and heritage through collective land ownership and responsible stewardship. We envision Upper Hammonds Plains as a community where affordable and secure land ownership is a reality, achieved through community-led development that is deeply rooted in cultural traditions and social equity. Aspiring to be a leader in developing community land trusts within Black communities provincially and nationally, UHPCLT is dedicated to preserving the cultural heritage and economic prosperity of our neighborhoods, while creating opportunities for Black families and individuals to access affordable housing, economic opportunities, and the tools necessary for building intergenerational wealth.

Name: The Pearl
Description:
Coming in 2024, with countless possibilities to discover, The Pearl will unearth new opportunities for local businesses and shops, restaurants, live music, arts, and entertainment.

Located on Main Street in the heart of downtown Fort Wayne, The Pearl is poised to become a cornerstone of growth and development.

This one-of-a-kind, mixed-use space will provide opportunities for local businesses, shops, and restaurants to take advantage of Fort Wayne’s consistent momentum. The Pearl will function as a natural hub for live music, arts & culture, education, and more, becoming a “must-visit” for locals and out-of-towners alike.

The Pearl is the next step in downtown development, offering 12,000 square feet of street-level retail space. It’s all within walking distance of Promenade Park, museums, additional shopping, and several surrounding neighborhoods. Prospective business tenants can expect built in foot-traffic from The Pearl’s residential offerings. The building’s upper floors will feature 76 single- and multi-bedroom apartments, plus several live/work units.

Description:
SAJE is a 501c3 non-profit organization in South Los Angeles. Since 1996 SAJE has been a force for economic justice in our community
focusing on tenant rights, healthy housing, and equitable development.

SAJE has taken many notorious slumlords to court, has established a land trust, and helped implement innovative popular education programs. We run a regular tenant clinic, help connect local residents to jobs, and fight for community benefits regarding future development through private agreements and public policies.

We believe that the fate of city neighborhoods should be decided by those who dwell there, and we convene with other organizations to ensure this occurs in a manner that is fair, replicable, and sustainable.

Name: HABITATbrokers
Description:
Fondatorii nostri au peste 12 ani de experienta in facilitarea tranzactiilor imobiliare pe piata din Bucuresti. De anul trecut am inceput sa ne extindem si in alte zone ale Romaniei.

In tot acest timp am invatat nu doar „sa vizionam” sau „sa semnam contracte”, ci si sa ascultam, sa intelegem ce isi doreste fiecare client, sa actionam conform acestei dorinte, sa sprijinim, sa mentinem legatura, sa ducem lucrurile la bun sfarsit.

DE CE SA LUCREZI CU NOI

 - Obtii know-how -
De-a lungul celor 9 ani de experinta de tranzactionare am intampinat tot felul spete. Am trecut printr-o criza economica. Ne-am adaptat. Am crescut. Suntem pregatiti sa preintampinam si sa rezolvam situatii. Putem sa te sfatuim de la cum cum e bine sa compartimentezi suprafata sau cat trebuie sa coste imobilul, pana la cum sa obtii finantare si de unde.

 - Externalizezi forta de vanzari -
Lasa profesionistii sa conduca prezentarea imobilului si negocierea pretului! Elimina costurile fixe si bazeaza plata acestor profesionisti doar pe performante.

 - Externalizezi serviciile de marketing - 
Online sau offline – marketingul imobiliar a evoluat. Noi stim sa folosim ambele medii, in functie de specificul bunurilor imobile si de publicul lor. Pentru proiectele noi si dezvoltatori, dar si pentru marii investitori, putem crea identitati vizuale si construi branduri coerente si credibile, utilizand o gama larga de strategii si canale.

 PACHETE DE SERVICII PERSONALIZATE

Serviciile pe care le oferim ajuta, proprietarii, fie dezvoltatori, investitori sau amatori, sa fie credibili, sa se conecteze cu clientii potriviti si sa isi maximizeze incasarile.
In functie de nevoi si de buget, putem include in colaborarea cu noastra, unul, cateva sau toate serviciile HABITAT Brokers.

Name: Arealudviklingsselskabet Sygehusgrunden P/S
Description:
Sygehusgrunden i Holstebro er et af Danmarks mest ambitiøse byudviklingsprojekter.

Gennem mere end 128 år var det tidligere sygehus en livsnerve for borgerne i Holstebro og omegn. I 2022 blev den sidste patient behandlet på her. Selvom sygehusets livsvigtige funktioner nu er fortid, så spiller de historiske og arkitektoniske bygninger en rolle i nutiden – og ikke mindst for fremtiden. 

Arealudviklingsselskabet Sygehusgrunden P/S er et selvstændigt partnerskab mellem Færchfonden, Holstebro Kommune og Aksel Nørgaard. Visionen er at transformere hele Sygehusgrunden til en livskraftig, attraktiv og bæredygtig bydel, der er en del af og et aktiv for Holstebro midtby.

Hen over de næste 10-15 år sælger vi bygningerne til investorer, der vil bevare, genbruge og transformere dem til nye formål, som bidrager positivt til visionen. Området vil i den periode fungere som et laboratorium for bæredygtig byudvikling, hvor vi skaber nyt liv i de gamle rammer og tester de enkelte bygningers potentialer.

Ambitionen er, at Sygehusgrunden bliver en levende bydel, der fungerer som et samlingspunkt i midtbyen og danner rum for kultur, kunst, uddannelse, erhverv og boliger.

Name: Church Housing
Description:
Several projects and groups are working to provide the Church's response to the housing crisis, informed by Coming Home. 

The Forum for Church Housing was formed in November 2024 to enable collaboration between all parties. It is convened and chaired by Bishop for Housing, Bishop Guli Francis-Dehqani, and meets quarterly, bringing together leaders of Church Institutions, housing experts, and partner organisations. The member organisations of the Forum aim to assist and enable the whole church in its approach to the housing crisis. Diocesan and parish representatives are warmly welcomed to contact any of the organisations listed. The Forum aims to promote a unified and effective approach to housing and community, following  the spirit of Coming Home and its recommendations for  housing which is safe, stable, sociable, sustainable and satisfying.

Name: Longleigh Foundation
Description:
Longleigh Foundation was set up in 2015 by a leading social housing provider in response to reductions in public spending and changes in welfare reform. Individuals and communities served by the social housing sector were experiencing greater vulnerabilities, disadvantages, and increased financial and emotional distress due to the cutbacks to – and even the disappearance of – local public and community services. We are in the minority of grant-making foundations in that we run grants programmes for both individuals and organisations. 

With a 5000% increase over the last five years, we have provided over £2m of Individual Grants funding, supporting hundreds of individuals and families each year. Through these grants, we help with daily essentials, such as food and fuel; with those larger items we all need, such as white goods and flooring; and we have helped people access further/higher education, training and employment through grants to pay for course fees or purchase required equipment and materials. 

We have distributed more than £3.5m in grants to organisations to deliver projects and research. These grants have enabled thousands of people to access ongoing support to help with areas of life including financial, emotional and physical health and wellbeing. We have funded innovative projects such as a self-build housing project for armed forces veterans and for research that look

By being an integral, strategic partner, Longleigh’s model helps social housing providers do more than can be done as landlords alone. Our dedication to our work means that the boards and executive teams in social housing organisations can focus on their core business, knowing that their desire to provide ‘more than a home’ for their residents is being continually addressed. 

To open a conversation about whether the Longleigh approach is of interest to your social housing or community-anchor organisation, please send us an email: contact@longleigh.org or contact us on 020 7164 6199.

Name: Worcester Common Ground
Description:
Worcester Common Ground (WCG) is a community development corporation in the Piedmont neighborhood of Worcester that promotes and develops permanent and sustainable improvement in the community through affordable housing, resident activism, and economic development. WCG rehabilitates abandoned housing by acquiring parcels of vacant land for new construction to provide area residents with affordable rental units, the opportunity to own their own home, and an avenue to contribute to an increased level of neighborhood stability, investment, and pride. 

Worcester Common Ground, Inc. (WCG) was founded in 1988 in response to rising concerns in the city about the high cost of housing, the displacement of families, and the large amount of vacant property. The dream of home ownership and economic opportunity for those living in the most underserved neighborhoods of Central Worcester was fading, and WCG decided to take action. Throughout our 30 years, we have had the great fortune of creating 176 units, 25 first-time home ownership properties, and 7 green spaces to serve our neighbors.

Community Development Corporations are 501(c)(3) registered non-profit organizations that are created to revitalize neighborhoods, address complex and systemic issues, and strengthen the community through access to affordable housing, community programming and opportunities for economic development. Worcester Common Ground focuses on the impact that affordable housing can make on rebuilding a community, from traditional rental units, to retail spaces, and first-time home ownerships. In addition to housing, we preserve and maintain greenspaces for both resident and public use to bring neighbors together, produce healthy food for the community, and provide opportunities for agricultural education and business.
By using the Community Land Trust model, Worcester Common Ground provides stewardship of Land Trust holdings in order to preserve scarce affordable housing and community resources.

Đội ngũ VinaLiving gồm các chuyên gia giàu kinh nghiệm, cùng mạng lưới đối tác uy tín trong lĩnh vực thiết kế, tài chính, quản lý và vận hành, đảm bảo sự chỉn chu và tối ưu trong từng giai đoạn phát triển dự án.

Established in 2010, VinaLiving is the real estate development brand of VinaCapital Group. For over a decade, we have pioneered the development of high-end residential and resort-style communities in Vietnam, earning multiple international awards.

Driven by the philosophy of “Resort Lifestyle,” VinaLiving delivers tranquil, private living spaces in harmony with nature. Our designs are refined, sustainable, and thoughtfully integrated with resort-style amenities to foster a strong sense of community.

Name: Wilden
Description:
Blenk Development Corp. is the company behind Wilden in Kelowna, the largest master-planned real estate development in the Okanagan Valley and beyond. 25 years after the master-plan has been established, the Wilden community is approximately 30% built out today. Wilden will eventually span between 2800 and 3000 units in single-family and multi-family neighbourhoods, a commercial centre, a school and numerous parks. About 50% of the roughly 1500 acres will remain either untouched or dedicated to natural park.   

Both companies, Blenk Development and Wilden Construction, are owned and operated by the Blenk family, consisting of three generations, most of them living in Wilden themselves. A small and dedicated team works hand in hand from early planning stages to sales and marketing. 

The Wilden group of companies have always been a driver of change, leading the way in nature sensitive growth and sustainable residential building practices. We are initiating and funding research towards more energy-efficient and eventually carbon-neutral living.

Our clients, residents and employees are at the core of our goals and values. We offer them a balanced lifestyle along with the security of a financially stable and meaningful enterprise.

Find answers to your questions, bite-sized help videos, or sign up for free online and in-person training at www.fourmillionhomes.org

Name: Holstebro Udvikling P/S
Description:
Holstebro Udvikling P/S er et selvstændigt, non-profit partnerselskab etableret mellem Holstebro Kommune og Færchfonden i 2018, med det formål at fremme en kommerciel, langsigtet og stærk byudvikling med Holstebro som den centrale motor i Nordvestjylland.
I perioden 2018 til midten af 2020 udarbejdede vi Byens Plan, som er en ambitiøs, sammenhængende og langsigtet Masterplan for midtbyen i Holstebro. Ønsket var at skabe bedre sammenhæng i byen og tiltrække investorer til konkrete byudviklingsprojekter.

Byens Plan blev realiseret efter et omfattende arbejde med inddragelse af borgere, organisationer, foreninger og virksomheder. En parallelkonkurrence om at udarbejde den endelige Byens Plan stod mellem tre arkitekthold med Team Vandkunsten som vinder. Holdet vandt bl.a. på deres overordnede tanker om at skabe nye forbindelser gennem byen, genetablere mere natur og forstærke handels- og kulturlivet i byen.

I juni 2020 blev Byens Plan overdraget til Holstebro Kommune.

Et af de bærende elementer i Byens Plan er seks visioner:

– Plads til flere beboere
– Styrke byens natur
– En mere levende midtby
– Forbindelser på tværs
– Ændret parkeringsstrategi
– Kulturen som dynamo for byens udvikling

Disse visioner skal Holstebro Udvikling arbejde videre med. Vi skal arbejde med grøn vækst og bæredygtig udvikling. Vores mål er at skabe en dynamisk og spændende bymidte, der er med til at tiltrække mere liv til byen, flere arbejdspladser og dermed vækst til hele Nordvestjylland i fremtiden.

Byens Plan blev til i et tæt samarbejde med borgere, interessenter og andre med interesse i Holstebros bymidte. Borgerinddragelse er et nøgleord for Holstebro Udvikling – og det fortsætter. Vi vil arbejde med borgere, foreninger, handelslivet, kulturinstitutioner og alle andre, der spiller en rolle i byen. 

Ved henvendelser kontakt adm. direktør, Maj Britt Rosenmeyer Olsen, på tlf. +45 4026 1900 eller mail mbro@hbudv.dk.

Il s’agit de mobiliser des ressources, de renforcer des capacités, de mettre en place des intermédiations avec ces collectifs et associations afin de produire d’autres informations, des alternatives d’aménagement ou des méthodes de débat.

Name: The Cork Student Housing Co-op
Description:
We are a voluntary collective of students and alumni from Cork’s third-level institutions, coming together to take action against the student accommodation crisis.

In the face of rising rents, corporate landlords, and a broken housing system, we’re building something different: a Student Housing Co-operative.

Our goal is simple but powerful: to provide not-for-profit, student-run accommodation based on the values of mutual aid, self-help, solidarity, and democracy. Through the well-established co-operative model, we’re creating a sustainable, affordable, and community-led alternative to the exploitative status quo.

We’re building a future where students are no longer priced out of accommodation and out of accessing third-level education, or forced into substandard living. Our mission is to create affordable, sustainable, and student-led housing that puts people before profit.

In our co-operative, students won’t just rent a room: they own their space, shape their community, and live with dignity.

We are committed to:

Democracy – every member has a voice and a vote
Sustainability – from how we live to how we build
Inclusivity – a home for all, not just the few
Community – where support, solidarity, and shared responsibility thrive

Contact us: corkstudenthousingcooperative@gmail.com

Objectivos
Promover a capacitação, interação e desenvolvimento dos jovens empreendedores, criando

socioeconômico de Moçambique. Nossa missão é dar voz aos jovens, criar oportunidades de 
aprendizado e fomentar uma nova era de empreendedorismo capaz de transformar o país em 
um ambiente mais próspero e inovador.

Visão

empreendedores em Moçambique, promovendo um futuro de prosperidade, sustentabilidade e 
impacto social, onde todos os sonhos são possíveis.

Educação

Name: Ember Road Development Corporation
Description:
At Ember Road Development Corporation, our mission is to create thriving and inclusive communities by providing affordable, subsidized housing solutions for low-income individuals who need a permanent supported living environment. We are dedicated to making a positive and lasting impact on society by addressing the pressing need for safe, secure, and sustainable housing options.

We believe that access to affordable housing is a fundamental right and an essential building block to leading healthy and fulfilling lives. Our goal is to ensure that everyone has a place they can call home, regardless of their circumstances.

Through strategic partnerships, innovative approaches, and a deep commitment to social responsibility, we strive to make a meaningful difference in the lives of those who are disadvantaged. By leveraging our expertise and resources, we aim to provide housing options that not only meet the basic needs of our residents but also foster a sense of community, dignity, and empowerment.

Name: Rise Development | ريز للتطوير العقاري
Description:
At Rise, we specialize in developing and selling beautifully designed multi-family resi- dential developments across Saudi Arabia, strategically located within high value mar- kets, so residents can enjoy city living at its finest. From site acquisition to ownership and sale, each unit is designed, built, and managed to high-quality standards by our team, which is comprised of experts in their fields, possessing years of experience, allowing us to be one of the leaders in the market.

Rise brings to each individual project a multi-sector expertise, and a tailored, metho- dical approach spanning site selection, due diligence, design, entitlements, and permit strategies, right through to team selection, bidding, and overall management of the design and construction process, to the sale or management of the asset.

We believe that buildings are more than physical structures, as they can also be ca- talysts for change. Because of this, our goal is to create vibrant and valued environ- ments for the people who live there every day. Whether you’re searching for your first or your forever home, whether you need to upgrade to a larger floor plan or downsize to a more efficient space, at Rise Development, our portfolio features a wide range of options for any stage of life.

We know that a “one-size-fits-all” approach doesn’t work when it comes to residential living. That’s why, with locations in key markets throughout the Saudi Arabia, we’ve truly got something for everyone.

Desde entonces, hemos puesto a disposición de la población casi 3.000 viviendas de protección oficial y somos una de las empresas y entidades promotoras de la vivienda, adheridas al Registro de Entidades Promotoras de Vivienda Protegida, auspiciado por la Diputación de Barcelona.
 
Somos un equipo multidisciplinar y con experiencia que velamos por la comodidad de nuestros inquilinos, que nos preocupamos por atender a nuestro cliente en todo aquello que pueda necesitar para que no deba preocuparse por su hogar.
 
Actualmente tenemos promociones en diversas comunidades autónomas como Cataluña, Madrid, Asturias y la Comunidad Valenciana.

Name: EpochEco
Description:
A next-generation housing company that builds AI-integrated Earthships for families across every income level—affordable, off-grid, resilient, and fully autonomous.

We believe modern homes shouldn’t trap you in rising utility bills, complex maintenance, or endless upgrades. For decades, commercialized housing has been built to be disposable—made from wasteful materials, tied to centralized systems, and optimized for profit, not people.

We change that.

Inspired by Earthship architecture and upgraded with modern technology, we design homes that:

Generate their own power using AI-optimized solar systems

Harvest and recycle water through smart, automated loops

Regulate indoor climate passively, and learn your habits over time

Require minimal maintenance through built-in predictive monitoring

With models starting under $50K, we’re making self-sufficient living possible for:

First-time buyers seeking security and savings

Families tired of renting their resources

High-income earners wanting to invest in regenerative living

Homesteaders, off-gridders, and climate-resilient pioneers

Each is built to last for generations—modular, upgradeable, and designed to be passed on, not torn down.

Own your land. Own your power. Own your water. Own your time.

This is not just a place to live—it’s a new era in how we build, own, and sustain.

 Off-grid by default
 AI by design
 Sustainable at every layer
 Configurable & affordable

Want to learn more or become a founding partner?
 Contact us to get early access or join our pilot program.

Name: Contix
Description:
Contix provides vulnerable people with a safe home and supportive environment, inspiring positive change and encouraging them to live independently in the community. 

Over the years, we have witnessed numerous success stories of individuals overcoming challenges and building a stable life with our support.

Who do we work with?
Based in Sheffield, we work with individuals with a history of homelessness, substance misuse, offending behaviour, and physical/mental health issues.

Person-centered support
Recognising that our residents have diverse and complex needs, we provide comprehensive, person-centred support. Our dedicated support workers and volunteers assist residents in accessing help from various medical and support service professionals.

Community collaboration
At Contix, we believe in the power of collaboration. We work closely with multiple agencies and local support services, including probation, third-sector organisations, and adult social care. Our excellent relationships with neighbouring boroughs ensure a coordinated approach to an individual's overall resettlement needs if necessary.

Name: Circle, Inc
Description:
We are pleased to present our capacity document, to showcase our ability to deliver affordable housing
communities. Our apartment-style, attainable housing comes complete with luxury amenities,
community gathering spaces and gated security for its residents.
Our team is comprised of two qualified co-developers with decades of experience both locally and
statewide.
Circle, Inc., chartered in 2002, is an Escambia County based non-profit Community Housing
Development Organization (CHDO). Circle maintains the CHDO designation both locally and
statewide.
We focus on five key areas of community development: attainable housing, economic development,
access to health care, education and recreation. Under our housing department, we have been involved

in the development of over 400 affordable homes in Escambia County. We have successfully co-
developed a local housing authority site by securing the funding. In addition, Circle has co-developed

attainable housing in Santa Rosa County, specifically in Milton, Florida. We work closely with local and
state government entities, including local community agencies.
As president of Circle, Inc, I have served the Escambia County and Pensacola community as the founder
and pastor of Calvary Christian Center for almost 40 years. The church has provided multiple programs
to the community in the form of low-cost day care, economic development, access to healthcare and recreation.

Name: Deane Estate Completions Ltd
Description:
Dealing with proposed, current and legacy schemes in directly employed roles over the last 18 years, a great many achievements have been made, including:

- More than 260 road & sewer adoptions and POS transfers completed.
- Tens of millions of pounds in bond reductions secured.
- Hundreds of thousands of pounds in commuted sum payments successfully contested.
- Tens of thousands of pounds in bond over run charges refunded.

Now running as a stand-alone company, we aim to work as an extension to your existing team, where we can provide support, or fully manage, adoption agreement and public open space transfer processes from day one, so that you can concentrate on your core business.

As part of dealing with legacy and historic sites, we are able to get bonds reduced or cancelled, freeing up headroom for new bonds to be put into place and thus current sites progressed. Bond headroom is a valuable resource for entering into new agreements and early cancellation removes the risk of over run charges being applied.

We are able to provide a range of services to suit estate completion needs of developers, management companies, NAVs, local highway or water authorities. Each request is reviewed with the client before we take something on, so that we can ensure there are no conflicts of interest between parties.

Legacy site resolution, negotiating technical approvals & legal agreements, reviewing CCTV surveys, regular H&S site visits and inspection are some of the services we can offer you.

At Community Modular, we empower the future of housing by supporting modular factory startups and scalable development initiatives. As a Certified B Corporation and Qualified Opportunity Zone Business (QOZB), we help manufacturers and developers accelerate the production of sustainable, cost-effective modular homes—providing real solutions to the housing crisis.

Name: First Wave Supply
Description:
First Wave Supply is a next-generation building materials company focused on delivering healthier, more resilient, and more responsible ways to build.

We distribute high-performance magnesium-cement systems that eliminate the toxins, fire risk, and moisture failures baked into conventional construction. No formaldehydes. No paper facers. No combustible assemblies. Just durable, code-ready materials that protect people, buildings, and long-term value.

Our mission is simple. Make it easier to build housing that is safer, faster to deliver, and built to last. Especially where durability, affordability, and risk reduction matter most.

Every product we supply is carefully vetted, transparently sourced, and supported with real documentation. Designed for builders, architects, and developers who want fewer problems on site and stronger assets over time. Delivered by First Wave Supply.

Name: Carib Housing
Description:
Carib Housings “Green Community” is a residential concept focused on the Caribbean islands. It stands for small-scale neighbourhoods in which diversity is encouraged. Families, singles, couples, people of age, with a disability or need for care are united in a casual way and look out for each other. In addition, Carib Housing promotes self-sufficient Eco Homes and Tiny Houses that are fire resistant and built to withstand hurricanes and earthquakes. At Carib Housing locations future residents will be living in Green Communities where own renewable energy sources (e.g., electricity, biogas) are applied. Each home is hooked up to a waste-management and waste-water management system which in turn is connected to surrounding landscaped “kitchen” gardens providing this little neighbourhood own and home-grown vegetables, herbs, flowers and various fruit.    
Other than most real estate platforms, Carib Housing is an alternative housing platform where (potential) tenants can interact with Carib Housing. This platform core business is to advertise and promote affordable hurricane and earthquake challenged yet fire resistant housing for those who embraces the self-sufficient living concept.

 رؤيتنا
أن نكون المطوّر العقاري الوطني الرائد في بناء مجتمعات سكنية عصرية تُعزز من جودة المعيشة وتُعبّر عن روح الهوية السعودية.

نُقدّم لعملائنا مشاريع مبتكرة مصممة بعناية لتلبي احتياجاتهم اليوم وتُلبّي أحلامهم غدًا، مدعومين بفريق محترف وشغوف يضع رضا العميل في مقدمة أولوياته.

خدماتنا
 تطوير المشاريع السكنية

We take a Housing First approach, because we know that a stable home is the foundation for rebuilding lives. From there, we connect people with the support they need—whether it’s finding a job, accessing healthcare, securing food and clothing, or receiving counseling.

Name: Samenbouw
Description:
Samenbouw is an architectural and social design project aimed at development of affordable homes. Samenbouw is an inclusive design toolbox, it includes a method and process of self-building a house and the co-creation tools for future homeowners, as well as designing a neighbourhood. We talk about a system because to achieve our goal within the current situation, in real estate tension, we need to disrupt traditional ways of building. 

In addition to being a toolbox, Samenbouw is also a building system. It revolutionizes the construction process by rethinking and simplifying each step, enabling even those without construction skills to assemble their homes effortlessly, as if they were assembling furniture.

The foundation, Stichting Samenbouw aims to build a neighbourhood of 40 homes in Brainport Smart District, in Helmond. 

Samenbouw is initiative of Bygg Architecture & Design and VDLP Architecten. It was developed in collaboration with Provinice Noord Branbant and Verberne Projecten.

Name: AMD Aserbaidschanische Mediziner in Deutschland e.V.
Description:
Der Verein wurde im Jahr 2020 gegründet und im Dezember 2020 durch Beschluss (02.12.2020, Nr. 7380) des Amtsgerichts Wiesbaden in das Vereinsregister eingetragen. Wir möchten Sie darüber informieren, dass der Verein der aserbaidschanischen Mediziner in Deutschland der erste Verein in der Bundesrepublik Deutschland ist, die Hunderte von aserbaidschanischen Medizinern vertreten wird, die in Deutschland leben und arbeiten.

Der Hauptaufgabe des Vereins ist es, einen Beitrag zu dem gegenseitigen Wissens- und Erfahrungsaustausches zwischen deutschen und aserbaidschanischen Medizinern zu leisten und eine kompetente Zusammenarbeit zwischen Ärzten zu etablieren.
 
Die in dieser Richtung geleistete Arbeit basiert auf der Stärkung der Beziehungen und des Austausches zwischen aserbaidschanischen Ärzten, Zahnärzten, Medizinstudenten und in Deutschland lebenden Wissenschaftlern.
 
Zu den Hauptzielen des Vereins gehört neben der Verbesserung der Berufsausbildung der aserbaidschanischen Mediziner auch die Unterstützung der Integration unserer Kollegen in die Deutsche Gesellschaft in den frühen Phasen ihrer beruflichen Laufbahn.
 
Nicht zuletzt ein besonderer Platz wird Schulungs- und Trainingsmaßnahmen in den Aktivitäten des Vereins eingeräumt. In diesem Zusammenhang stehen die Entwicklung von Bildungs- und Sozialprogrammen, die Organisation von Konferenzen und damit die Förderung der medizinischen Ausbildung im Vordergrund.
 
Gleichzeitig steht eine enge Zusammenarbeit des Vereins mit deutschen und internationalen Organisationen des gleichen Profils im Vordergrund.
 
In Anbetracht dessen beabsichtigt der Verein neben ordentliche Mitglieder immer einen Vorstand und Ehrenmitglieder von Fachleuten, um sich zu versammeln.

Name: ROI Property Group
Description:
In 2014, Rob Fuller founded ROI Property Group, LLC (ROI), a Real Estate Development company based out of California. Originally, ROI invested mainly in fix-and-flip operations for single-family homes and apartment buildings. From its inception until 2016, ROI flipped homes and apartments, including some luxury properties. Through heavy renovation, ROI improved the quality of their respective communities. Once finished, ROI either sold the homes to homebuyers as turnkey rentals or held them in a rental portfolio, earning a return from capital appreciation, as well as cash flow from rental income.

ROI didn’t stop at fix-and-flips. Starting in 2016, ROI shifted away from individual home renovations and started to focus on buying land for larger, new-home-build projects. 

ROI has centered its attention on growing markets such as the Colorado Springs and the Atlanta Metro statistical area. Both markets have seen increased home and rental prices, but because the cost of living is relatively affordable, people continue to migrate to these areas while maintaining proximity to the metropolis labor force.

Each of these points has made it advantageous for ROI to have several projects in these markets. ROI’s main strategy is to buy land, re-zone it, entitle it, complete site development like utilities and roads, and build homes for the purpose of holding in its own portfolio. ROI has many exit opportunities such as selling paper or finished lots to national homebuilders, or selling completed homes to institutional build-to-rent firms. Ultimately, ROI plans to sell for short term profit or hold for long term passive income. 

ROI Property Group is currently headquartered in Orem, Utah, just outside of Salt Lake City. As it continues to grow, ROI strives to find profitable projects that will bring more quality homes to the market.

Name: ECAHLI
Description:
ECAHLI is an integrated community infrastructure platform designed to deliver long-term, asset-backed solutions across housing, employment, essential services, and productive local economies.

The platform addresses structural gaps in underserved and underdeveloped regions by combining residential development, agricultural production, light manufacturing, healthcare, education, and community services into a single, scalable infrastructure model.

ECAHLI communities are planned as self-sustaining systems rather than isolated projects. Each development integrates land, utilities, social infrastructure, and economic activity to ensure long-term viability, measurable impact, and replicability across regions.

The model is structured to support blended finance, enabling collaboration between governments, private investors, development institutions, and strategic sponsors. Capital deployment prioritizes durable assets, employment creation, and locally anchored value chains.

ECAHLI is designed for jurisdictions seeking:

Sustainable housing delivery

Job creation and skills development

Productive use of underutilized land

Resilient community infrastructure

Long-term economic participation rather than aid dependency

The platform is currently advancing pilot developments with a focus on scalability, governance alignment, and institutional-grade execution.

Focus Areas

Integrated housing infrastructure

Agriculture and food systems

Light industry and local manufacturing

Healthcare and education facilities

Community services and utilities

Engagement

ECAHLI engages with:

Governments and public agencies

Institutional and private investors

Development finance institutions

Strategic and technology partners

All engagements are structured through formal briefings.

Name: Homes for Iowa
Description:
Homes for Iowa, Inc. (HFI) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit committed to tackling three of Iowa’s most pressing challenges: the shortage of affordable housing, the workforce gap, and high recidivism rates.

Through innovative partnerships, HFI builds affordable, high-quality homes while providing valuable trade skills to justice-involved individuals. Each home is constructed at the Newton Correctional Facility in Newton, IA where participants gain hands-on experience in carpentry, construction, and problem-solving—skills that prepare them for successful careers upon release.

The result is a powerful win-win solution:
1. Communities gain affordable homes to help attract and retain workforce.
2. Employers benefit from a pipeline of skilled laborers ready to contribute.
3. Returning citizens receive training and purpose, reducing recidivism and strengthening Iowa families.

Since its founding in 2019, Homes for Iowa has delivered hundreds of homes across the state to nearly all 99 counties creating opportunities for growth in small towns and urban neighborhoods alike. By uniting government agencies, local communities, businesses, and correctional programs, HFI demonstrates how collaboration can drive meaningful impact.

At our core, we believe that a safe home, a good job, and a second chance are the foundation of a stronger Iowa.

Name: Staywise
Description:
StayWise is a comprehensive mobile application designed exclusively for college students seeking comfortable and convenient accommodation options near their campus. With a user-friendly interface and a vast database of PGs (paying guest accommodations) and hostels, StayWise simplifies the daunting task of finding the perfect place to stay during your academic journey.

Discovering the ideal PG or hostel has never been easier. Using StayWise, students can browse through a wide range of listings, filtering them based on their preferences, such as budget, location, amenities, and room types. Detailed descriptions and high-quality images provide an accurate representation of each accommodation option, allowing students to make informed decisions.

With real-time availability updates, students can check the occupancy status of each PG or hostel, ensuring they secure their desired accommodation well in advance. StayWise also offers a convenient booking feature, enabling students to reserve their rooms seamlessly within the app, eliminating the need for tedious paperwork and multiple visits.

To enhance the overall user experience, StayWise goes beyond accommodation listings.
StayWise prioritizes student safety and well-being. It includes features such as user reviews and ratings for each PG or hostel, empowering students to make informed choices based on authentic experiences shared by their peers. Additionally, the app offers a reliable support system, allowing students to report any concerns or issues they may encounter during their stay.

Whether you are a local or an out-of-town student, StayWise aims to simplify your accommodation search and help you find a comfortable and welcoming space to call your own throughout your college years. Download StayWise today and embark on a hassle-free journey towards securing the perfect PG or hostel near your college!

Our approach combines innovative technology, strategic development efforts, and community welfare initiatives to transform the real estate landscape and urban development.

Our flagship initiatives, such as the Track Realties App and the Flood Mitigation and Urban Planning Project, highlight our commitment to leveraging technology and multidisciplinary research to create impactful, sustainable solutions in real estate and urban planning.

Name: Pri Studničke
Description:
Nachádza sa v tesnej blízkosti Železnej studničky a jeho lokácia ho predurčuje stať sa v Bratislave unikátnym projektom kvalitného mestského bývania v prírodnom prostredí.

Jednoducho, mesto aj les na jednom mieste.

Domov plný skvelých príležitostí – aj tak by sme mohli charakterizovať rezidenčné bývanie PRI STUDNIČKE. Blízkosť mesta a tesného susedstva lesoparku Železnej studničky robia z tohto miesta dokonalý priestor na príjemné a pohodlné bývanie. Zároveň vnímame obrovský potenciál tejto lokality do budúcnosti v súvislosti s plánovanou výstavbou globálneho vedecko-výskumného centra Eset Campus v tesnej blízkosti s naším projektom.

Vybrať si môžete zo širokej ponuky bytov. Na prízemí sa nachádzajú veľkorysé byty s predzáhradkami. Na typických podlažiach s 1,5, 2, 3 a 4-izbovými bytmi sme vytvorili balkóny s priestorom na stolovanie a trávenie voľného času. Na každom poschodí sú len tri, maximálne štyri byty s výťahom v strede schodiska, čo podporuje komorný charakter bývania.

Our corporate goal is to provide quality, affordable and sustainable housing and associated services for people in housing need whilst protecting the long-term viability of the Association.

The corporate goal is underpinned by several Strategic Objectives:
1.	To invest in its existing housing stock to ensure that the Association provides the highest standard of accommodation possible. 
2.	To provide a comprehensive and responsive customer service to tenants, sharing and factored owners which supports sustainable tenancies and delivers customer satisfaction. 
3.	To actively promote and support resident engagement in the management, maintenance, and development of their homes. 
4.	To contribute to the supply of high quality, accessible, secure, affordable, and sustainable homes where financially viable, whilst maximising community benefits. 
5.	To ensure that the Association’s work is underpinned by effective financial, administrative, and management processes set within a framework of effective corporate governance.
6.	To ensure that OVHA recruits and retains sufficiently trained and experienced Board members and suitably qualified staff and satisfies all health, safety and environmental requirements and legislation.

Name: ROM-D (Regionale OntwikkelingsMaatschappij Drechtsteden)
Description:
De Regionale Ontwikkelingsmaatschappij Drechtsteden werkt aan de economische ontwikkeling van onze regio en zet zich in voor versterken en verbeteren van bedrijfsterreinen en industriegebieden.

De belangrijkste doelstelling van de ROM-D is het meewerken aan het behoud en de creatie van werkgelegenheid, door via gebiedsontwikkeling werklocaties te ontwikkelen. Dit betreffen veelal ontwikkelingen en herontwikkelingen van bedrijfsterreinen.

ROM-D is vooral een uitvoeringsorganisatie en opereert vrij autonoom en proactief in de regio. Zij spot probleemlocaties, mogelijkheden en kansen en legt nauw contact met (betrokken) eigenaren om met hen in gesprek te geraken over mogelijke (her)ontwikkelingen en/of verplaatsingen.

De ROM-D onderzoekt de mogelijkheden door business case te vervaardigen, waarbij zo nodig subsidiemiddelen worden aangetrokken om onrendabele delen van de projecten te dekken. De ROM-D handelt daarbij altijd binnen de grenzen van de geoorloofde staatssteun. In de Drechtsteden gaat het vaak om vrijspelen van watergebonden locaties ten behoeve van bedrijven die daar behoefte aan hebben.

De ROM-D is te typeren als het ‘oliemannetje’ tussen markt en overheid. ROM-D werkt altijd intensief samen met betrokken overheden (kent daar de weg goed) en lokale marktpartijen.

Name: The Community Tent
Description:
At The Community Tent, we are on a mission to make a meaningful impact in the lives of homeless individuals and slum-dwellers through our innovative 3-phased approach.

Phase one of our project focuses on providing immediate assistance and supportive services to those in need. We meet individuals where they're at, offering a range of activities and resources designed to alleviate the burdens faced by those living in slums or experiencing homelessness.

In phase two, we take a proactive approach to address housing insecurity by repurposing abandoned buildings, both commercial and residential, to offer temporary and permanent accommodation to homeless individuals, families, and residents of slum areas. By providing safe and dignified housing options, we aim to create stability and hope for those in need.

But our work doesn't stop there. Phase three of The Community Tent is dedicated to tackling the root causes of homelessness and slum dwelling. Through community empowerment and grassroots organizing efforts, we strive to advocate for policy changes that prioritize the creation of affordable housing and the implementation of effective homelessness prevention programs. By working together, we can build a future where everyone has access to safe, secure, and affordable housing.

Join us in our mission to create positive change and build stronger, more resilient communities for all. Together, we can make a difference, one tent at a time.

Onze focus ligt voor de volle 100% op levensloopbestendigheid, het opbouwen van community ’s en het bevorderen van samenwerking.

is a mission-aligned C-Corporation wholly owned by the Viviscent Wellness Foundation.

 Created to drive sustainable economic growth in underserved communities,

While the Foundation leads with healing and service, Viviscent, Inc. builds the infrastructure, companies, and systems that make that mission scalable and economically viable.

Name: Headwaters Community Housing Trust
Description:
Developers can build housing. Local governments can incentivize housing. And businesses can invest in housing. Today, these three sectors have embraced that there is urgent need for homes that community members can afford. But they all face an unprecedented housing market and are unaccustomed to working together in such an environment. Headwaters Community Housing Trust (HCHT) exists to help them navigate how to raise capital for below-market homes, sell homes to community members, and assure that, once built, the homes will forever remain priced at levels locals can pay.

Bozeman, MT residents are struggling with ballooning home prices. This includes dedicated community members who maintain Bozeman’s popular parks and trails, own and operate businesses, plow our streets, teach our kids, and keep us healthy. Our city must expand opportunities for these vital workers to find homes that they can afford to buy. HCHT is helping government, businesses, and developers come together to finance and build the needed housing.

Name: Astley Business Park Action Group
Description:
The Astley & Mosley Common Warehouses Action Group is a community-led movement formed to protect residents, families and local schools from the devastating impact of industrial mega-warehouses being built in the heart of our neighbourhood.

What began as a handful of concerned residents has quickly grown into a network of hundreds, united by one belief: our community deserves better.

We are campaigning for:
	•	Transparency and accountability in planning decisions.
	•	Immediate mitigation measures such as sound barriers, mature trees and stricter environmental protections.
	•	Health and safety to be prioritised, with urgent review of 24/7 operations, fire risks and air quality near homes and schools.
	•	Fair treatment for residents, many of whom now face unsellable homes, financial hardship and mental health struggles as a result of this development.

Our work includes:
	•	Coordinating legal advice and Freedom of Information requests.
	•	Engaging local and national media to highlight what has happened.
	•	Gathering evidence of noise, pollution and property damage.
	•	Lobbying MPs, councillors and key stakeholders for change.

We are not opposed to development, but we are opposed to reckless planning that puts profit before people.

Together, we are determined to hold decision makers to account and to fight for a healthier, safer, and fairer future for Astley and Mosley Common.

Name: Aequo Foundation
Description:
Aequo Foundation is a nonprofit dedicated to advancing racial and economic equity by addressing the root causes of poverty through real estate, education, healthcare access, and wealth-building opportunities. We believe all people deserve to live with dignity, security, and purpose, especially those historically excluded from prosperity, including Black and Brown communities, women, and working-class families across the country.

We develop and support housing and community-based solutions that are scalable, sustainable, and people-centered. From affordable housing to workforce co-living models like Carehaus, we design spaces that not only provide shelter but also integrate services like job training, food security, childcare, and health support. Our resident services approach strengthens both individuals and neighborhoods, ensuring people don’t just survive, but thrive.

Through our Founders Program, we support non-traditional developers—including women and emerging Black and Brown real estate leaders—by offering access to capital, technical assistance, and networks. By expanding who gets to build and own in our communities, we're reshaping the future of equitable development.

Our work is grounded in deep listening, data-driven planning, and community-led design. We don’t parachute in—we build trust, grow local capacity, and stay accountable to those we serve.

Aequo means equity. It’s not just our name—it’s our North Star.

If you believe in building a future where prosperity is shared and dignity is non-negotiable, we’d love to connect.

Torni a casa, trovi il caos e tre messaggi arrabbiati nel gruppo WhatsApp. Ti suona familiare?

Un'app che parla la lingua della convivenza moderna. Organizzazione intelligente che si adatta al tuo ritmo, senza drammi.
Per chi la facciamo:
 Studenti che vogliono studiare, non litigare
 Professionisti che cercano una casa, non un campo di battaglia

Cosa ci rende speciali:
 Semplice come respirare - Se tua nonna la usa, è perfetta
 Unisce invece di dividere - Meno conflitti, più aperitivi insieme
 Bella da guardare - Anche l'organizzazione può essere elegante
 Rispetta il pianeta - Meno sprechi, più consapevolezza
Tecnologia React Native e Supabase, sicurezza GDPR, sincronizzazione istantanea. La parte noiosa? Ce ne occupiamo noi.
Cerchiamo complici: sviluppatori visionari, università innovative, gestori illuminati. Se credi che la convivenza possa essere migliore, parliamone.
Il nostro sogno: un mondo dove "Chi pulisce il bagno?" non rovina più la serata.
Perché la casa non è solo dove dormi. È dove vivi.

Name: Tempe Coalition for Affordable Housing
Description:
Tempe Coalition for Affordable Housing (TCAH) is redefining how affordable housing is created and sustained in Tempe. We envision a community where every person has a safe and secure place to call home, allowing them to thrive in life and contribute to a stronger city.
Rising housing costs are pushing too many residents out of stability. That’s why we purchase, develop, and manage high-quality properties to keep them permanently affordable. By owning and stewarding these homes, we prevent sudden rent increases, ensuring long-term security for individuals and families with incomes up to 80% of the median income.
Our approach is collaborative and data-driven. We partner with developers, the City of Tempe, and financial experts to expand affordable housing, integrate inclusive communities, and strengthen local development. With every project, we demonstrate how permanently affordable housing improves health outcomes, economic stability, and neighborhood resilience.
Together with our partners, we’re building a model of housing equity that other cities can follow.

Description:

Nestled in Brazil’s vibrant Atlantic Forest, Ecohaus stands as a beacon of innovation in sustainable living. This pioneering project goes beyond being a retreat—it’s a place where education and lifestyle converge to teach self-reliance through permaculture and a deep connection with nature.

Educational and Regenerative Approach

At Ecohaus, we embrace permaculture principles to create systems that mimic nature’s ways, aiming to establish sustainable agricultural practices that respect and enhance the local biodiversity. Our workshops, led by seasoned experts, are designed to pass on knowledge and skills that empower individuals to live sustainably, using resources wisely and creating productive ecological environments.

Connection with Nature

Intimately integrated into its biodiverse setting, Ecohaus offers immersive experiences that deepen the understanding of natural rhythms and ecological balance. Name: Weatherizers Without Borders
Description:
Our organization started with the vision of adapting a successful US public policy, the Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP), designed to subsidize weatherization services for low-income households. Seeing the need for public policy programs such as WAP outside of the US, specifically in Latin America, WWB was founded as a global partnership of expertise with the goal of expanding weatherization programs to communities that stand to benefit. 

Our Mission is to develop weatherization programs around the world, collaborating with governments, NGOs, and private stakeholders to build successful sustainable home improvement policies that address local needs of low-income communities across the globe. We want to create a world where adequate and affordable housing is a universal right, and everyone has access to sustainable housing that supports their health, safety, quality of life, and efficient use of resources. Our organization works to create a forum for weatherization and home sustainability knowledge and innovations of like-minded agencies and professionals. We work together to develop weatherization ecosystems and piliot projects, which then turn into programs that generate or improve sustainable home improvement public policies in different cities and countries.

Benefits of weatherization come in the form of improved health outcomes, energy efficiency, and money saved. Weatherization works as an indirect way of promoting upward social mobility, alleviating poverty, and combatting climate change.

Name: Association of Housing and Community Development Law
Description:
After serious consideration, several senior attorneys have decided there is merit to create a bar association for those whose practice is exclusively focused on affordable housing and community development (“HCD”). While the American Bar Association (ABA) Forum on Affordable Housing and Community Development Law (the “Forum”) has provided and continues to provide quality programming and service to its members, some who have been active in this practice area have come to the conclusion that the HCD bar could be well served by an association that operates from a platform separate and apart from a large organization.
There is a consensus  that (i) the ability to express opinions and take positions on issues important to the practice area in a timely manner should be unrestricted, ii) an independent bar association exclusively serving the HCD bar could operate in a manner that delivers more for the dues dollar (rather than subsidizing other non-HCD practice areas), and (iii) an independent association will have greater flexibility to work collaboratively with government agencies 
In view of the foregoing, the Association of Housing and Community Development Law (“AHCDL”) has been formed, with an initial focus on advocacy, which has been impeded by an umbrella organization’s requirements that mandate approval by a hierarchical chain to address to outside entities and, even after approval, prohibits anything from being represented as a position of the organization. The new independent AHCDL will be able to speak in its own name on positions of importance to its members. As the AHCDL matures, it will become an “Advocate” for the communities it serves and the members its supports.,” finding ways to educate the HCD bar, convene our members to develop best practices to better serve clients, advocate for policies and procedures that will advance the cause of developing healthy communities and collaborate with public agencies to develop practical solutions to policy initiatives

Name: everyplace
Description:
Everyplace partners with refugee communities to create beauty, belonging, and dignity in the places they're living right now—because life doesn't pause in displacement, and dignity can't wait for policy to catch up.

The Problem We Address

More than 75% of refugees live away from home for years, sometimes decades. Yet the spaces they inhabit—tents, camps, warehouses, shipping containers—were never designed for long-term living. These environments communicate impermanence and exclusion, even as life continues: children grow up, families form, milestones are marked. When humanitarian response focuses solely on survival, it overlooks the fact that people are also living. Millions spend years, sometimes entire childhoods, in places that erode dignity, hope, and any sense of home.

Our Approach

Everyplace partners with local leaders in refugee communities and supports  communities’ visions, plans and priorities to making the places they live right now feel more like home.  This includes gardens, murals, gathering spaces, and the reimagining of everyday environments. We do not replace humanitarian aid or policy reform; we intervene where those systems fall short, working to restore humanity inside long-term displacement.

We follow the lead of communities already doing this work. The impulse to create home exists. We recognize it, fund it, and amplify it, bringing these acts of agency from the margins to the center of global displacement response.

Our Origins

Everyplace builds on foundational research conducted through Clark University's Home Ground Lab, which developed frameworks demonstrating that beauty is not optional in humanitarian contexts, it is infrastructure for dignity, healing, and belonging.

Structure

Everyplace operates as a project of Players Philanthropy Fund, a 501(c)(3) public charity (EIN: 27-6601178). Contributions are tax-deductible to the fullest extent of the law.

 Working internationally

We support renters, homebuyers, homeowners, and individuals experiencing homelessness through education, advocacy, and resource access. By partnering with stakeholders, we work to expand housing opportunities, promote policies, and build sustainable communities across San Bernardino and Riverside Counties. 

ISCHC drives impact through advocacy, partnerships, education, and innovative housing solutions.  

Our Collective Priorities are housed within our working pillars.   
1- Educate & Advocate for Public Policy- Advocate for local, state, and federal policies that expand affordable housing opportunities in the Inland Empire, including funding programs. 
2- Wealth Building and Preservation- Support housing stability through wealth-building strategies that expand financial access and build regional resources. 
3- Expansion of Housing Solutions- Develop and explore innovative housing strategies, projects, and programs that increase the housing supply and affordability in the region.  
4- Increasing Regional Involvement- Build cross-sector partnerships to align regional housing efforts and foster collaboration. 
 
BankOn Inland SoCal Coalition- Ensures there is access to safe and affordable bank and credit union accounts. 

OUR WORK 
The ISCHC works across the Inland Empire, collaborating with over 300 members, including developers, nonprofits, financial institutions, local governments, health organizations, and faith-based groups. We serve urban, suburban, and rural communities, ensuring that renters, homebuyers, homeowners, and individuals experiencing homelessness have access to safe, affordable housing. Through strategic partnerships and advocacy, we address housing challenges and support policies that foster community development and thriving neighborhoods.

Name: Fairmount Indigo CDC Collaborative
Description:
The Fairmount Indigo CDC Collaborative (FICC) is made up of three Boston community development corporations (CDCs): Codman Square Neighborhood Development Corporation, Dorchester Bay Economic Development Corporation, and Southwest Boston Community Development Corporation. FICC seeks racial, climate, and health equity and justice in our communities through: increased affordable housing, excellent public transit, pathways to good jobs and financial resilience, sustainability innovations, expanded green space and opportunities for active living, and vibrant public places. To see these objectives through, we organize local residents and businesses and coordinate action with neighborhood partners, public agencies, political representatives, and social investors. FICC is currently focused on bringing climate justice and health equity to the communities it serves through a combination of community organizing and legislative advocacy.

Our goals include the following: 
1. Collective political power
2. Economic and wealth-building opportunities
3. Transit-oriented affordable housing development
4. Sustainability, climate justice, and health equity
5. Equitable public transit
