Name: Gannon lifestyle communities
Description:
The Gannon’s know a real community can’t be manufactured; it has to be nurtured.
One of the first differences you’ll discover that distinguishes a Gannon Built Retirement Estate from most other retirement villages around Australia, is that it is being built by a family rather than a big development company, organisation or charity.
The Gannon family has built a solid reputation over the last 25 years for building some of the most successful retirement estates in this country. Tom and Michael Gannon are involved every step of the way and create their estates based on simple family values and respect for the people who will live there.

Gannon History
The Gannon family began developing retirement estates in the late 1980’s.  Originally from regional NSW work in the construction industry had seen them move across the country, finally settling in Adelaide.  The movement of the family across many regional towns in NSW, Vic and SA would provide a detailed knowledge that would become invaluable in the development of regional retirement estates.
Tom snr and Mavis Gannon were amongst the pioneers in the development of modern retirement estates.  The first development was Vermont Retirement Estate, located in Adelaide south western suburbs.  Today it is part of the LendLease portfolio, but a review of the Estate’s features shows that our model and business strategy was very much realised from this first development.

Name: Evolve CIC
Description:
Evolve CIC stands as a beacon of hope and transformation in the landscape of social care and rehabilitation in the United Kingdom. Established with the profound purpose of addressing two critical societal challenges - homelessness and reoffending among military veterans and prison leavers - Evolve CIC has carved out a unique and vital role. This Community Interest Company (CIC) not only acknowledges the complex needs of these groups but actively works towards creating sustainable solutions for their reintegration into society.

The founding of Evolve CIC is rooted in a deep understanding of the multifaceted challenges faced by military veterans returning to civilian life and individuals transitioning out of the prison system. These groups often find themselves navigating a world that feels alien and unforgiving, where the road to a stable and fulfilling life is fraught with obstacles. Homelessness and the risk of reoffending become stark realities for many, underscoring a gap in societal support and resources. Evolve CIC was established to bridge this gap, offering a comprehensive approach that encompasses housing, training, support, and employment opportunities.

At the helm of Evolve CIC is a board of highly accomplished and experienced professionals, each bringing a wealth of knowledge and expertise to the table. This strong leadership team is complemented by a visionary CEO, whose passion and dedication serve as the driving force behind the organization's mission. Together, they form a formidable force, united in their commitment to fostering change and making a tangible impact in the lives of those they served.

BHP PURPOSE 

The organization’s main purpose shall be to enhance wealth building to facilitate access to affordable housing and to support neighborhood revitalization in the City of Brockton by:

b. Serving as a leader in the Financial Literacy arena.

d. Working with City-based and other foundations, and other not-for-profit organizations.

Whether you're looking for a cozy studio apartment, a shared house with roommates, or a convenient dormitory near campus, Zeuty has you covered. But Zeuty is more than just a housing marketplace – it's a community. Connect with fellow students, chat with potential roommates, and get insider tips on the best neighborhoods and amenities. With Zeuty, finding your dream housing has never been easier or more enjoyable.

Stay tuned for the launch of Zeuty on iOS and Android platforms, and get ready to swipe your way to the perfect student housing experience. Don't settle for less – find your ideal home with Zeuty today!

Name: Author Matt Smith
Description:
Hi there!

I'm Matt Smith.  The author of I AM APPLICANT. 

A book detailing a from-the heart account of our family’s seven year oddysey in renovating and moving into our new home in Sausalito, California. I never knew about the housing crisis in the USA, until we purchased our new property and came face to face with endless bureaucracy. Finally, after seven years, we were only able to live in our home. 

Having lived through the experience of a enacting a significant remodel in Marin County, California, I have seen first hand the many challenges that a sponsor of a Proposed Project must overcome.

Using data-driven approaches, this book delves into the specific aspects of the Design Review and Building Department reviews of proposed new development that are failing. Or, not working as they were intended, and adding to the significant costs of building new homes in this state.

Learn More: iamapplicant.com

We build the fastest, strongest, and most money-saving homes in Canada — with delivery in less than 90 days and assembly in just hours.

Our homes are:

 2x more energy efficient than traditional builds

 2x stronger than wood-framed homes — tested to withstand winds double a Category 5 hurricane

 Designed to create financial freedom — perfect for backyard suites, AirBnB units, or affordable starter homes

Every home is fabricated using advanced composite materials that outperform traditional wood in strength, longevity, and sustainability — while diverting an average of 450,000 plastic bottles from landfills and oceans per unit.

We’re launching in Halifax, Nova Scotia — but our mission is national. Canada needs millions of new homes, and we’re ready to scale to meet the challenge.

Whether you're a homeowner, architect, investor, or engineer — let’s talk.
This is housing reimagined. Built in Nova Scotia. Built to last.

Description:

Mrs. Willis holds a Master of Science in Nursing, dual Certified Family and Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioner, with over twenty years’ experience working in the medical field and social services sector. They are committed to tearing down the negative stigma which has been given to residential homes by showing their organization will thrive on being “A Place Where Hope, Family, and Grace create a Divine Embrace.”

"He comforts us in all our troubles so that we can comfort others. When they are troubled, we will be able to give them the same comfort GOD has given us". 2 Corinthians 1:4 NLT

Description:

20 years later this mission hasn’t changed! During this time, we have assembled a group of dedicated employees and developed 19 communities comprising over 3,000 units. Our goal is not to be the biggest but be our best at what we do.

Name: Game of Homes
Description:
Game of Homes is a fun, engaging workshop designed to tackle serious issues in social housing — whether that’s complaints, service redesign, policy changes or something else entirely. 

It’s grounded in behavioural science and self determination – autonomy, competence, relationships: meaningful – and gives participants an equal voice. 

Gamification is not new but it brings out the child in all of us, generating creativity, trust and new ways of thinking

Over a few dynamic hours, people co-create real solutions using a structured flow, striking scenery boards, and powerful prompts. It’s playful on the surface, but the outcomes are realistic and actionable. 

It’s always facilitated by people who’ve worked inside housing providers, so we really understand the pressures, the politics, and the importance of transparency and accountability.

Why is it needed? Because top-down solutions built on outdated frameworks just don’t cut it anymore—people want genuine, authentic engagement. 

If you’ve got a problem you’re stuck on, this might just unlock it—and get your colleagues talking.

Name: HOMELESS-R-US CORP.
Description:
Homeless-R-Us is a 154-acre diverse therapeutic community designed to house, employ, and rehabilitate the homeless population. We provide temporary and permanent housing and various resources. The facility is a one-stop destination that provides clients within an eight-step program that teaches them necessary life-skills. This collaborative approach to empowerment is the keystone to developing self-sufficiency in the clients. The steps include personal development, vocational training, substance abuse counseling, interpersonal skills building, community involvement, leisure activities, independent living skills, and many more. It aids individuals and families, frequently homeless, as they move from dependent, into independent, self-sufficient lifestyles. 
On-site services include:

Name: European Housing Coop
Description:
The housing crisis in cities across Europe isn’t only a shitty situation for some individuals. It has the dangerous potential to tear us as a society and the dream of a united and open Europe apart: A Europe in which we can choose to live where adventures, love, and work are calling us — or one day will.

Let's keep this vision alive for the future - by using the core of a 150 year-old proven model to build long term affordable housing.

We want to give cooperative, affordable housing a real boost across cities in Europe. How? By reinterpreting one of the original core principles of coops: “Alone we can do so little; together we can do so much”. We bring citizens, local cooperatives, European companies, tech startups, owners of underused buildings and impact capital in a new mode of collaboration. To kickstart the (re)development of local lighthouse projects for sustainable living - which all become part of a growing pan-European network.

It's a big adventure:
We build one umbrella European Housing Coop (EHC) that we all co-own. And while different members contribute and benefit in different ways, we all unite around one shared vision: Creating a better and more sustainable way of urban living for millions of Europeans.

Now: Do you want to join us as a citizen or a company when we turn this vision into a pilot across 3 European cities - starting in Spring 2026? Then let us know know on www.housingcoop.eu!

Name: Profilex Modular Solutions

The NAA is the leading vocie for the rental housing industry and trusted partner.  NAA serves 153 affiliates, over 82,000 members and the more than 10 million rental housing units they operate globally.

Name: White Horse Housing Association
Description:
We are a specialist rural housing association that has successfully built, managed and improved affordable homes in rural areas since 1984.
We only build homes in rural areas and currently have 430 properties across 55 separate villages in Wiltshire, Swindon and Somerset.
Our stated purpose is: To be a trusted provider of excellent services and affordable, sustainable homes, working together with rural communities.
We pride ourselves on providing a high quality, personalised and responsive service that meets the needs of our residents. We understand local issues and have a record of supporting residents to help them sustain their tenancies. This has resulted in high levels of satisfaction and engagement.
Our Purpose and Values support our work and set our strategic direction. These have a strong rural focus, not just about the provision of housing, but also maintaining the vitality and viability of rural communities.
In celebrating more than 40 years of excellent service we are determined to maintain our ethos of putting our residents first by:
Ensuring properties are only let to people in housing need with a local connection.
* Working with local town and parish councils to provide new, modern rented homes where possible.
* Ensuring empty properties are attended to, allocated and let to the new resident as quickly as possible.
* Helping to adapt homes where possible to meet current and future resident * needs.

House prices and private rents in rural villages are out of the reach of many on low incomes and we are committed to providing much needed affordable homes and keeping villages sustainable.
We are a charity registered with the Regulator of Social Housing, a Community Benefit Society and a member of the National Housing Federation.

Name: Davis Community Meals and Housing
Description:
Davis Community Meals and Housing is a 501c3 nonprofit, nondenominational organization whose mission is to provide low-income and homeless individuals and families with housing, food, and supportive services to help them rebuild their lives. Founded in 1990, DCMH is governed by a board of directors and operates programs ranging from free meals to supportive housing. 

For more information on DCMH and its services please visit https://daviscommunitymeals.org.

For information on Paul's Place, a project of DCMH that is a first-of-its-kind, multi-functional, four-story building that offers emergency, transitional and permanent supportive housing, please visit https://paulsplacedavis.org/.

Name: Hallie Development

Name: STADTBAU GmbH PFORZHEIM
Description:
Wir sind das kommunale Wohnungsunternehmen in Pforzheim. Seit über 50 Jahren steht die STADTBAU GmbH PFORZHEIM für die Aufgabe, Pforzheimer Bürgerinnen und Bürgern ein Zuhause zu bieten.

Wir bauen und vermieten preisgünstigen Wohnraum und ermöglichen so, dass auch Familien, Ältere und sozial Schwächere bezahlbar wohnen können. Dies ist das Kernthema unseres sozialen Auftrags, aber dennoch nur ein Teil unserer Tätigkeit.

Die STADTBAU prägt entscheidend das Stadtbild von Pforzheim. Mit jährlichen Investitionen in Millionenhöhe sanieren und bauen wir in der ganzen Stadt. Alles was wir tun und bauen ist dabei nicht auf reine Zweckerfüllung beschränkt. Wir sind bestrebt, funktional und gestalterisch intelligente Projekte umzusetzen, die unsere Stadt in mehrfacher Hinsicht voranbringen. Auch das städtebauliche Erscheinungsbild dieser Projekte und ihre Auswirkungen auf die Entwicklung der Stadtgesellschaft und die Stadtökologie sind für uns deshalb von großer Bedeutung. Daneben werden denkmalgeschützte Gebäude – als historische Bausteine für die Identität unserer Stadt von großer Wichtigkeit – durch uns erhalten, respektvoll saniert und einer dauerhaften, angemessenen Nutzung zugeführt.

Als kommunales Wohnungsunternehmen sind wir uns unserer Verantwortung für eine nachhaltige Entwicklung Pforzheims ganz besonders bewusst. Neben der Berücksichtigung sozialer und städtebaulicher Belange leisten wir durch sukzessiven Einsatz modernster Technik in unserem Wohnungsbestand einen wichtigen Beitrag zur Schonung natürlicher Ressourcen und zum Erhalt unserer Umwelt.

Description:
Soul Housing was established in 2016 to provide “safe discharge” and housing solutions to under-resourced individuals throughout Los Angeles County. With over 1,000 licensed boarding home beds available amongst 60 locations, our mission is to provide a “safe place to heal” to those facing homelessness or housing insecurity, in hopes of helping them regain independence and build a better life.

Soul Housing is currently contracted as a Community Supports provider for Recuperative Care (Medical Respite), Short Term Post Hospitalization Housing, and Day Habilitation. 

With respect to Recuperative Care, we offer the following services:
1. 24/7 supervision and post-hospital medical care
2. Medication assistance and monitoring
3. Daily vital monitoring and weekly care assessments
4. Psychiatric and medical professionals on call 24/7
5. Onsite housing coordinators and community health workers
6. Coordination with case management and housing transition service providers
7. Scheduling coordination for medical appointments and transportation
8. Assistance with applying for housing assistance, DMV, and new benefit programs
9. On site drug and alcohol counselors
10. Life skills training

Home Safe Housing was conceived by Prowgress, a Hague based social impact fund and UK Structured Finance, a social investment banking boutique, to deliver affordable homes across England.

Both Prowgress and UKSF are ‘Social Entrepreneurs’ who seek a capped fee based return for the provision of social good. The partnership has resulted in the current Home Safe delivery team (which also includes a raft of professional advisors from property, legal and financial worlds). 

Home Safe Housing operates all over England.  We are always extending our local authority partnerships and are currently acquiring homes in over 20 Broad Rental Market Areas, including most in London and many in Essex, Kent, Surrey and Sussex.

We do not own any studio flats, but can consider any property with at least one separate bedroom.  If you have properties to sell or you are a council seeking to reduce your waiting lists, please do get in touch.

Name: American Indian Community Development Corporation
Description:
The American Indian Community Development Corporation provides culturally unique initiatives, housing, and entrepreneurial programs that strengthen American Indian communities.
Withdrawal Management: AICDC has served as the provider for Hennepin County Chemical Withdrawal / Detoxification Services since 2002. The facility is located at 1800 Chicago Avenue South, Minneapolis, MN. With over 7,000 persons admitted each year* services include withdrawal management, chemical health assessment and referral, mental health referrals, in-house support groups and other referrals based on specific needs of our clients.
Homeless Services: During the COVID-19 pandemic AICDC provided basic services to over 11,600 homeless individuals at its mobile Hygiene Services Area and KOLA Overnight Drop-in Center.  In August 2020, AICDC raised $7.7 million for the acquisition, rehabilitation and operation of Homeward Bound Temporary Living facility at 2012 Cedar Ave South in Minneapolis.  This 50-bed, 24-hour, emergency shelter provides the region’s first homeless shelter targeted to American Indians. 
AICDC has been the Twin Cities primary American Indian Developer since its inception in 1992.  AICDC believes in a sustainability model for all of its developments. AICDC is conservative in its approach, always keeping its mission at the forefront of all programming, planning, and development. 
Many Rivers Group LLC - Real Estate Company: To help make homeownership a reality, AICDC created Many Rivers Group, LLC, a culturally sensitive real estate company aimed at closing that homeownership gap. To increase homeownership opportunities for American Indian families.
To foster the development of life skills, such as household budgeting, financial management, and home maintenance. To foster community development by strengthening the social and economic bonds of Native communities through increased homeownership.

Name: Niagara Development Group
Description:
At Niagara Development Group, our focus is aimed at Building Financially Sustainable Dwelling Units For All no matter your social, economic, or financial situation. 

When it comes to residential construction, the construction industry is stuck in the past. Prolonged construction timeframe, outdated practices, and custom builds lead to increased construction costs, unobtainable home prices, and drawn out wait times for home owners to move in. 

Our solution to the looming housing shortage crisis is to build smart, sustainable, and efficient. Customization is one of the biggest factors when it comes to time and cost. By eliminating specific materials that cost a fortune or take months to get delivered, we can still maintain a high level of quality AND be able to erect homes faster. 

Our goal is to bring back the "starter home" for so many first time home buyers, retirees, and empty nesters. We fully understand that with all of our available product options that this will not be for everyone. We are not here to compete with the builders or developers that are constructing the 4 or 5 bedroom homes with the movie theatre basement or spacious primary bedroom. We are looking to build 1 and 2 bedroom homes for people who cannot afford $400,000+ homes or are looking to downsize and have less to maintain. This will provide home owners with a home that they can have equity in and call their own. If they outgrow this home in a few years, they can sell and upgrade to something bigger with more features or hold on to the property and make it a rental for supplemental income. 

At NDG, we feel that we have a great product and opportunity to help some many people accomplish their dreams of home ownership without being stressed about a large mortgage or becoming "house poor". Your goals, are our mission.

Our model addresses social isolation and housing affordability for older people, helping them live with dignity in welcoming, family-style homes close to local amenities.

Name: Building Industry Association of Okaloosa and Walton Counties, Inc.
Description:
When you become a member of the BIA, you will become one of over 250 local BIA members, will join one of more than 800 local builder associations in the nation and you automatically will become a member of the 10,000-plus corporate-member Tallahassee-based Florida Home Builders Association (FHBA), and the National Association of Home Builders(NAHB) in Washington, D.C.

You are a part of a 220,000 corporate-member network and have access to a wide range of information, activities, and services – as well as volunteer opportunities that will contribute to the success of your business. About one-third of NAHB’s members are home builders and/or remodelers. The remaining members are associates working in closely related fields within the housing industry, such as mortgage finance and building products and services.

The BIA’s cornerstone is its governmental affairs, political action, and legal defense programs designed to promote and protect homeownership opportunities in Northwest Florida. Membership is available in Builder and Associate categories and is further tailored to your specific needs through BIA council and committee participation. You will find a network of peers in each council and committee discussing and addressing the issues you deal with every day.

We invite you to join us. Membership allows you to meet industry peers, stay informed of crucial issues, gain a competitive edge, and get involved in your community. By becoming a member of the BIA, you allow yourself access to the heart of your profession.

People recognize your company as a leader in the industry when you join the BIA. As a member, you pledge to your customers, peers, and staff that you and your services are highly qualified and adhere to enhanced standards of professionalism.

These comprise Guild Cottages; Shakespeare Court, Mary Newlands, John Roberts and St Joseph’s Homestead.

Our Mission is to provide low-cost, self-contained accommodation for older people of limited financial means. In all we offer accommodation to 47 residents. Our philosophy is to encourage independent living. We also offer support to the residents through our two part time scheme managers. Our scheme managers  regularly visit the residents in their own homes. We also run three or four outings each year for residents. We offer the residents support with benefit claims but also encourage their families to be involved.

Name: Lets Rebuild Tuskegee
Description:
Let’s Rebuild Tuskegee – Organization Description

Let’s Rebuild Tuskegee is a nonprofit organization dedicated to revitalizing Tuskegee, Alabama, through community development, housing innovation, and civic engagement. Founded in 2016, our mission is to restore pride, opportunity, and sustainability in Tuskegee by addressing abandoned properties, creating pathways to homeownership, and empowering residents through education and investment.

Our Core Programs

Clean-Up Program: Mobilizing volunteers to clear abandoned properties, cut overgrown lawns, remove debris, and beautify public spaces to ensure safer, cleaner neighborhoods.

Rent-to-Own Housing Initiative: Developing new construction homes that allow residents to build equity and transition from renters to homeowners, supported by financial literacy and credit improvement programs.

Community Investment Vehicle: Engaging alumni, investors, and community members to channel resources directly into sustainable projects that create long-term growth.

Membership & Engagement

We offer membership tiers for students, alumni, community members, and supporters, giving individuals the opportunity to have a voice in shaping Tuskegee’s future. Members drive the direction of the organization through active participation in committees such as Construction, Fundraising, Community Planning & Development, Political Engagement, and more.

Vision

To transform Tuskegee into a thriving model of community-driven development by creating clean, safe spaces; building affordable and innovative housing; and fostering economic opportunities that benefit generations to come.

At Let’s Rebuild Tuskegee, we’re not just improving buildings — we’re rebuilding hope, opportunity, and legacy.

Key Features:
 Sustainable Housing: EFCC prioritizes sustainability, offering residents eco-friendly, energy-efficient homes that reduce environmental impact.
 Enercea Turbines: Our underwater turbines harness the energy of ocean currents, providing clean and reliable power for our communities, day and night.
 Global Reach: EFCC's innovative approach is not limited by borders. We're committed to exporting our technology and housing solutions worldwide.

Join us on our journey to redefine urban living for the better. Follow our page to stay updated on our latest developments, sustainable initiatives, and opportunities to get involved.

Name: Jolshiri Abashon
Description: Jolshiri Abashon, a sister concern of Army Housing Scheme (AHS) was incorporated on April 11, 2011 as a company limited by guarantee under The Companies Act 1994. Jolshiri Abashon intends to execute the project by developing around 2136 acres of land at Rupgonj Upazilla, Narayanganj. The project will be implemented in ten years in three different time phases. The estimated project cost is $2 billion. The project is being primarily funded by the money collected from the military officers. Limited sales and investment on residential and commercial plots are also providing significant funds. Major General Md Hasan Uz Zaman, ndu, afwc, psc, M Phil is the Chairman of the Board of Directors of Jolshiri Abashon. The project has been operating under strong supervision and effective control of Managing Director Brigadier General Muhammad Saifur Rahman, SUP, ndc, afwc, psc, M Phil. The registered office of the Jolshiri Abashon is situated at Zia colony, Dhaka Cantonment, Dhaka- 1206.

Name: Stanislaus County Affordable Housing Corporation
Description:
Stanislaus County Affordable Housing Corporation (STANCO) is a California 501(c) (3) non-profit corporation formed in 1991 under the sponsorship of the United Way of Stanislaus County. Since that time, STANCO has acquired numerous properties and constructed scores of units – some of which were retained under STANCO for affordable housing, and some of which were sold at a below-market rate to eligible families in the community. In other instances, we have acquired and renovated distressed housing properties for reuse as single- or multi-family housing for low-income and special needs families.  

STANCO has demonstrated a successful history in providing transitional and permanent supportive housing to individuals with mental health disabilities, as well as owning and operating housing units at rent below market rates for persons who meet certain income eligibility as required by various governmental regulatory agreements. More recently, STANCO has branched into providing housing and shelter program management services for various governmental agencies. All told, at peak we currently own or manage more than 250 units.

https://linktr.ee/somoscoliving

Name: Bronx Begeleid Wonen
Description:
Bronx Begeleid Wonen is een kleinschalige, sinds 2016 HKZ -gecertificeerde, zorgaanbieder, gespecialiseerd in het bieden van woonbegeleiding aan jongeren en (jong) volwassenen met een verscheidenheid aan problematiek, waaronder een licht verstandelijke beperking, (psycho)sociale/forensische-, psychiatrische- en gedragsproblematiek. 

Bronx biedt onder andere (woon)begeleiding en huisvesting waarbij cliënten (semi-) zelfstandig wonen. De intensiteit van de begeleiding is afhankelijk van de hulpvragen en mate van zelfstandigheid van de cliënt en kan uiteenlopen van bijvoorbeeld 12 uur begeleiding per week tot aan 24-uurs begeleiding per etmaal. Begeleiding kan variëren van ondersteuning op het gebied van sociaal functioneren, ondersteuning bij de regie en de uitvoer van ADL/HDL taken (zelfzorg en huishoudelijke taken) en hulp bij financiën tot aan het zoeken van een passende dagbesteding (school, stage/werk en een positieve vrijetijdsbesteding). Zo nodig kan er ondersteuning worden geboden in het zoeken naar een passende (GGZ-)behandeling. 

Voor elke cliënt wordt er in gezamenlijkheid met de cliënt en het betrokken netwerk een individueel begeleidingsplan opgesteld. De intensiteit van de begeleiding en de leerdoelen van de cliënten wordt vastgesteld aan de hand van de zelfredzaamheidsmatrix (ZRM). Tijdens evaluatiemomenten worden de met de cliënt opgestelde leerdoelen geëvalueerd. Bekeken wordt of de gestelde doelen zijn behaald en wat ervoor nodig is deze te behalen. Indien mogelijk worden er afspraken gemaakt over de intensiteit van de begeleiding. Bronx beschikt over een 24-uur bereikbaarheidsdienst voor de cliënten van de organisatie. In geval van nood, is de begeleiding binnen 30 minuten ter plaatse.

Name: The Warehouse NJ
Description:
The Warehouse NJ, a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that supports individuals and families as they transition into permanent housing following a time of crisis such as homelessness, abuse, natural disaster, or political asylum. Since 2019, TWNJ has been “helping to create a new beginning” for these individuals and families by transforming their new housing into a home with furniture and basic household items.

TWNJ collects and inventories gently used furniture and household items for the sole purpose of supporting our clients. TWNJ works closely with our clients who select their own furniture from the TWNJ inventory and then TWNJ coordinates the move-in and placement to ensure that the home reflects the client’s needs and preferences. It is one thing to have a roof over your head, it is quite another to have a new mattress to sleep on, a sofa to sit on, and a table to eat at – all of which TWNJ makes possible.
All TWNJ clients are based on referral from a local nonprofit; most have secured an apartment through a government-issued Section 8 housing voucher. Our partner agencies, listed below, have been supporting the clients in some capacity, but have minimal household items to offer to the clients as they transition away from their services. Community partners do not compensate TWNJ for its services, nor do they share in moving expenses.

Name: Sustainable Developments
Description: We are a non-profit organization within the University of California, Riverside and our goal is to design and build a sustainable home. We are a team of 60 divided into three departments: Business, Engineering, and Outreach. Each department is split into smaller subteams, with one Director for each Department and one Lead for each Subteam. The team comprises 60 students from various disciplines at the University of California, Riverside. Each team member works at our Construction Site for at least three hours per week. The Engineering Department manages our product's design and construction phases, including our Architecture, Construction, Laboratories, Modeling, and Systems teams. Our Business Department manages the team's equipment, recruitment, and funds, including Competition, Finance, Organizational, and UI/UX Design. The Outreach department oversees all social media engagement, our market viability, and sales potential and communicates with sponsors through Investments, Marketing, and Sponsors Subteams. Our approach to the competition revolves heavily around the design of our HexHomes. We are conducting extensive research to maximize the environmental factor of our homes while maintaining high-quality living spaces. HexHomes is a new innovative urbanized concept that utilizes hexagonal-shaped factory-built pods to increase the number of orientation possibilities and provide customers an additional layer of customization with more walls and square footage per fabricated unit while eliminating unnecessary space required for hallways. Along with the unique structure of our homes, we are also looking into different materials and suppliers to increase the sustainability and affordability of our homes. Our team conducts extensive research and cost comparisons of all materials, finishings, furniture, and lighting to amplify these spaces' comfort, functionality, and adaptability while prioritizing off-site fabrication and sustainability.

Name: Wa-Karroong
Description:
The project, named Wa-Karroong, meaning “to give home” in the Wadda Wurrung language, represents a blueprint for Indigenous housing that prioritises cultural respect, sustainability, and intergenerational living.

The Wa-Karroong Project is an Indigenous housing initiative developed in partnership with the Wathaurong Co-op and delivered by Harnest Constructions, TBNG, Inotek, Accurate Construction Services, Plus Studio. Supported by a collaborative team of suppliers and services who have 
generously donated time and resources, the project provides a scalable model for culturally respectful, sustainable housing that honours intergenerational living and community connection The home has been designed in consultation with the Wathaurong community, incorporating spaces that support flexible family living, connection to country, and cultural practices. With construction 
now underway, completion is expected within six months.

The Wa-Karroong Project will stand as a symbol of collaboration, respect, and shared responsibility, with the goal of replication across other regions in Australia.

Located in Norlane, Victoria, Wa-Karroong demonstrates how community consultation  and modern methods of construction (MMC) can work hand-in-hand to create quality, culturally appropriate, replicable and environmentally conscious housing solutions.

This project showcases what’s possible when we build on Country, with Country, combining cultural involvement and partnership, advanced prefabrication technology and social impact. 
Our vision is to create a scalable, repeatable model for community-led housing that supports:
•	Respectful, inclusive partnerships with Traditional Owners
•	Faster, smarter delivery through prefabrication
•	Employment and skills pathways in regional communities
•	Sustainable materials and reduced environmental impact

Wa-Karroong is not just a project, it’s a pathway forward for housing reform and community empowerment across Australia.

Name: Aminima Leefruimten
Description:
Aminima is een innovatief bouwsysteem waarmee je eenvoudig ruimtes kunt samenstellen die je op elk moment kunt aanpassen aan je behoeften, je omgeving en de seizoenen. Aminima kan snel en eenvoudig worden gemonteerd, getransformeerd en gedemonteerd. Met Aminima kun je allerlei soorten ruimtes creëren. Of het nu gaat om werkruimtes, ontspanningsruimtes, openbare ruimtes of tentoonstellingsruimtes: er zijn geen speciale bouwvaardigheden vereist.

De Aminima module is ontworpen voor een snelle en eenvoudige installatie. Er is geen specialistische kennis vereist. Je hoeft niets te snijden, te boren, te meten of te berekenen. Het enige wat je nodig hebt is een paar 17-gauge steeksleutels en een accuschroevendraaier. Je hoeft geen netspanning aan te sluiten voor montage/demontage. Afhankelijk van je ervaring kan een Aminima module in één of twee dagen worden gemonteerd.

Zo creëer je met een minimum aan tijd, werk en middelen een leefruimte die maximaal veelzijdig, elegant, demontabel en transformeerbaar is.

Name: Florida Property Receiver Force, LLC
Description:
JUDICIAL RECEIVER

FLORIDA PROPERTY RECEIVER FORCE ("FPRF") is a bonded, licensed and insured Court Appointed Receiver located in Melbourne Beach that offers a No-Cost Receivership Program throughout the State.

As Receiver, FPRF rehabs, manages, maintains, secures and rents the vacant delinquent properties at no cost to the COA/HOA. The COA/HOA has no liability or cost and is held harmless. FPRF pays the COA/HOA 30% of the rent or their monthly assessment, whichever is greater.

FPRF can include any properties that are delinquent and vacant or renter-occupied, including abandoned; Lender or COA/HOA owned or in foreclosure; and Probate.

Prior to removing the property from Receivership, FPRF recovers for the COA/HOA the statutory “Safe Harbor” where applicable or the entire delinquent balance. 

FPRF reduces COA/HOA debt and liability; beautifies the community by eliminating code violations and maintaining and preserving the homes; improves the community’s economy; increases property and adjacent property values; and ensures the safety of residents, communities, and properties by eliminating infestations and reducing neighborhood crime, vandalism, and unlawful occupation.

Name: Battle Creek Housing Commission
Description:
Housing Commission: Battle Creek Housing Commission
Providing Affordable Housing Solutions in Battle Creek, Michigan  
The Battle Creek Housing Commission (BCHC) is dedicated to ensuring safe, decent, and affordable housing for low- and moderate-income families, seniors, and individuals in Calhoun County. As a HUD-recognized housing authority, we manage Federal Public Housing units and administer the Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher Program, serving over 799 households while fostering healthy, vibrant communities.  
Our mission is to support those facing housing challenges—whether due to limited income, age, or disability—by offering a range of options, from townhouse-style apartments to single-family homes. With a commitment to quality and fairness, we empower residents to thrive in stable, welcoming environments.  
Established in 1960 and headquartered at 250 Champion St, Battle Creek, MI, BCHC collaborates with local partners and property owners to expand housing opportunities. Follow us to learn more about our programs, community impact, and dedication to housing equity.

Name: CoopGeeni
Description:
En COOPGEENI, creemos que el acceso a una vivienda digna, saludable y sostenible es un derecho, no un privilegio. Como cooperativa integral de servicios, vivienda y consumo, ofrecemos soluciones innovadoras en construcción, rehabilitación y energía renovable, siempre bajo los principios de la Economía Social y Solidaria (ESS).
 ¿Qué hacemos?
 ● Obra Nueva NZEB (nearly-zero energy building) → Viviendas cooperativas en cesión de uso, diseñadas para la eficiencia y el bienestar.
 ● Rehabilitación energética integral de edificios → Más de 1.000 viviendas renovadas, reduciendo el impacto ambiental y mejorando la calidad de vida.
 ● Comunidades Energéticas Locales (CEL) → Impulsamos el autoconsumo y la soberanía energética con más de 11 proyectos en marcha.
 ¿Por qué elegirnos?
 ● Conseguimos hasta un 73% en subvenciones o financiación para nuestros clientes.
 ● Ofrecemos estudios previos a éxito, asegurando viabilidad antes de ejecutar.
 ● Diseñamos con principios de salud, bienestar y eficiencia energética.
 ● Garantizamos la ejecución con altos estándares de calidad.
 ● Acompañamos en el mantenimiento post-ejecución.
 ● Ahorro fiscal como cooperativa.
 En COOPGEENI, normalizamos la innovación para ofrecerte soluciones regenerativas y de menor impacto ambiental. Trabajamos contigo y para ti, creando comunidades sostenibles y resilientes.
 Conéctate con nosotros y forma parte del cambio.

Name: Texada Island Non-profit Seniors Housing Society (TINSHS)
Description:
Texada is a remote, sparsely populated island community with a rich history of mining and logging industries, blended with coastal BC beauty. The island’s residents are predominantly robust seniors while the housing stock is rural, spread apart and not senior-friendly. Many seniors are faced with the challenge of aging in demanding rural conditions. Currently, there are no alternative housing options for seniors whose homes are not safe, accessible or adaptable
. 
Incorporated in 2019, Texada Island non-Profit Seniors Housing Society (TINSHS) is a registered charity directed by a volunteer working Board of Texada Island residents. Our mission is to create 40 units of affordable age-friendly housing for Texada seniors, enabling them to age close to family, friends and community. 

Our vision for an affordable age-friendly housing development includes a cluster of accessible, adaptable, eco-friendly modules situated on a partially forested parcel close to amenities and services. We envision a development that is harmoniously integrated into the natural forest setting, in alignment with Texada’s rural character, and includes a communal building, gardens, trails and outdoor gathering spaces.

TINSHS is currently seeking support to purchase land for our affordable and environmentally sustainable seniors housing project. We are grateful for any donations made towards this goal and happy to discuss opportunities for partnership, publicity and recognition for our funders and sponsors.

Name: Congregational Land Initiative
Description:
The Northern CA Congregational Land Initiative (CLI) guides faith congregations in manifesting their mission by overviewing and exploring the feasibility to develop affordable housing on their properties.  An experienced faith-rooted team of professionals and leaders from the Bay Area conducts a 4-session series with a cohort, and offers a process for assessing feasibility and defining vision.  Congregations who are exploring or pursuing this mission and who own underutilized property and buildings will learn a development process that supports the spiritual life of the congregation and serves their community.  

Congregations’ underused parking lots, buildings, and land could be transformed for housing and other community needs that would create an income source as well as meet the growing needs of their communities. Congregations will renew their missional calling while serving neighbors who lack affordable housing.  Many congregations have used this process to rediscover their mission to “love thy neighbor” while also spurring their congregation’s rebirth and service to the community.  The first stage of participation in this cohort is that congregations understand the process and development options to achieve their vision.  A second stage is offered at the end to create and test a preliminary plan for their property to serve housing needs while generating self-sustaining income.
 
Excellent work is already being done by professional consultants and institutions to build capacity in congregations to develop affordable housing.  Yet, there are very few faith-rooted developers whom congregations know they can trust to help them discern a path forward that considers their history, their values, and their desire to help their congregation thrive and serve their community.  An intermediary church-advocate team experienced in affordable housing development is essential to advise churches of their rights and possibilities before engaging with a developer.

Name: PUSH Ministry
Description:
PUSH Ministry is a pioneering Community Housing Development Organization (CHDO) with a passion for eradicating housing instability in Metro Atlanta. We focus on preserving affordable housing by rehabilitating existing properties and developing new, net-zero, affordable single and multi-family housing solutions in Metro Atlanta's Qualified Opportunity Zones. Our A.R.C.H. (Acquire, Renovate, Community Housing) Initiative serves as the cornerstone of our approach to creating vibrant and sustainable communities.

Our Mission:
At PUSH Ministry, our mission is to provide access and opportunity to affordable housing, empowering families to become self-sufficient. We are dedicated to fostering communities where individuals, families, and neighborhoods can thrive and flourish.

Collaboration for Impact:
We collaborate with local governments, housing providers, and philanthropic organizations to create and maintain affordable housing units. Our commitment to partnership enables us to ensure that each property we preserve and develop is safe, sustainable, and affordable for residents.

Our Vision:
We envision a world where everyone has access to safe and affordable housing, and we work tirelessly to provide the support and resources necessary to make this dream a reality. At PUSH Ministry, we believe that housing is a fundamental human right, and we strive to create inclusive communities that empower individuals and families to build a brighter future. Join us in our mission to transform lives and build resilient communities.

Name: THIS Foundation
Description:
We provide a safe and stable refuge for women who have survived domestic violence, sexual assault, or sex trafficking—helping those who have been out of their abusive relationships for at least two years to rebuild their lives by achieving financial independence and securing long-term, stable housing.

We partner with domestic violence shelters and survivor support organizations for referrals and seek subsidized funding to ensure housing affordability. Through our Tiny Home Model, we provide survivors with a stable path to secure housing while connecting them with mentorship programs like SOAR to foster personal and professional growth.

Description:
In Nigeria, thousands of people work every day but still sleep on the streets. From artisans and cleaners to security guards and drivers, they keep the city running, yet have nowhere to call home.

Vamilihouse is changing that.

We are a social enterprise providing affordable shelter for low-income and homeless individuals by:
-  Converting shipping containers into safe, co-living spaces and also looking at revamping abandoned government buildings into functional shelters

Our goal is simple: create clean, secure, and low-cost housing that restores dignity. For just ₦1,000 a night, residents get a bed, electricity, shared toilets, and a roof over their heads.

This isn’t charity, it’s a sustainable solution.
We believe shelter is a basic necessity, not a luxury.

Because no one earning an honest living should sleep outside.

We believe that a society of connected people is better than a society of separated individuals and actively seek projects that encourage cultural diversity, inclusion, and equity.

Our work is currently centered on the revitalization of Downtown New Port Richey, Florida, a charming town founded in the early 20th Century. We do this through education, the activation of public spaces, and projects that have the greatest potential to improve the community and create places for people to connect.

With our deep belief in the power of walkable urban places, we have taken a strategic approach to help bring the town back to life. We seek out the projects that seem most challenging but have the greatest potential to improve the town for residents, businesses, and visitors. Then we invest aggressively in inspiring design, high-quality construction, and a thoughtful curation of the businesses and activities that enliven these re-invigorated spaces.

Today, Central Virginia Housing is fully staffed and offers many programs geared towards providing affordable housing services in the counties of Stafford, Spotsylvania, Fredericksburg, Caroline, Fauquier and King George.

We work with families and individuals to try and prevent them from losing their housing when crisis strikes. We equip those who walk through our doors with the information and tools necessary for them to be successful renters or homeowners through counseling & education, community collaboration and case management.

Name: Sumberkima Beach
Description:
Whilst fishing will always reduce the overall underwater wildlife, it isn't necessarily bad for the ocean if, like all things, done in moderation and allows for the stocks to replenish. Industrial fishing trawlers, however, have a devastating impact on the ecosystems from the seabed to the communities that make their living from them; coupled with climate change and pollution, these once harmonious communities are now struggling to survive.

One of those communities is in the Northwest of Bali, in the village of Sumberkima. We have embarked on a regeneration project with local families to improve their micro economy slowly. By developing sustainable tourism experiences, we diversify the sources of income, making the community less reliant on fishing alone. By doing so, we provide visitors with a genuine taste of Balinese life whilst simultaneously ensuring a brighter, more secure and sustainable future for all.

Learn more about our parent company People & Planet (www.linkedin.com/company/people-and-planet-group) and our sister project Sumberkima Beach: (www.linkedin.com/company/sumberkima-beach).

Name: Bioparque Technochtitlan
Description:
At Bioparque Technochtitlan, we are dedicated to preserving nature while empowering communities through innovative, sustainable practices. Our mission is to create a harmonious balance between human development and environmental stewardship, providing unique experiences that reconnect people with nature and inspire positive environmental action.

Located in the heart of nature, we offer a variety of services, products, and workshops that promote ecological responsibility and local craftsmanship. Our park serves as both a sanctuary for biodiversity and a space for educational, recreational, and cultural activities. From reforestation projects to bamboo processing and the production of eco-friendly crafts, we are committed to fostering sustainable living practices.

What We Offer:
Ecotourism: Immerse yourself in nature, bird watching, and nature tours that showcase our diverse ecosystem and conservation efforts.
Sustainable Agriculture & Reforestation: Join our reforestation initiatives or learn about permaculture, organic farming, and sustainable bamboo cultivation.
Artisanal Products: Our bamboo bicycles and other handcrafted products represent our commitment to eco-friendly alternatives and local craftsmanship.
Workshops & Education: We offer a range of workshops, from permaculture to bioconstruction. Our hands-on learning experiences are designed to empower individuals with skills for sustainable living.
Community Development: We work closely with local artisans, farmers, and communities, providing employment opportunities and fostering skills development in rural areas. Our goal is to uplift communities through sustainable employment and education.
Future Expansion: As we grow, we aim to become a center for eco-innovation, developing technologies for bamboo processing, green construction, and renewable energy systems.

Through every product and service, Bioparque Technochtitlan strives to create a better future by integrating sustainability into everyday life.

The UK has a significant shortage of social homes to meet current demand. Over 1.2 million households are on the waiting list for social homes in England, while almost 100,000 households are living in temporary accommodation, including 120,710 children. Councils spent £1.6 billion on temporary accommodation in 2021-22 alone.

Oakfold Developments has been formed to help alleviate some of the pressures felt by councils and households alike, whilst creating financial opportunities for landowners.

Name: Quintazur, Senior Residences
Description:
Quintazur es una marca mexicana de residencias para personas mayores dedicadas a ofrecer un modelo integral de vivienda asistida, a través de un abanico de servicios profesionales e integrales abarcando el bienestar físico, emocional, social y espiritual de cada persona. 

Nuestro compromiso es brindar un hogar seguro, cálido y profesional donde las personas mayores puedan vivir de forma plena, activa y digna, dentro de una comunidad con sentido. En Quintazur, cada residente es visto como una persona completa, con historia, valores y un presente que merece atención, respeto y conexión.

Todos nuestros servicios de cuidado son personalizados y se adaptan a las necesidades específicas de cada residente. Ofrecemos programas especializados de atención médica continua, incluyendo servicios de Memory Care diseñados para personas con Alzheimer u otras condiciones cognitivas. Nuestro equipo multidisciplinario, altamente capacitado y en constante actualización, también se enfoca en la prevención y manejo de enfermedades crónicas comunes en México, como la diabetes, la hipertensión y la depresión, garantizando un acompañamiento integral y de calidad.

Cada espacio, cada programa y cada interacción en Quintazur está pensado con intención. Aquí, la calidad de vida y el sentirte acompañado es una experiencia diaria. 

Nuestro compromiso va más allá del cuidado: buscamos crear comunidad, construir vínculos y honrar la historia de vida de cada persona. En Quintazur, envejecer es seguir siendo.

Op een kavel bij het August Allebéplein realiseren we 132 betaalbare huurwoningen in collectief beheer. Huurders bepalen samen hoe ze willen wonen: van de indeling van het gebouw en het gebruik van gemeenschappelijke ruimtes tot het dagelijks beheer.

De Bundel is meer dan alleen woningen. De begane grond en de tuin worden gedeelde ruimtes, toegankelijk voor de hele buurt. We willen bijdragen aan een inclusieve, toegankelijke woonomgeving — ook buiten onze eigen voordeuren.

Tot de bouw van start gaat, gebruiken we de kavel als ontmoetingsplek. In de tijdelijke kas, de Glazen Bundel, organiseren we workshops, buurtevenementen, kunstbijeenkomsten en expertsessies. Hier bouwen we alvast aan de gemeenschap die straks in De Bundel zal wonen.

Golvet bestämmer lutningen på tröskeln som fjädras ned vid belastning. Detta innebär att när du passerar med en rullator, rullstol, palldragare etc så fjädrar tröskeln ner och blir helt plan. Beroende på höjd så blir nivån med andra ord alltid noll. Det finns ingen kant som sticker upp. 

Målet är även att hålla produkten lokal i den mån det går, och på det sättet bidra till samhället genom arbetstillfällen.

A pocket neighborhood is a new approach to neighborhood community consisting of a group of clustered homes gathered around a shared green space. The founder of this concept, Architect Ross Chapin, has this to say about pocket neighborhoods. "These are settings where nearby neighbors can easily know one another, where empty nesters and single householders with far-flung families can find friendship or a helping hand nearby, and where children can have shirttail aunties and uncles just beyond their front gate."

Name: Portugal Development Co.
Description:
Restoring the livability of Portugal’s historic city centers

Portugal Development Co. was founded with a clear goal: to breathe new life into Portugal’s architectural heritage while expanding access to homes in the country’s most culturally rich and walkable neighborhoods. At a time when housing demand is becoming a major issue in Portugal, yet thousands of historic buildings remain vacant, we see an opportunity to do things differently and more responsibly.

We focus on unlivable, abandoned properties in historic urban cores, transforming them with care and respect for their architectural identity. Purchasing and restoring these once-proud buildings is often hindered by bureaucratic and legal challenges. Our approach is patient, committed, and rooted in long-term thinking. The result is well-designed homes that balance old-world character with modern comfort.

By doing this work, we make it possible for more people to live in vibrant city centers filled with culture, beauty, and connection. For residents, it means access to authentic, livable neighborhoods. For communities, it means a quiet but powerful form of revitalization — driven by restoration, not replacement.

This is not just about preserving the past. It is about making Portugal’s historic neighborhoods part of a thriving, livable future.

Name: GCS Support Services
Description:
GCS is an organisation providing safe, supported housing for individuals experiencing or at risk of homelessness, with a specialist focus on refugees.

We recognise that many of the people we support have experienced significant trauma, displacement, and loss. Our services are built around a trauma-informed approach, ensuring that care is delivered with empathy, safety, and an understanding of the impact of past experiences. We aim to create a stable and respectful environment where individuals feel secure, valued, and empowered to begin their recovery and integration journey.

Our work goes beyond simply providing accommodation. We offer holistic, person-centred support tailored to each individual’s needs—whether that’s help accessing healthcare, learning life skills, finding education or employment opportunities, or connecting with local community resources. We work closely with local authorities, charities, and other partners to ensure each resident has a strong support network around them.

At GCS, we believe that with the right environment and the right support, people can overcome adversity and build fulfilling, independent lives. Our mission is to walk alongside them on that journey—offering not just housing, but hope, dignity, and a fresh start.

Name: Common Knowledge Group
Description:
Common Knowledge is a nonpartisan organization committed to expanding community engagement and collective imagination for more inclusive and thriving local communnities. We believe that the complex issues facing our governments, economy and environment need the talents and energy of diverse community members, especially those who are assumed to be marginalized. Common Knowledge co-creates breakthrough community engagement programs that support sustainable social change.

The name Common Knowledge embodies two underlying principles:  1) there are things that should be common knowledge but aren’t – such as how does my local government work, how do we provide health care, how can I get involved, etc. and 2) there is an essential knowledge that only comes from dialogue between diverse participants that generates mutual understanding and common ground for action.

Through a unique fusion of affordability and cutting-edge technology, we've crafted a living space that doesn't just meet basic needs but elevates the standard of living. But our vision doesn't stop there. We're redefining luxury living, setting new standards for opulence and refinement. For more details on our groundbreaking approach to both affordable and luxury homes, visit our website and witness the future of living unfold.

Name: Ulster County Habitat for Humanity

Ulster County Habitat for Humanity, founded in 1996, is a nonprofit organization that helps families build and improve homes. Studies demonstrate that affordable housing plays a critical role in strong and stable communities. 

Every person deserves a decent, safe place to live. Together, with our supportive community, Ulster Habitat helps families build homes, ensuring a brighter future that has been demonstrated to be multi-generational.

2021 marks the completion of 20 local homes for 20 local families. Habitat partner families are good neighbors and exhibit pride in ownership. Each new Habitat house contributes to the tax base of Ulster County, and to the long-term stability of the family and the community. We anticipate building 3 homes a year going forward.

Our ReStore opened in 2013, and generates hundreds of thousands of dollars annually in support of our housing mission, as well as supporting reuse, recycling, and affordable re-homed goods for our neighbors.

Wat VPRC uniek maakt, is dat wij niet geloven in één oplossing. Te snel wordt gedacht in technische oplossingen, zonder goede verkenning van de opgave. Wij helpen opdrachtgevers besluiten, door met beslisinformatie de effecten en consequenties van keuzes inzichtelijk te maken.

VPRC biedt oplossingen voor de meest uiteenlopende opgaven in relatie tot gebiedsontwikkeling, circulariteit, energiemanagement, huisvestingsvraagstukken en vraagstukken gericht op grootschalige vastgoedportefeuilles. Name: Provo City Housing Authority
Description:
The Provo City Housing Authority was created in 1971 by a commission of the City of Provo.  Our primary functions include the administration of the Public Housing and the Housing Choice Voucher program in the City of Provo.  These two programs are federally funded through the Department of Housing and Urban Development.  We have regularly received a designation of “High Performer” by the Department of Housing and Urban Development in both Public Housing (Public Housing Assessment System) and the Housing Choice Voucher program Section 8 Management Assessment Program.

We believe that the opportunity to have safe, secure, and affordable housing is an essential foundation for successful pursuit of life goals.  Without the foundation and security of a home, the pursuit of education, career advancement, and other essential tools on the path to self-sufficiency are difficult, if not impossible, to find.

Good customer service is at our core of values; we feel strongly that our approach to those we interact with contributes to our success in being more than just a housing provider. We care about those with whom we interact, including neighboring property owners, landlords, City officials, our community partners, and the Provo residents we serve.

Name: The Housing Mediators
Description:

Our bespoke consultancy acts as a critical friend. We provide quality assurance and offer tailored solutions to meet the needs of landlords seeking expert advice or facing resource and time constraints. Our mediation is focused on social housing disputes, offering landlords an alternative approach to resolving conflicts. Whether mediating disputes between residents, directly with a resident, or addressing workplace conflicts, our team of experienced mediators is ready to assist.

Name: Plant Housing and Development
Description:
We are committed to delivering high-quality homes to Indigenous communities across Canada.
PlantHD is an Indigenous-led Housing and Development company that uses a "For Indigenous, By Indigenous" approach. Our Product line is called ACE Homes. Our community is growing and we have a lot of people and organizations backing Plant Housing and Development - which is exciting! It will take a community to bring massive change. 

ACE stands for A - Affordability, C - Culturally-Rooted, and E - Energy Efficient homes. ACE homes are ultra-affordable; we design the homes to include any nation's identity (like colours, logos of the nation, Cree aspects for example) to represent their culture; and they are high-quality eco-friendly materials that are energy efficient and will make bills like electricity non-existent. Over its lifespan, ACE Homes saves a LOT of money as compared to traditional homes. 
PlantHD takes a different approach than traditional developers. Aside from NOT charging an arm and a leg, we co-create with Indigenous leadership. 
We prioritize the needs and preferences of Indigenous nations, we don't provide the outright solution.  
They are also not tiny homes or modular. They need to be assembled on-site, and the current products we have span from 750 - 1500+ square feet. We're constantly building larger and larger homes and different types of products that will benefit communities. 

We offer help to obtain funding from a variety of sources (federal, provincial, municipal, and other sources) for the nation to get the homes that the Nation needs.

PlantHD also has a range of partnerships for Building Services (site work, plumbing, sewage, electrical, etc.)

Name: High Peak Housing
Description:
PREDICTABLE AND STABLE RETURNS

Investing in social housing and supported living offers unparalleled predictability. These contracts provide property owners with a fixed monthly rental income over a set tenancy period. What's more, the social housing or supported living provider takes on the responsibility for all bills, maintenance, and property management at no additional cost to the property owner. This means you receive a steady rental income regardless of void periods or other disruptions.

HASSLE-FREE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT

One of the key benefits of these contracts is the elimination of common landlord headaches. You won't have to worry about non-payment of rent, lengthy court battles to reclaim possession, or the recurring costs of letting agents and contract fees. Everything is handled for you, allowing you to enjoy the benefits of your investment with peace of mind.

MAKING A DIFFERENCE

Beyond the financial advantages, investing in social housing and supported living allows you to contribute to a greater purpose. By providing homes for those in need, you play a crucial role in supporting vulnerable individuals and strengthening communities. It's an investment that not only yields financial returns but also creates a positive social impact.

Name: Solidarity Eco-Haven Housing Foundation
Description:
Solidarity Eco-Haven Housing Foundation is a non-profit organization dedicated to providing sustainable, affordable rental housing solutions for low-income and very low-income seniors and veterans. The foundation addresses the critical housing crisis faced by some of the most vulnerable members of the community, ensuring that everyone—regardless of financial circumstances—has access to safe, dignified, and eco-friendly living spaces.

Mission and Vision
The foundation’s mission is to create compact, environmentally responsible rental units, typically ranging from 400 to 600 square feet, that incorporate advanced green technologies such as solar power, on-demand hot water systems, and rainwater collection. These features significantly reduce both the environmental footprint and ongoing utility costs for residents, fostering long-term financial stability and improved quality of life.

Solidarity Eco-Haven’s vision extends beyond providing shelter; it aims to build communities that foster independence, social connection, and a sense of belonging, particularly for seniors and veterans who may otherwise face isolation. The organization’s approach combines innovative small-home design, accessible features for aging in place, and community support systems to promote dignity and security for all residents.

Key Features and Benefits

Eco-friendly construction with solar energy, efficient water systems, and sustainable materials

Accessible design tailored to the needs of seniors and veterans

Reduced carbon footprint and significant utility cost savings

Community-oriented environments to combat social isolation

Partnerships with local businesses and organizations to enhance resident support

Impact and Community Engagement
Through fundraising initiatives and community partnerships, Solidarity Eco-Haven Housing Foundation empowers residents, advocates for housing equity, and advances environmental stewardship. The foundation invites support from individuals, businesses.

Description:
My Housing Options CIC is an accommodation and support provider offering housing solutions for individuals facing homelessness and housing insecurity. We deliver safe, high-quality homes alongside a strong framework of personalised, high-level support that addresses both immediate need and long-term stability.

We work closely with Local Authorities and Registered Providers to ensure that each placement is more than just a roof, it is a pathway from crisis to independence. Our platform enables individuals to move from homelessness into a home, where they can stabilise, rebuild, and prepare for a sustainable future.

Our mission is to create sustainable, high-quality housing solutions for individuals experiencing homelessness by leveraging strategic real estate investments. We are committed to delivering stable financial returns for our investors while generating meaningful social impact. 

Through partnerships with local governments, nonprofits, and service providers, we develop and manage properties that offer safe, dignified, and supportive living environments, empowering residents to rebuild their lives

The Services We Provide;

Houses of Multiple Occupation: 
The provision for houses of multiple occupation (HMO’s) accommodation for homeless individuals.

Large Multi-Unit Buildings: 
The provision of large residential multi-unit accommodation buildings for emergency / temporary housing for greater London local authorities.

Supported Accommodation: 
Housing provision to help homeless individuals develop independent daily living skills, and learn how to maintain their own tenancy, with the scope of moving into their own long-term home. We have the expertise in delivering housing and support solutions for vulnerable groups of society. This includes people that have experienced homelessness due to current challenges in the housing market or those who have been identified as having housing and additional support needs.

Assessment of Needs: 
We conduct thorough assessments to understand the specific needs of each resident, ensuring tailored support that addresses their unique challenges. Once we have received a referral, we assess the individuals support need via a face-to-face interview.

Housing Support: 
We assist our residents in applying for housing benefits and liaise with local councils to secure necessary support and referrals.

Skill Development: 
Our programs focus on equipping residents with essential daily living skills, knowledge on how to maintain a tenancy and preparation for transition to long-term independent housing.

Name: Edinburgh Building Retrofit and Improvement Collective
Description:
Existing buildings can be made more energy efficient, comfortable and healthy to live and work in through planned maintenance, retrofit and introducing local heat and energy systems. We bring together, encourage, educate and empower the developing local ecosystem of community groups striving to do these things in homes and community buildings.

We act as a knowledgeable, trustworthy, independent and non-commercial intermediary between communities and tradespeople/professionals who can help with this work. Decarbonisation of buildings is a huge opportunity (and challenge) for us all. We aim to encourage and facilitate skills development and community engagement for professionals moving into this work.

A community can be a tenement, street or neighbourhood, or one of common interest (such as properties with needs of a similar type). Working at community-scale offers many advantages, including: getting the best technical results by improving a whole building, developing stronger and more resilient communities by working together and economies of scale.

Дейността на компанията датира от 2004 г., като за повече от 20 години история сме завършили успешно над 35 сгради и комплекса с обща РЗП от над 140 000 кв.м. в столицата София и големите ски и морски курорти на България. Към днешна дата компанията развива нови сгради и комплекси в луксозния сегмент на жилищното строителство, като проектите ни се отличават с елегантна съвременна архитектура, висока енергийна ефективност, използване на най-висок клас строителни материали и оборудване, прилагане на най-модерни строителни технологии, развитие на концепции за комфортна, благоприята и хармонична жилищна среда.

ТАСКОВ & СТОЯНОВ разчита на ефективен бизнес модел, който се е доказал в годините. Следвайки идеята за бутиков тип строителство, партньорите залагат на няколко важни принципа в своите най-нови проекти:

• ХАРМОНИЧНА, КОМФОРТНА И ЗДРАВОСЛОВНА ЖИЛИЩНА СРЕДА
• КОНЦЕПЦИЯ ЗА ВИСОКА ЕНЕРГИЙНА ЕФЕКТИВНОСТ
• ИЗЯЩНА АРХИТЕКТУРА И ЛУКСОЗНИ ОБЩИ ЧАСТИ
• АВАНГАРДНИ СТРОИТЕЛНИ ТЕХНОЛОГИИ
• ВИСОК КЛАС МАТЕРИАЛИ ОТ СВЕТОВНИТЕ ЛИДЕРИ
• КАЧЕСТВЕНИ СТРОИТЕЛНО-МОНТАЖНИ РАБОТИ
• РЕШЕНИЯ ЗА ЛЕСНА ЕКСПЛОАТАЦИЯ И НЕСКЪПА ПОДДРЪЖКА
• ДЪЛГОВЕЧНОСТ НА СГРАДАТА

За постигане на така заложената концепция ТАСКОВ & СТОЯНОВ партнира дълготрайно с редица доказани специалисти, водещи архитекти, уважавани проектанти и дизайнери в областа на строителството, ландшафта, интериора, енергийната ефективност, системите за зелени и пасивни сгради. През последните години компанията прилага все повече научен подход в строителството, имплементирайки различни иновативни методи, технологии и материали, разработени както от български, така и от чуждестранни специалисти и развойни компании.

В резултат на нашите усилия, така създадената стойност на жилищния продукт надвишава значително неговата цена! Това е най-добрата гаранция за постигане на удовлетворение сред клиентите на компанията.

To close the housing gap by creating beautiful, affordable homes that uplift communities, protect the environment, and transform lives.

 Our Impact

Delivering large-scale affordable housing projects that transform lives and communities, leveraging automation at scale. 

Proven success in projects across the Caribbean — from eco-lodges in Guyana to community housing in Barbados.

Addressing critical challenges of urbanization, inequality, and climate resilience in Small Island Developing States (SIDS).

 Our Difference

Vertically Integrated Supply Chain: From sustainable timber sourcing and processing to modular assembly and installation, we capture more value and deliver homes more efficiently.

Sustainability at the Core: Every home is designed with environmental responsibility and durability in mind.

Scalable Solutions: Adaptable for housing, tourism, schools, and community infrastructure.

 Our Vision

Duravilla is more than a home builder — we are shaping the future of housing in emerging markets. With a rapidly growing pipeline, cutting-edge automation, and strong partnerships, we are poised to deliver thousands of homes, generate strong returns, and create lasting social impact.

Name: Private Congregate Care Alliance
Description:
Established in 2018, the PCC Alliance is a national alliance of organizations working towards the goal of systemic recognition of the unmet needs of people with disability who live in PCC settings throughout Australia. The focus is on elevating the voice of, and challenges experienced by, people with disability who live in congregate care settings (high density, privately run, institutions), with an emphasis on the findings of the Disability Royal Commission (Oct 2023), and in tune with UNCRPD. 

The PCC Alliance is comprised of a diverse membership including legal, advocacy, disability representative, research, mental health, allied health, housing and welfare organizations. We welcome new members, from across Australia. 

Private Congregate Care (PCC) facilities are for-profit accommodation and support facilities that house large numbers of people with psychosocial disability and other disabilities. They exist Australia-wide, and are titled supported residential services (SRS) in Victoria, Psychiatric hostels in WA, Licenced Boarding Houses in NSW, Supported Residential Facilities in SA and Boarding Houses and Hostels in Queensland. 

Collectively, it is estimated that they accommodate thousands of Australians with disability and those who cannot live independently, without support. 
For example, evidence provided at Disability Royal Commission Hearing 26 (September 2022) confirmed that in Victoria, SRS accommodate up to 4000 people, 79% of whom live with disability. 30% or approximately 1000 are NDIS participants.  There are currently 117 SRS in Victoria. SRS accommodate up to 80 people at a single residence.  And that's just Victoria alone. 

Australia-wide, many PCC residents do not have contact with family members, independent advocacy or the support of a legal guardian. Substantial evidence indicates that PCC residents are at heightened risk of exploitation, violence, abuse and neglect (DRC Final Report, 2023).

Name: Amigour Management of Assets | Jewish Agency Housing Projects
Description:
Founded in 1972 as a non-profit company, Amigour is a subsidiary of the Jewish Agency and is Israel’s leading sheltered housing company and second largest public housing group.

There are 1,080,000 Israelis over the age of 65, 200,000 of whom live close to the poverty line and cannot afford to purchase or even rent sustainable lodgings. Amigour operates fifty-seven housing projects nationwide to provide basic living needs for over 7,500 disadvantaged elderly. A large majority of these are new immigrants from the Former Soviet Union and Holocaust survivors. Without support, these men and women would face devastation and ruin.

In addition to putting a roof over their heads, Amigour housing projects provide residents with a broad range of social and cultural services in its on-site facilities. This rich and varied programming, which runs the gamut from art workshops to exercise classes and from concerts to volunteering, greatly enhances the residents’ quality of life. Many of these activities are carried out in partnership with community centers, schools, the Israel Defense Forces and local volunteers.

Amigour receives funding for existing projects from the Israeli Government, the Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation, the Claims Conference, Keren Hayesod, Federations, Foundations and personal donors.

While Amigour currently provides 6000 residential units ( 1300 residential units were built thanks to the Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundaiton) for the elderly, the need is constantly growing with some 27,000 seniors who still lacking proper housing and are on a waiting list.

To counter the critical shortage of nursing facilities in Israel, Amigour has built nine new nursing homes in Kiryat Yam, providing a combined 320 nursing beds for Amigour residents. These facilities provide 24-hour care and intensive treatment programs.

Name: Housing Authority of the City of Elizabeth
Description:

We are an urban housing authority that has successfully demonstrated its capacity to deliver a broad range of social, economic, educational, and redevelopment services. Our ability to accomplish this is only further enhanced by partnerships we have established within the city, as well as with county and state agencies.

The Housing Authority of the City of Elizabeth is responsible for 1325 units of public housing. Of these, 1018 are owned and managed by the authority and 307 are mixed-finance units that are subsidized by HACE but are managed by private entities. In addition to its public housing, the agency administers 1432 Section 8 Rental Assistance Vouchers. The Voucher program consists of grant monies received from the federal government for Shelter Plus Care Awards and the traditional tenant-based Section 8 assistance program.
