Name: Haven Home Foundation
Description:
Haven Home exists to purchase, develop, and provide housing that advances the growth and goals of the communities we serve.
We accomplish our mission and vision by purchasing and renovating single-family homes, developing LIHTC projects, investing in communities that need it most, offering life coaching and financial literacy classes, helping individuals move from renting to homeownership, and truly walking alongside each person we serve.
To desegregate Affordable Housing, Haven Home will purchase and provide housing that will improve neighborhoods and transform lives through economic empowerment by creating a market-based solution.
Desegregate: We believe in the power of scattered housing, the majority of affordable housing is condensed into apartments or new construction neighborhoods consisting of only affordable housing, thereby excluding these neighbors. By creating inclusive housing that gets individuals back into communities we create a balanced mix of socioeconomic status, allowing communities to grow and help one another.
Improve Neighborhoods: There are an estimated 17 million vacant homes in America, this does not account for distressed homes or homes in disrepair. We plan to invest in these communities and bring their housing stock up to a quality standard while preserving affordability. 
Economic Empowerment: With fast-growing rental rates and plummeting access to sustainable housing costs, we know that providing manageable housing costs is only one part of the equation. Providing career, financial, and life counseling is a vital and important part of our mission to change lives in a sustainable way.
Market-based Solution: We understand that in order to create the most impact we needed to create a sustainable model that could gain access to the capital required to scale our impact. Leveraging our background in Real Estate Operations, we are creating a solution that relies not only on charitable donations but also can access capital markets.

Name: County Corp
Description:
Here at County Corp, we work hard to improve our community by striving to provide affordable housing to those who need it the most. That means we build new homes, update existing homes, work with developers to build affordable housing, and much more.

County Corp is the private non-profit development corporation for Montgomery County. The mission of County Corp is to improve our greater Dayton-Montgomery County community by offering affordable housing and economic programs for residents and small businesses. Our Vision is to be the premier provider of programs that create safe, stable neighborhoods and successful small businesses.

County Corp grew out of the need for Montgomery County to actively participate in affordable housing and economic development. In 1978, the Board of County Commissioners and their staff pursued the concept of a non-profit that could do development work prohibited to an Ohio county under the Ohio Revised Code. Incorporation approval from the Ohio Attorney General on December 17, 1979, made County Corp the first housing and economic development non-profit in any Ohio county. We opened for business on April 1, 1980.

Name: Ramps
Description:
Ramps Inc. is a Japan-based startup building infrastructure for distributed living, starting from Higashikawa, Hokkaido.

As cities become increasingly dense and regions face population decline, existing mobility and tourism models tend to prioritize short-term, transactional movement. Many communities lack the foundation to welcome people as recurring participants in local life.

Ramps designs systems that make moving, staying, and returning easier over time, enabling people to build lasting relationships with places beyond one-time visits.

We work with municipalities, regional stakeholders, and hospitality operators — from small inns to mid-sized accommodations — to support long-term regional coordination.

Platforms

• ensembles
A platform where people connect through clubs, events, and hands-on experiences of local life.
As part of ensembles, we operate an accommodation model for repeat visitors, supported by a patented on-site storage system that allows guests to leave belongings behind — creating continuity similar to a second home.

• tomarin
A hospitality and regional platform that integrates reservation and guest information, supporting accommodation operations and shared regional understanding.

Vision

Starting from Higashikawa, Ramps aims to ease urban congestion while expanding access to richer ways of living — scaling a model where people return, connect, and contribute across regions in Japan.

We are building this future with a small, committed team — and are looking for people and partners who want to shape how we live, move, and connect beyond a single place.

Description:
Gap Supported Housing provides safe and supportive accommodation for up to 13 adults experiencing homelessness. Those we help must be able to actively participate in their own recovery, finding solutions to addressing the root causes of their homelessness. 

Through life skills training and confidence-building activities, we foster self-esteem and independence. We also offer support with volunteer placements, job searching, and housing options, ensuring residents have the tools they need to successfully move on to a home of their own.

Most residents stay with us for about six months, but everyone's journey is different. Some move on sooner, while others may need up to two years of support before they’re ready to successfully live independently in their own home.

Name: Stichting Hoven voor IJsselmonde
Description:
Liever samen dan solo - meergeneratie gemeenschappelijk wonen in IJsselmonde. Het is tijd voor woonvormen die passen bij deze tijd én de toekomst. 

Wij zijn Stichting Hoven voor IJsselmonde: een bewonersinitiatief dat bouwt aan woongemeenschappen waar jong en oud samen leven, elkaar ondersteunen en ruimte vinden voor ontmoeting én privacy.

In de regio IJsselmonde (Barendrecht, Rhoon, Ridderkerk, Rotterdam) dromen we van hofjes en erven:  
* Zelfstandig wonen in een sterke gemeenschap
* Variatie in bewoners en woningtypes: starters, gezinnen, ouderen; koop én huur
* Gebruik maken van elkaars kennis, talenten en hulp
* Samen bouwen aan duurzame, zorgzame buurten
* Goede balans tussen sociale interactie en privacy
 
Wij denken vooruit.
De urgentie om anders te wonen wordt breed gevoeld, maar de uitvoering blijft achter. Doorstroming van ouderen naar geschikte woningen maakt ruimte voor anderen op de woningmarkt – en meergeneratie wonen kan daarin een sleutelrol spelen.

Stichting Hoven voor IJsselmonde is opgericht in 2021 door vier vrijwilligers. Inmiddels hebben we een netwerk van 300 geïnteresseerde huishoudens in Barendrecht en omgeving.

Wij zijn potentiële bewoners én partners voor gemeenten en projectontwikkelaars. Wij:
* denken mee over functionele wensen en ontwerpprincipes,
* werven en informeren geïnteresseerden,
* helpen bij het matchen van bewoners en het opzetten van een bewonersvereniging.
 
We bouwen op bestaande kennis en ervaringen uit Nederland en internationaal, en brengen enthousiasme en praktische kennis mee om ideeën om te zetten in werkelijkheid.

Enthousiast geworden? Doe mee! Neem contact op: bestuur@stichtinghovenvoorijsselmonde.nl 

Altijd op de hoogte? Schrijf je in voor onze nieuwsbrief en blijf op de hoogte van nieuwe projecten, bijeenkomsten en inspirerende verhalen. https://www.stichtinghovenvoorijsselmonde.nl/nieuwsbrief.

www.stichtinghovenvoorijsselmonde.nl

Name: Open Zoning
Description:
A collaboration between the Harvard GSD Urban Stack Lab x Cornell Tech Jacobs Urban Tech Hub, Open Zoning is empowering the nation with actionable zoning knowledge, bypassing the current exclusionary system and setting the foundation for a new way forward in the bottom-up fight against the housing crisis.

Zoning is the gatekeeper of all development -- and it's contributing to the housing crisis. Its complexity and inaccessibility are insurmountable barriers for thousands of small developers who build an essential tier of equitable and severely under-provided housing, ‘missing middle’ housing. But – it doesn’t have to stay this way.

Open Zoning is building the infrastructure to transform zoning codes into legible, actionable data for everyone. The core of this effort is the Open Zoning Feed Specification (OZFS), a revolutionary national data standard for machine-readable zoning information that will leverage the power of open-source communities to fuel a national, open-sourced database of zoning information for missing middle housing, the Open Zoning Database. These novel technologies are driving the development of the Open Zoning Platform, a first-of-its-kind, open platform for visually displayed zoning information, effectively empowering the nation with actionable zoning knowledge.

Name: Voluntaria Community
Description:
Voluntaria began with one individual’s two-year journey in an intentional community, where discovering voluntaryist ideals shed light on how communal living could thrive by prioritizing personal freedom and well-being. Instead of forcing participation, we believe that contributions to the community should flow naturally from each person’s surplus. By honoring the non-aggression principle and natural law, we aim to build a cohesive, value-driven model for communal living—one that addresses the pitfalls causing over 90% of intentional communities to fail.

Today, our mission is to establish a community development nonprofit that creates safe, sustainable spaces for individuals to grow—literally and figuratively. We cultivate organic food through robust food forests, host educational events, and foster a supportive environment where genuine connections can flourish. By embracing some level of self-sufficiency—such as relocating away from cities and focusing on regenerative agriculture—Voluntaria members can navigate uncertain times while building resilience.

We also acknowledge the broader social and political climate, where intrusive government actions often disrupt personal freedoms. Inspired by groups like the Amish, who have successfully chosen a more self-reliant path, Voluntaria encourages opting out of exploitative systems whenever possible. As famously noted in the film “War Games,” sometimes the best way to win is not to play.

Imagine a simpler way of life centered on healthy relationships, abundant gardens, and the principle of voluntary participation. We believe real change emerges when a group of like-minded people, sharing common values and morality, come together to create a living example of peace, prosperity, and cooperation—even if the world around us falters.

Join us in making this vision a reality. Imagine a society built on consent, voluntaryism, and natural law—imagine Voluntaria.

Name: Cherokee Indian Housing Division
Description:
For years, the Eastern Band of the Cherokee Indians (EBCI) operated two separate housing departments, the first being the Qualla Housing Authority (QHA), established in 1962. QHA was created to serve as a Tribally Designated Housing Entity (TDHE) to administer federal funds for low-income housing activities. In 2005, the second housing department, Housing and Community Development (HCD) was established. Unlike QHA, HCD operated exclusively on tribal funds, as opposed to federal funds.
Also, unlike QHA, which as a TDHE, day-to-day operations were overseen by an Executive Director who reported to a Board of Commissioners (BOC), HCD was overseen by the Secretary of Housing who reported directly to the Principal Chief.
After a slew of attempts to bring the two housing departments together under one roof, it finally happened on August 20, 2020 (Cherokee Code Ord. No. 158), effectively dissolving the TDHE, Qualla Housing Authority became Qualla Housing Services (QHS), and Housing & Community Development became Tribal Housing Services (THS), both departments of the new Cherokee Indian Housing Division.

The dissolving of the TDHE meant the dissolving of the BOC, which is now known as the Housing Committee and serves as an advisory council with no authority over day-to-day operations. Chapter 44 – HOUSING | Code of Ordinances | Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians, Tribes and Tribal Nations | Municode Library

Name: Lake Huron Regional Development Corporation
Description:
Welcome to the Lake Huron Regional Development Corporation (LH-RDC). We believe that for Northeast Michigan to thrive, every resident must have access to a safe, quality, and affordable home. That's not just a vision; it's the foundation for strong families, a stable workforce, and vibrant rural communities.

Our region is facing a serious housing shortage that threatens our economic growth and community vitality. The LH-RDC was created to tackle this challenge head-on. We are not just building houses; we are building a framework for long-term success.




How We Make an Impact

Our role is to be a regional engine for housing development, filling a critical gap where projects might not otherwise be financially viable without outside support. We do this by:


Developing and Preserving Homes: We lead the development of new single-family homes and support the rehabilitation of existing ones , ensuring families, workers, and seniors can afford to remain in the region.





Empowering Local Communities: We provide the technical support and expertise that our local governments and partners need to turn housing plans into reality. Our role is not to replace local leadership but to empower communities.




Building Strong Partnerships: Success depends on collaboration. We bring together municipalities, employers, developers, and state and federal agencies to align efforts and pool resources.



Our Commitment

This effort belongs to all of us. Our work is grounded in transparency, fiscal discipline, and a deep commitment to ensuring that community voices shape every project we undertake.



Thank you for your interest in our work. Together, we will chart a course toward meaningful progress for our region. We invite you to connect with us and be part of the solution.

Name: KINVII-Regenerative Modern Living
Description:
KINVII is a regenerative living project designed to redefine what community can be. We are building intentional spaces where sustainability meets innovation, and where people, nature, and technology coexist in balance.

Our first KINVII campus in Washington will feature 100 modular homes, a 100 pad RV park, and a 400 unit storage facility anchored by a solar powered microgrid and a shared coworking and community hub. Every part of the design reflects a simple belief: that living well should also mean living responsibly.

KINVII blends the warmth of neighborhood life with the intelligence of modern design. Each resident contributes to the ecosystem, earning participation credits through community work, sustainability projects, and creative collaboration. This model reduces costs, increases connection, and transforms residents into co stewards of their environment.

Our approach is infrastructure first: build the foundation, generate energy, and grow the village from the ground up. The project integrates permaculture design, renewable energy systems, and smart modular housing to create scalable, self sufficient communities that can be replicated across the country.

KINVII is not just a place to live. It is a movement toward regeneration, equity, and belonging. We are building for families, digital nomads, seniors, and single parents who want something more than just a roof over their heads. We are creating a home for those who believe the future can be designed with intention, beauty, and care.

Join us as we build the first regenerative living campus in Washington and set a new standard for sustainable, community driven living.

 KINVII: Where Nature and Design Thrive Together.

We’re tackling the state’s housing crisis with solutions that offer both new paths to home ownership and innovative ways to build equity in existing properties. Through ADU development, home subdivisions, and innovative co-ownership models, we're creating new entry points to homeownership while cultivating vibrant, connected neighborhoods.

Our approach turns conventional housing wisdom on its head. Instead of high fences and single-family homes, we ask: What if we take down the fences? What if we turn toward our neighbors rather than away from them? We believe thoughtfully designed shared spaces can transform how people live together, making multifamily living not just affordable, but desirable.

Blending carefully considered private spaces with communal amenities, our projects foster well-being, facilitate resource sharing, and encourage meaningful human interaction. By reimagining housing as semi-collective rather than stand-alone, we're helping Californians build equity and community.

We've identified the three biggest obstacles for homeowners: the complexity of permitting and logistics, securing financing, and the overall lack of a seamless end-to-end service. Our platform acts as a concierge partner, simplifying the complex process from initial design to final construction. We offer modern designs, streamline the permitting process through partnerships with local municipalities, and guide our clients toward favorable financing options.

At Homsolvr, we believe we are building more than homes; we are building solutions for a new era of Canadian housing. Our track record, controlled production, and strategic partnerships with financial and regulatory bodies give our clients the confidence to navigate the complex housing market with ease.

Program Management & Compliance: We specialize in optimizing the administration of Housing Choice Voucher (HCV) programs, ensuring strict adherence to HUD regulations and maximizing program efficiency.

Policy Development & Research: Our team conducts in-depth policy analysis and research, developing evidence-based recommendations that address critical housing challenges and inform strategic decision-making.

Technical Assistance & Training: We deliver tailored technical assistance and engaging training programs on a range of HUD initiatives, including Moving to Work, Choice Neighborhoods, and ConnectHome, both on-site and remotely.

Capacity Building: We empower organizations to enhance their operational capacity through strategic planning, process improvement, and effective stakeholder engagement.

Our Expertise:
With over 16 years of experience in the affordable housing sector, Housing Choice Solutions LLC brings a deep understanding of Public and Indian Housing (PIH) programs and a proven track record of delivering impactful results. We are committed to fostering collaboration, driving innovation, and creating sustainable solutions that improve the lives of individuals and families in need.

To provide expert guidance and support that empowers organizations to create and maintain thriving and sustainable communities.

Oregon needs project managers who can unite partners to build housing more rapidly. Technical assistance providers who can bring together land owners, market rate builders, affordable housing providers, advocates and financing experts to achieve housing goals. That’s why we created Housing Production Partners.

Name: KKN-RM UINSATU Gegerian Tulungagung
Description:
Tentang Gegerian | KKN-RM UIN SATU Tulungagung 2025
KKN Geger adalah program pemberdayaan masyarakat berbasis multidisipliner yang dilaksanakan oleh KKN-RM UIN Sayyid Ali Rahmatullah Tulungagung. Fokus utama program ini adalah mendorong pembangunan berkelanjutan di Desa Geger melalui penguatan literasi digital dan penerapan konsep desa ramah lingkungan.

Melalui kolaborasi antara mahasiswa, masyarakat lokal, pemerintah desa, dan mitra kelembagaan, KKN Geger hadir untuk menjawab tantangan nyata di desa serta menggali potensi lokal yang ada.

Dengan tema:
“Literasi Digital Menuju Desa Ramah Lingkungan”,

misi kami adalah membangun desa yang cerdas, mandiri, dan berwawasan lingkungan melalui transformasi digital yang inklusif.

 Program Unggulan:
· Digitalisasi Pariwisata Desa: Mempromosikan potensi wisata lokal berbasis teknologi digital
· Pemanfaatan Limbah Sapi: Mengolah limbah ternak menjadi pupuk organik ramah lingkungan
· Pembuatan Eco-Enzyme: Produksi cairan serbaguna berbahan sampah organik untuk mendukung kebersihan dan pertanian
· Lomba Muharram: Menumbuhkan semangat religius melalui kegiatan lomba keagamaan
· Negeri Dolanan: Wahana permainan tradisional untuk anak-anak SD guna melestarikan budaya lokal

 Visi Kami:
Menjadikan Desa Geger sebagai desa percontohan dalam literasi digital dan pelestarian lingkungan yang berbasis partisipasi aktif masyarakat serta kearifan lokal.

Name: Ecovillage Findhorn CBS
Description:
The Ecovillage Findhorn Community Benefit Society was founded in order to take over stewardship of the lands and assets from the Findhorn Foundation charity, which has since closed, to preserve for future community use.

We did it! As of Monday 18th November 2024, we all, the community, via Ecovillage Findhorn Community Benefit Society, became the owners of both Part 1 (Guest Lodge, YP, Bunkhouse, Village Green, Solum (FoD Green), Phoenix shop building, mobile home land, long let caravans, bungalows and yurt) and Part 2 (Universal Hall, Biomass boiler, Skylab - ex Big Sky, laundry and the associated land), thanks to a dedicated, hard-working and small board and staff team and large community!

Ecovillage Findhorn CBS aims to stewarding the community-owned land and assets to foster regeneration, connection, eco-spiritual education, and the arts. Working closely with local community organisations, such as the Findhorn Foundation SCIO and New Findhorn Association (NFA) and members of The Park Ecovillage Findhorn, we care for these lands and assets to ensure a vibrant present and future for all. 

We still have a lot to achieve, such as buying out Part 3 and more! So please support our community!
In 2025 we are opening the 18 beds accommodation of the Guestlodge and Bunkhouse to retreat holders, holding their programmes in our vibrant community. If you are a facilitator, please enquire here: bookings@ecovillagefindhorn.uk.

Get involved and be part of the journey!
Stay up to date with our riveting newsletter here: ecovillagefindhorn.uk
Subscribe to support: ecovillagefindhorn.uk/donate

We hope to see you soon!

Description:

We actively purchase and promote land for development, focusing on sites with short, medium, or long-term potential - whether for residential, mixed-use, or commercial use. Our work spans both direct acquisitions for our own portfolio and collaborative land promotion partnerships with landowners who want to unlock hidden value in their land.

We consider a wide range of opportunities, including:

• Garden land and infill plots
• Larger houses or bungalows on generous plots
• Redundant commercial and brownfield sites
• Select greenfield, green belt, and emerging grey belt land

With recent planning policy shifts creating new grey belt opportunities, we’ve developed a strong specialism in identifying, promoting, and securing planning on sites previously considered constrained - helping landowners achieve substantial and often life-changing uplifts in value.

All acquisitions and promotions are self-funded, enabling us to act quickly and decisively, including on a pre-planning or unconditional basis. Our approach combines the flexibility of a private developer with the planning expertise of a specialist promoter, ensuring mutually beneficial outcomes for every partner we work with.

With a proven track record of navigating the planning system, we bring experience, funding, and local insight together to deliver results and long-term opportunities.

We provide affordable, sustainable housing solutions, including refugee shelters, long-term care facilities, and homeless shelters, and products such as hunting cabins, cold storage and portable offices that are custom-designed and built from recycled plastic waste.
Countries worldwide face a Housing crisis with a massive shortage of homes for expanding populations. According to the United Nation's statistics, about 1.6 billion people live in substandard housing, and 100 million are homeless. The housing affordability crisis is also at the forefront as housing prices are currently more than three times the median family income and unaffordable in more than 90% of 502 international cities. Our portfolio of housing products will transform the housing market for a sustainable and inclusive economy by working and investing in housing infrastructures and land developments. 
Sustainable Development is a development that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs. Sustainable development practices help countries grow in ways that adapt to the challenges posed by climate change, which helps protect critical natural resources for our future generations. The company uses recycled plastic waste to reduce the environmental impact and global warming.

Name: Nunatsiavut Housing Commission
Description:
The Public Agency for Housing for Nunatsiavut Government. The Nunatsiavut Housing Commission may acquire, construct, develop, maintain, manage and supply housing in Nunatsiavut for Inuit families and individuals. The Commission is specifically mandated to establish and administer programs and to acquire, construct, develop, maintain, manage and supply housing in Nunatsiavut for Inuit, Inuit households and Inuit families who are members of a vulnerable group.

The NHC has the power to be involved in the construction, development, management, and operations of housing of all types, from emergency shelters to affordable home rentals. The NHC is particularly mandated to respond to the housing needs of Inuit who are vulnerable, meaning those living with addictions, homelessness, disabilities, physical and mental, violence in the home, or those who are on a fixed income or low-income, elders, youth, children in need of social protection, and families at risk of being separated from their children. 

This new Agency responsible for housing represents years of effort and brings to life the right to adequate housing all Beneficiaries have pursuant to Article 2.4.28 of the Nunatsiavut Government Constitution.

Name: National Housing Construction Project
Description:
NHCP: Mission & About Us
To provide scalable, permanent housing solutions—from Conex units to Apartments to end homelessness nationwide. 
The National Homelessness Conservation Program (NHCP) is a nationwide, non-profit initiative dedicated to solving the housing crisis through an innovative, scalable, and fiscally responsible approach. 
Our Core Focus: Diverse, Permanent Housing Solutions
We recognize that there is no single solution to homelessness. That is why the NHCP champions a portfolio approach to housing development, focusing on four highly efficient, community-integrated models: 
Refurbished Conex Units: Rapid deployment for immediate, safe shelter. 
Tiny Homes: Sustainable, cost-effective individual and couple housing. Apartments: Efficient use of land for transitional and permanent supportive housing. 
Standard Housing Units: Integrating formerly homeless individuals back into traditional neighborhoods. 
The NHCP Difference
Our strategy is driven by the principles of Project Management: efficiency, resource optimization, and rapid deployment. We work with local governments, community leaders, and private sector partners to unlock underutilized resources and accelerate the creation of stable, long-term housing. 
We are building a coalition of industry leaders, volunteers, and advocates committed to providing every American with a roof over their head. Follow us to track our progress, learn about regional initiatives, and discover how you can bring the NHCP model to your community.
#EndHomelessness #HousingSolutions #NHCP #VeteransFirst

Description:
Small Living Company is dedicated to redefining housing in Ontario through innovative, sustainable, and affordable Small Living solutions. We specialize in tiny homes, modular housing, and secondary dwelling units (SDUs) that offer practical alternatives to traditional housing—whether for personal use, rental income, or community development.

Our mission is to provide high-quality, energy-efficient, and zoning-compliant tiny  homes that empower individuals, families, and communities to embrace a simpler, more sustainable lifestyle. By navigating Ontario’s evolving zoning laws, building codes, and municipal bylaws, we ensure that our customers can seamlessly integrate small housing solutions into their properties—whether in urban, suburban, or rural settings.

What We Offer
 Custom & Prefabricated Tiny Homes – Designed for comfort, efficiency, and long-term living
 Modular & Accessory Dwelling Units (ADUs/SDUs) – Ideal for rental income, multi-generational living, or affordable housing initiatives
 Sustainable Building Solutions – Energy-efficient materials and off-grid options
 Regulatory Guidance & Permitting Support – Helping homeowners and developers navigate legal requirements
 Partnerships & Community Projects – Collaborating with municipalities, developers, and organizations to expand housing options across Ontario

At Small Living Company, we believe that tiny homes create big opportunities. Whether you're a homeowner looking to maximize your property’s potential, an investor exploring rental income, or a community leader seeking affordable housing solutions, we provide expert guidance, turnkey solutions, and personalized support to make small living a reality.

Connect with us to explore how small living can work for you!

Name: G.E.R.U. Utrechtsch Studenten Corps
Description:
Het Gezelschap tot Exploitatie en Realisatie van Utrechtse Corpshuizen (GERU) streeft er sinds 2011 naar om leden van het Utrechtsch Studenten Corps te helpen bij het vinden van een geschikt studentenhuis, ofwel Corpshuis. Door middel van ondersteuning aan zowel investeerders als leden, streven wij ernaar om op lange termijn meer Corpshuizen te realiseren. Het GERU is daarom een onmisbare speler voor zowel leden als investeerders.

Het doel van het GERU is om het aantal woonplekken te vergroten, zodat ieder lid een Corpshuis in de mooiste studentenstad van Nederland, wetende Utrecht, kan bemachtigen. Op dit moment woont 65% in één van de 100 Corpshuizen die beschikbaar zijn voor enkel onze leden. Door de samenwerking met het GERU zorgen wij ervoor dat de woningen altijd bezet zullen blijven en dat er nooit leegstand komt te zijn. Echter zijn er momenteel nog steeds 420 leden voor wie het niet mogelijk is om een woonplek in een Corpshuis te bemachtigen. 

Het GERU is er alles aan gelegen om het aantal woonplekken te vergroten. Daarom staan wij open voor alle mogelijke contacten die hierbij kunnen helpen. Echter worden wij door investeringspartijen en door de Gemeente Utrecht, die vrijwel geen omzettingsvergunningen afgeeft, hierbij belemmerd. Daarom richten wij ons ook op het vinden van huizen die deze vergunningen al hebben en onderhouden wij nauw contact met de gemeente Utrecht om deze vergunningen te verkrijgen.

Wilt u meer weten over het GERU en wat wij doen of wilt u meer informatie over hoe u kunt bijdragen aan ons doel? Neem dan gerust contact met ons op via onze website https://www.usc-geru.nl. Wij staan u graag te woord en hopen u binnenkort te begroeten als onze supporter.

Better Living Together's Core Values:

Community - We believe that everyone should have access to a safe space in which to live, love, work and play, where people care about each other and share authentic friendships.

Responsibility - We believe that each individual’s contribution to the community ensures the well-being of the greater whole, and each individual plays a role in promoting and protecting the rights of each other.

Respect - We believe that every person deserves to be treated with kindness and to live within a culture of tolerance, compassion, and appreciation.

Diversity - We believe that each of our lives is enriched through new experiences and relationships that honor and embrace the range of humanity, including differences in ability.

Inclusion - We believe that everyone benefits from a culture of belonging, where individual talents, beliefs, and backgrounds are valued. We recognize and acknowledge the feelings and lived experiences of each other, especially those most different from ourselves.

Agency - We believe that all individuals will experience greater self-determination, fulfill their potential, and thrive through access to affordable resources, options, and support.

Physical hubs in underused urban and rural spaces that act as "living showrooms" to provide maximum exposure to companies with sustainable products, that function as community hubs to support local initiatives with the goal of applying lessons learned to a global index of solutions.

Modular Commons is a collaborative market place for companies to distribute sustainable products and systems. Name: The Farm at Okefenokee
Description:
The Farm at Okefenokee is a regenerative farm community located on the edge of the Okefenokee Swamp in South Georgia, built around a simple idea: real food, clean water, and meaningful connection to the land create healthier people and stronger communities.

More than a place to live, The Farm is a working landscape where residents, staff, and partners grow, raise, and steward what they consume. Our regenerative approach restores soil health, protects water, and produces nutrient-dense food—fruits, vegetables, eggs, beef, pork, poultry, and fish—grown and harvested on site.

Property owners are members of our Farm Owners Association, sharing in the harvest and participating in a living system that values stewardship over speculation. This model creates long-term resilience, transparency, and true alignment between the land and the people who call it home.

Beyond agriculture, The Farm is a hub for education, hospitality, and social impact. Through partnerships like the Market Garden Project with Trinity Rescue Mission, we provide hands-on training, purpose, and nourishment to individuals rebuilding their lives.

From farm-to-table dinners and orchard harvests to regenerative grazing and water management, everything we do is designed to reconnect people to where their food comes from—and why it matters.

This is not an agrihood.
It’s a living ecosystem for a better way of life.

Built in the 1960’s, the properties have been continually up-graded to meet more modern standards, including most recently, ground-source heating. Most of the housing is for single occupancy, however, two bungalows are designed for couples sharing. 

There is a communal building, which is free to use for all residents to promote socialising, entertainment, and peer-support. 

It is close to the local amenities, around a 500-yard walk to the Market square, 600 yards to the Doctors Surgery and a 50-yard walk to the nearest bus stop, outside of the Fire Station.

Name: Szych Foundation
Description:
Startujemy.

Szych Foundation powstaje jako odpowiedź na narastające zjawisko izolacji społecznej — ludzie są bliżej niż kiedykolwiek, a jednocześnie coraz rzadziej czują prawdziwą więź, bezpieczeństwo i przynależność.

Zmieniamy to już dziś. Wprowadzamy narzędzia dla pokoleń, które są najbardziej połączone cyfrowo, a zarazem najbardziej samotne emocjonalnie.

Nasze obszary działania:

• Infrastruktura społeczna – rozwiązania leczące przyczyny, nie jedynie skutki.
• Technologie społeczne – systemy oparte o dane, emocje i zachowania.
• Badania nad samotnością i relacjami – analiza trendów i zjawisk społecznych.
• Narzędzia świadomościowe – edukacja, autoanaliza i wsparcie emocjonalne.

Nasza misja:

Tworzymy technologie i narzędzia, które nie przykrywają objawów kryzysu relacji — lecz docierają do jego źródeł.

Nasze rozwiązania umożliwiają:

• odbudowę prawdziwych, głębokich relacji,
• odnalezienie autentycznej przynależności,
• odzyskanie poczucia wspólnoty i bezpieczeństwa emocjonalnego,
• ponowne połączenie się ze światem — w zdrowy i świadomy sposób.

Wierzymy, że przyszłość zaczyna się od relacji: z sobą, z innymi i ze światem.

Kto za tym stoi:

Szych Foundation została stworzona przez Jakuba Szycha – analityka zachowań i twórcę rozwiązań opartych o dane, zajmującego się relacjami społecznymi w Internecie, emocjami i technologią.
Projekt łączy osobiste doświadczenia, badania i praktyczną pracę nad systemami, które realnie wpływają na ludzką świadomość i poczucie przynależności.

Jeśli czujesz, że świat potrzebuje odbudowy więzi – dołącz już teraz:
https://szych.foundation

Name: Cohousing Harrow
Description:
Cohousing Harrow is a community from diverse backgrounds in terms of age, ethnicity, religion, employment status, interests, outlooks and abilities.  We want to create an intentional community and be part of the growing movement towards co-housing across the UK and abroad. 

We have come together to develop a community which values our diversity, creates good neighbours, supports future generations and inspires the wider community in North London and surrounding areas. 

We intend to build good quality, affordable, and sustainable housing which meets a variety of needs in terms of space, mixed financial structuring, and our shared values.

What is cohousing?
Cohousing is an intentional community model with shared values that is designed and managed by the community members.  A cohousing community consists of families, single people, couples and adults from a variety of backgrounds, bringing different skills and having differing needs.  Residents have their own private housing units but also share communal facilities.  They manage the development of the community, share activities and support each other.  The aim of cohousing is to overcome many of the inherent problems with individualised housing, such as affordability and isolation, and to ensure more sustainable living practices. 

The initial community members develop and co-design the community and housing project.  Later, as the community grows or changes, the residents manage, maintain and share communal activities.  There is a balance between private and communal spaces and engagement.  An important principle of a cohousing community is collective ownership and management of the community and its spaces. To this end, financial and legal aspects are co-designed and the community develops its own democractic form of governance.

Name: J49
Description:
J49 was established in response to the enormous need for affordable housing in London. We operate as a not-for-profit organisation and a registered charity, dedicated to tackling housing challenges with compassion and innovation.

We achieve this by incorporating community ‘hub’ spaces into our developments — places for mentoring, networking, and relationship-building.

Our work is guided by three key priorities:

Creating multi-generational living spaces that also serve as vibrant community hubs.

Reducing homelessness and limited housing options among young people, with a particular focus on the 18–35 demographic.

Adding a new dimension to the ‘London way of living’ by providing practical support and guidance for residents.

As a trusted partner of Homes England and the Greater London Authority, J49 is proud to be working with local authorities nationally — including West Sussex, East Sussex, Plymouth, Lancashire, Croydon, and Lewisham — to deliver affordable, sustainable housing solutions that make a lasting impact.

Through these collaborations, J49 transforms under-utilised assets into thriving spaces for residents, local businesses, and the wider community.

Name: PSA Living
Description:
PSA Living is London’s only systemically therapeutic, sports and wellbeing supported living home for young women aged 16+.

Founded by Porsha Robinson, Shakira Ashmeil and Allana Ashmeil — three Black women with lived and professional experience — PSA Living exists to challenge the status quo in supported accommodation.

We don't just offer housing. We offer healing.
Our trauma-informed S.T.E.P.S programme provides each young woman with a personalised plan that includes one-to-one support, mentoring, therapeutic group work, creative expression and access to sports and fitness. We believe that mental and physical wellbeing go hand in hand — and we make space for both.

At PSA Living, we centre care, consistency and community. We understand the complex needs of young people who are often navigating systems that weren’t built with them in mind. Our approach is culturally responsive, emotionally intelligent and rooted in the belief that every young woman deserves not just safety — but joy, belonging and opportunity.

We’re not here to tick boxes. We’re here to raise the standard.
PSA Living is home.

Name: Ashtanga Eco Village
Description:
Ashtanga Eco Village is on a mission to change how we live and connect with the world around us. We're building both online and physical communities where people who care deeply about health, nature and sustainability come together to create an everlasting change. 

Our vision is to develop self sufficient and self aware eco-villages across the globe, using sustainable design and practices that protects the environment and creates emotionally resilient and eco-conscious communities.

It’s not just about reducing waste or conserving resources; it’s about inculcating a deep connection with the environment that influences every aspect of life, from the products we use to the way we interact with each other.

The impact of such a lifestyle on the collective health of the community is profound. When people live in tune with nature, they are more likely to experience physical, mental, and emotional well-being. Clean air, fresh food, and a close-knit community enriches an environment where individuals thrive. The emphasis on sustainability also nurtures a sense of purpose and fulfillment, as members know their efforts contribute to something greater than themselves.

The problem lies in our disconnected and unsustainable way of living that harms both the planet and our well-being. Rapid urbanization, overconsumption, and reliance on non-renewable resources have caused environmental degradation, loss of community, and declining health all across the globe. 

Our solution is a holistic approach which involves building communities that live in harmony with nature, who actively minimizes their carbon footprint through sustainable practices and products. 

We truly believe that reconnecting with the environment and each other directly addresses these issues, building a healthier, more resilient way of life for all of humanity.

The PHC aims at the development of the housing sector and contributes to solving the housing crisis that has been exacerbated by the dire economic and political situation in the Palestinian territories. The PHC was able to recruit more than ($250) million to contribute to solving habitations problems in Palestine and from which 11 thousand  Palestinian families, more than 3 thousand families from Jerusalem, benefited through the council’s various programs and projects. The PHC contributed to the preparation of plans, strategies and studies in the area of housing in order to solve the housing mounted problem owing to the unsettled political conditions.  

PHC was awarded the UN-Habitat Scroll of Honor Award 2007 in recognition of its exceptional efforts in helping to provide homes, employment prospects, and bringing hope to countless Palestinian families. PHC also received Dubai International Award for best practices in improving living conditions and knowledge transfer for 2008.

Name: sherlo homes GmbH
Description:
Das ist sherlo – das war sherlo.
Von 2021 bis Sommer 2025 plante sherlo als Mietshäusersyndikats-Initiative drei Häusern für über 60 Personen im Sheridanpark in Augsburg. Viel Engagement, viel Unterstützung, viele DirektkreditgeberInnen.
Nun aber ist Schluss. All die Jahre hat sherlo jede neue oder geänderte Anforderung aufgenommen und überwunden. Die letzte Hürde aber war zu hoch: 
In 2025 sind die Fördertöpfe für sozialen Wohnungsbau in Bayern leer. Und dann beschließt die Bayerische Landesregierung, dass Fördermittel für den sozialen Wohnungsbau (EOF) künftig verteilt und zu einem späteren Zeitpunkt ausgezahlt werden. 
In Folge hätte das Wohnprojekt alles vorfinanzieren müssen.
So können private Initiativen und eben sherlo keinen Beitrag für die Schaffung bezahlbaren Wohnraums schaffen.
Sherlo wird nun rückabgewickelt, das Grundstück wird an die Stadt zurückgegeben.
Wer noch helfen will bei der Rückabwicklung, insbesondere um die Verluste der DirektkreditgeberInnen möglichst klein zu halten, der schaue bitte auf https://gofund.me/f693ac4b4. 
DANKE an alle, die sherlo während all dieser Zeit unterstützt haben.
Neuste Infos ansonsten weiterhin auf www.sherlo.org.

Name: Embrace Your Shine
Description:
At the Embrace Your Shine  Campus, we are dedicated to making a tangible difference in the lives of the UNSEEN young people in Kansas City. As an 501c3 organization, we focus on a holistic approach to addressing Hidden Homelessness, recognizing that safe housing is just one piece of the puzzle. 
In addition to building supportive housing, we recognize and believe that the root cause of long-term success is not just technical skills or career preparation - it is mindset. Many transition-aged youth & young adults in our community face challenges that are beyond their control. Challenges such as housing insecurity, toxic stress, and lack of consistent support leading to self-doubt, hopelessness, & survival mode thinking that undermines their ability to fully benefit from educational, workforce programs, or career opportunities. 

To address this, EYS is also launching Embrace Life, a 20-wk Personal Development Bootcamp designed to build the INTERNAL TOOLS necessary to thrive. The purpose of this program is to strengthen their mental well being and help them unlock self belief, resilience, & shift limiting beliefs so participants can pursue their highest aspirations with confidence. 
The Embrace Life program serves as an extension to local existing workforce & training programs already equipping young people with trades, entrepreneurial skills, and career pathways. 

The Embrace Your Shine Campus combined with the Embrace Life program gives young people the opportunity to build the resilience and self belief they need to thrive - not just in school or work, but in life! 
Blooming a generation of young people empowered to rewrite their story and grow stronger than their circumstances. 

The result is not only improved mental wellbeing, but also greater engagement in school, training, & career pathways. 
we aim to Enrich Local Ecosystem Developments with our unique approach - COMBINING transitional housing w/ Comprehensive support - WILL Empower  these up-coming leaders.

Name: Village Development Association
Description:
The Village Development Association is a non-governmental organization founded in August 1989 with the aim of supporting the survival of small villages in Hungary. Its founders included folk cultivators, regional planners, architects and pastors. The focus of their activities is on economically and infrastructurally backward, multiply disadvantaged and cross-border small landscapes. The Association focuses on the reconstruction of local communities, the stimulation of local action and the practical issues of village modernization. 
One of the most significant achievements of the Association was the establishment of the institution of village caretaker service, which was developed by its founder, Bertalan Kemény. The village caretaker is the leaven and trustee of the community, who serves the village with great autonomy according to the needs of the people living there. Based on the idea born in 1990, the village caretaker service was established in 1991, which now has almost 2000 active village caretakers. 
The Society has launched several programs and projects for the development of rural life and villages. These include the "My Grandfather's House" program series, which aims to maintain the traditional building culture and help the craft survive. Within the framework of the program, lecture series and construction camps are organized, where participants can gain practical experience of traditional construction techniques. 
In addition, the Society organizes the Bertalan Kemény Village Development Award ceremony and the related conference, where village trustees and rural and village developers are rewarded and various aspects of rural life are discussed. 
Overall, the activities of the Village Development Association are aimed at strengthening Hungarian rural communities, supporting local initiatives and preserving traditional values.

The primary location the Smoketown Community Land Trust will be doing its work is in the Smoketown Community of Louisville, KY. Smoketown is a neighborhood one mile southeast of downtown Louisville, Kentucky. Smoketown has been a historically black neighborhood since the Civil War. It is the only neighborhood in the city that has had such a continuous presence. The defined service area for the Corporation shall be to the east, South Brook Street, to the south East St. Catherine Street, to the west the CSX railroad tracks that are parallel to Logan Street and to the north East Broadway. Name: Suncoast Housing Collaborative
Description:
The Suncoast Housing Collaborative (SHC) was formed in 2022 as an intentional partnership between service providers, community members, and property owners focused on identifying solutions to the housing shortage in Manatee and Sarasota counties.

Objective
SHC believes in a Housing First model which aims to connect people experiencing homelessness and housing insecurity to permanent housing as the first step in gaining self-sufficiency.

We believe that success goes beyond obtaining physical shelter. Wraparound services are provided by local nonprofits to help each unique person or family receive the resources they need to establish long-term housing stability.

How It Works
We offer financial incentives and other benefits to landlords, property owners, and building managers in Sarasota and Manatee counties who lower rental barriers for applicants coming from the SHC. These barriers include prior eviction history, requirements that a tenant must earn at least 2x monthly rent, not allowing pets, or criminal history. 

New members of our program will receive $2,000 for each of their first five units leased up through the SHC. Existing members receive a universal $500 for each move-in without limitation.  We also offer a property owner guarantee fund above and beyond a security deposit in the rare case of excessive damages to property. Along with all this, we also partner with Suncoast Community Mediation in case 3rd party mediation services are needed. 

Tenants coming through the SHC will meet regularly with Case Managers who provide the support needed to ensure a successful tenancy including mental health support, and life skills training like budgeting and house upkeep. 

If you're a landlord or property owner in our community, we hope we'll hear from you soon to see how we can work together and make sure all of our neighbors have a safe and affordable place to call home.

ASAM 3.1 Community Housing

We are a fully permitted, government-backed regenerative community on Mexico’s Riviera Nayarit — the first of its kind in the region. Spanning 171 hectares, the project integrates residential, hospitality, healthcare and commercial spaces within a circular economy model that has fully self-sufficient water, renewable energy, food and waste systems.

With federal, state and municipal support, Los Pueblos offers developers and investors a rare opportunity to align with a project that is both ESG-driven and market-ready. Designed to meet LEED and EDGE certification standards, the community represents a new era of resilient, wellness-centered living.

Anchored in environmental stewardship and cultural respect, Los Pueblos is more than a development. It is setting a new benchmark for how prosperity, wellness and ecological protection can thrive together. From oceanfront hotels and wellness-driven retirement living to biodiversity corridors and job creation for local communities, Los Pueblos is shaping the future of regenerative development.

Connect with us to explore investment opportunities, partnership possibilities — for both hotels and residential communities — and the groundbreaking vision we’re bringing to life.

Name: Ulo
Description:
Ulo partners with local authorities to deliver affordable housing without upfront capital investment. Through innovative long-term funding partnerships with institutional investors, we enable councils to expand housing supply while maintaining full control over tenant nominations and property management.

Our model addresses the temporary accommodation crisis by providing councils with immediate access to quality homes that transfer to full council ownership after the partnership term. Properties are protected from Right to Buy and generate rental income that councils can reinvest in community services.

Founded by experienced housing, finance and legal professionals, Ulo works with councils across the UK to create sustainable housing solutions that serve communities for decades.

We are building partnerships that work for everyone: councils reducing temporary accommodation costs, communities gaining permanent housing, and residents finding stable homes.

The project development designed by the company's architects HLC will use an area of 240 hectares. The 18-hole golf complex of championship standard in the valley, designed by international golf architect Dave Thomas, will be overlooked by carefully sited luxury villas, townhouses and apartments.

To the north a charming Portuguese style village will bring local colour and character to the development and a distinctive apartment complex will have commanding view over the river. There will be a magnificent 5-star hotel on high ground overlooking the river and Spain beyond. 

Two further villages, Golf Village and Guadiana Village will complete the complex, making it a self-contained resort of the highest quality, rivalling all Europe's finest developments.

Name: Bhutan Drinchengang Regenerative Village (DRV)
Description:
Drinchengang Regenerative Village (DRV) is pioneering Bhutan’s first living blueprint for regenerative prosperity—where heritage, innovation, and community resilience converge.

Once home to Bhutan’s master masons who built the kingdom’s timeless Dzongs—monumental temple-fortresses that served as spiritual centers and administrative strongholds, helping to unify Bhutan—Drinchengang now stands at the forefront of renewal. Guided by Bhutanese values and the vision of His Majesty The King, we are transforming this historic community into a model village rooted in regenerative principles—revitalizing culture, strengthening livelihoods, and advancing decentralized infrastructure.

Our strategy integrates:
	•	Cultural Architecture & Climate Regeneration – reimagining traditional homes with resilient, green design.
	•	Village-Led Prosperity – cooperative enterprises, regenerative agriculture, and experiential hospitality.
	•	Decentralized Infrastructure & Tokenization – blockchain-enabled land co-ownership and village tokens.

Name: Caracara Ridge
Description:
Welcome to Caracara Ridge, an exclusive community nestled in the tranquil hills above Ojochal, Costa Rica. Tucked into jungle hilltops alive with wildlife, Caracara Ridge offers sweeping ocean and Talamanca Mountain views, cooling tropical breezes, and the peaceful symphony of the rainforest—while beautiful beaches, shopping, and renowned restaurants are just minutes away.

Guided by a nature-first philosophy, each estate-sized homesite is thoughtfully planned to work with the land—preserving mature forest, contours, and sightlines—so owners enjoy stunning vistas of the Pacific Ocean, rainforest, rivers, and waterfalls without sacrificing habitat or serenity. Our community embraces low-impact living and long-term stewardship, inviting residents to connect with the landscape while protecting the biodiversity that makes the Osa region extraordinary.

Highlights:

Estate-sized lots designed to preserve natural beauty and maximize views

Abundant wildlife corridors and lush jungle surroundings

Minutes to beaches, services, and Ojochal’s acclaimed dining scene

Peaceful setting with ocean + mountain panoramas and constant breezes

Community ethos centered on privacy, conservation, and quality of life

Explore Caracara Ridge—where thoughtful design, protected nature, and effortless coastal living come together in the heart of Costa Ballena.

Name: Manaaki Rangatahi
Description:
Manaaki Rangatahi is a mokopuna-focused, systems change rōpū. We work alongside housing providers and youth services across the motu, using kotahitanga and collective impact to bring awareness to youth homelessness. 

Our calls to action are:

1. Right to Housing: Enshrine the right to housing by enacting legislation
2. Government Policy: Prohibit government agencies from releasing youth into homelessness
3. Prevention strategy: Develop a comprehensive strategy to prevent/end youth homelessness
4. Data Collection: Collect Data on the Size and Scope of Youth Homelessness in Aotearoa
5. Commission Research: Commission Research into the Effects of Youth Homelessness on Adult Homelessness
6. Upskilling: Invest in upscaling and upskilling youth housing/homelessness services

Through a coordinate and evidence based, kaupapa Māori approach, we aim to prevent, and end youth homelessness in Aotearoa.

Our Approach:
Future-Proof Design: Infusing papakāinga with forward-looking architectural solutions to ensure adaptability and sustainability for future generations.

Cultural Harmony: Seamlessly integrating Māori values into every facet of design and construction, creating homes that resonate with the whānau wairua.

Tech-Driven Solutions: Harnessing the power of technology to enhance efficiency, security, and connectivity within the papakāinga, while maintaining cultural authenticity.

Community Collaboration: Prioritising community input in the planning process, ensuring that each papakāinga is a collective expression of dreams and aspirations.

Green Building Practices: Implementing eco-friendly materials and sustainable construction methods to minimise environmental impact and promote holistic well-being.

Our Services:
Project management
Strategic planning
Plan implementation
Establishing systems and processes

Identifying and connecting hāpori to funding and investment opportunities 
Funding applications

Communication plans

It's a place where youth, families, and neighbors come together to learn, grow, and create a brighter future. We’re excited to lead a planning process to work with our community to chart the future of the Neighborhood Hub site, and opportunities for equitable development in East Kinston.

We are currently working with the Raleigh-based architecture studio Design Workshop to create a site and facility framework plan for a larger facility that is more responsive to the needs of our organization and community. Our vision for this space is for it to be one that is multi-functional—with the ability to hold large meetings and gatherings, to be used as a staging area for emergency relief and response efforts, to be renewable and sustainable, and to have a solar microgrid to collect and store energy to keep the facility (and our programs) operational in the event of a disaster or power outage.

Name: Eden Purposeful Property
Description:
Eden Purposeful Property create long-term homes for vulnerable people by transforming underused buildings into high-quality supported housing.

At Eden Purposeful Property, we believe everyone deserves a safe, stable place to call home — especially those too often forgotten by the system:
 Care leavers
 People ready for hospital discharge
 Families stuck in temporary accommodation
 Individuals at risk of rough sleeping or repeat homelessness

We work in partnership with landlords, investors, housing providers, and local authorities to deliver tenant-first housing solutions with social impact baked in.

Our model is simple:

 We source and refurbish suitable properties across the UK
 We partner with trusted, regulated support providers
 We offer secure long leases (often 3–10 years) with full repairing responsibilities
 We ensure the people housed receive ongoing care and support

The result?
• Landlords get guaranteed, hands-off income
• Providers get access to safe, compliant homes
• Local Authorities reduce their budgets and bed-blocking
• Vulnerable individuals get the dignity and safety of a real home

Whether you're a landlord with a property, an investor looking to create impact with your capital, or a supported housing provider looking for long-term accommodation — we should talk.

Name: The Earl of Southampton Trust
Description:
About Us
The Earl of Southampton Trust is an amalgamation of five charities, the oldest of which, called ‘Godwin’s charity’, dates from 1597 and the largest of which was called ‘The Earl of Southampton Trust’.  We work in the Ancient Parish of Titchfield, which encompasses Titchfield, Stubbington, Warsash, Locks Heath, Park Gate, Lee-on-the-Solent and Whiteley. The Earl of Southampton Trust seeks to ensure that it makes a real difference to its beneficiaries and makes a positive impact on people’s lives. 

Housing
The Earl of Southampton Trust has a long history of housing people in need and currently has 19 houses situated in Titchfield village.  We provide housing for people in need and currently resident in the Ancient Parish of Titchfield.  The Trust currently owns 10 two-bedroom houses, 7 one-bedroom houses and 2 three-bedroom houses in Titchfield village. Seven of these properties are for older people only. When one of these properties becomes available it is advertised around the Ancient Parish. 

Grants
The Earl of Southampton Trust provides one-off grants to help local individuals and organisations to improve the quality of their lives. Applications for grants must be made by a recognised agency and are means-tested.

Trustees
The charity is governed by a board of trustees. The trustees are responsible for the governance of the Trust and are unpaid and give their time freely to the charity. The day to day running of the charity is entrusted to the Clerk to the Trustees.

Nuestro equipo de arquitectos altamente capacitados y creativos se dedica a diseñar espacios excepcionales que combinan funcionalidad y estética.

Nuestros servicios incluyen:
1. Diseño arquitectónico: Creamos planos y diseños innovadores que se adaptan a las necesidades y deseos de nuestros clientes, desde residencias hasta espacios comerciales y públicos.
2. Desarrollo de proyectos: Nos encargamos de la planificación y gestión integral de proyectos inmobiliarios, desde la concepción hasta la entrega final.
3. Consultoría y asesoría: Brindamos asesoramiento experto en materia de inversión inmobiliaria, normativas y tendencias del mercado, garantizando decisiones informadas y exitosas.
4. Remodelaciones y rehabilitaciones: Transformamos espacios existentes, rejuveneciendo edificios y otorgándoles una nueva vida para cumplir con las expectativas actuales.
5. Diseño sostenible: Nos comprometemos a aplicar prácticas arquitectónicas sostenibles para reducir el impacto ambiental y crear entornos respetuosos con el medio ambiente.

En E Habitare Real Estate, nuestra dedicación a la excelencia y la búsqueda constante de la innovación nos distinguen, y nos esforzamos por superar las expectativas de nuestros clientes en cada proyecto que emprendemos.

Name: Bridgeway Community Housing & Health Inc.
Description:
Bridgeway Community Housing & Health Inc. (Bridgeway CHH) is a mission-driven organization dedicated to innovative housing development, sustainable financing solutions, and equitable housing policies that strengthen communities. We specialize in affordable housing, workforce housing, and mixed-use developments that promote long-term stability, economic growth, and social impact.  

Our work spans strategic housing development, public-private partnerships, and innovative financing solutions to create affordable, accessible, and high-quality homes for underserved populations. We collaborate with government agencies, financial institutions, nonprofit organizations, and community leaders to drive policy reforms, housing affordability, and inclusive economic development.  

Our Focus Areas: 
Developing Innovative Housing Solutions – We create sustainable, affordable, and workforce housing that fosters economic stability and community growth.
Strengthening Communities – Through strategic partnerships, inclusive development, and community-driven initiatives, we empower neighborhoods and families.
Advancing Equitable Policy & Financing – We advocate for fair housing policies and leverage innovative funding models to expand access to safe, affordable homes.

Let’s Build Together!
Bridgeway CHH is committed to transforming communities through sustainable housing solutions. Connect with us to collaborate, invest, or support our mission of expanding housing opportunities and economic stability.

(주)천지인코퍼레이션은 부동산 개발과 마케팅 분야에서 또 다른 시각으로 새로운 부동산 분야의 발전을 위해 노력하여 왔습니다. 대표이사는 물론 임직원 모두 현장에서 함께 뛰어다닐 수 있는 진취적인 회사가 바로 천지인코퍼레이션입니다.

소비자의 니즈(Needs)가 무엇인지 정확히 분석하여 고객이 만족할 수 있는 창의적인 사업 구도로 맡겨진 프로젝트를 한치의 흔들림 없이 수행하여 성공의 열매로 결실을 거두도록 하겠습니다. 또한 신뢰, 정직, 열정이 살아 꿈틀거리는 천지인코퍼레이션의 모습을 여러분께 보여드릴 것을 약속드립니다.

기획에서 분양 및 운영관리에 이르기까지 그동안 쌓아온 천지인코퍼레이션만의 노하우(Know-how)로 급변하는 부동산 시장과 다양한 소비자의 요구에 부응하여 무에서 를 창조하는 천지인코퍼레이션이 될 것을 약속드립니다.
"We make value and share profit with our partner"

Name: Samson Housing Group Ltd
Description:
Samson Housing Group (SHG) is a forward-thinking housing association dedicated to creating high quality, affordable, and sustainable housing across the UK. Founded on the principles of social responsibility,  equality, and innovation, SHG is part of the wider Samson Group—alongside Samson Homes, Samson  Consultants, and Samson Space. 

Our mission is to deliver homes that not only meet urgent housing needs but also empower communities, improve lives, and leave a lasting positive impact.

The Full Story
Samson Housing Group (SHG) is a forward-thinking housing association dedicated to creating high quality, affordable, and sustainable housing across the UK. Founded on the principles of social responsibility,  equality, and innovation, SHG is part of the wider Samson Group—alongside Samson Homes, Samson  Consultants, and Samson Space. 

Our mission is to deliver homes that not only meet urgent housing needs but also empower communities, improve lives, and leave a lasting positive impact.

Our Origins & Purpose
Born out of a deep understanding of the housing challenges in the UK, SHG was established to tackle the shortage of secure, high-quality housing. We believe everyone deserves the dignity of a safe, welcoming, and affordable home—regardless of background or circumstance.

We approach this mission holistically, managing the entire development lifecycle from acquisition and planning to design, construction, and long-term stewardship.

What We Do
SHG works collaboratively with local authorities, care providers, and funders to deliver a range of supported and general needs housing, tailored to the unique needs of residents. We specialize in:

Permanent social and affordable housing
Supported housing for vulnerable groups
Regeneration and refurbishment of existing stock
Mixed-tenure and design-led
 
Through our group companies:
Samson Homes brings design excellence and build quality.
Samson Consultants ensures every project is guided by expert valuation

Name: Fuller Center for Housing of Delaware
Description:
The Fuller Center for Housing of Delaware is a covenant partner with The Fuller Center for Housing located in Americus, Georgia. We are a faith-driven and Christ-centered, promoting collaboration and innovative partnerships with individuals, organizations, corporations, and religious groups of all faiths in an unrelenting quest to provide adequate shelter for all people in need. 

Specifically, we are a nonprofit organization that improves communities in Delaware by giving a “hand up, not a hand out” with simple, decent tiny houses, accessory dwelling units and small scale actions that lead to big changes to increase housing. We are driven by this ministry because we believe that people should have healthy housing choices near where they work, learn, play, and pray at a price their salary allows them to afford. 

Our work is made possible by partnerships.  We work in communities where we are invited — in rural and urban communities of all sizes. We are willing to work in any place where people need safe, simple, affordable housing and have requested our help. The community knows what it needs, and Fuller Center provides a mechanism to get it done.

Further, when a community experiences improvements in housing, not only do you see increases in security, employment, and real estate values. But you see reductions in crime, joblessness, and homelessness. 

The Fuller Center for Housing and the FCHDE are 501(c) (3) nonprofits. All donations are tax-deductible.

Name: Mosquito Creek Foundation
Description:
The Mosquito Creek Foundation is a management body that incorporated in 1995; it is comprised of 6 board members and approximately 40 staff (plus Alberta Health Services staffing). The Foundation is a non-profit organization that is dedicated to safe, secure housing of seniors located in the communities of Nanton and Stavely in the MD of Willow Creek, in southern Alberta.

Mosquito Creek Foundation owns and operates the Silver Willow Lodge in Nanton which was originally built in 1977. In 1984 the Foundation assumed management of Seniors Self Contained Housing at Kilmorie Place and Oxley Manor in 1995; both buildings are registered under the Alberta Housing Act.

An addition to the Silver Willow Lodge was completed in May 2009 and is classed as a designated supportive living facility (DSL) regulated by Alberta Health Services. This facility boasts nice one bedroom units and 21 studio rooms, bringing the total to 30 rooms and the building as a whole to 81 units.

Name: Cottington Close Resident Management Board
Description:
Cottington Close Estate is in Kennington, London SE11.

Previously managed by Lambeth Council we became a TMO in September 1993.

Now renamed RMO (Resident Managed Organisation) to be more inclusive of all residents on our estate. We have a small, friendly in-house staff team, a thriving community garden and lots of events for residents.
A Tenant Management Organisation (TMO) is an organisation set up under the UK Government’s Housing (Right to Manage) Regulations 1994, which allow residents of council housing or housing association homes in the UK to take over responsibility for the running of their homes.

Cottington Close TMC became a corporate body and annually elected a management committee to run the body. This body then entered into a formal legal contract with Lambeth, known as the Management Agreement.

The Management Agreement details precisely which services are managed by the TMO on behalf of the landlord. Our management include day-to-day repairs, allocations and lettings, tenancy management, cleaning and caretaking, and rent collection. The TMO’s operations are funded by the management fees payable by Lambeth under the agreement.

Cottington Close TMC changed its name to Cottington Close Resident Management Organisation (RMO) to encompass not just Lambeth Council tenants but leaseholders and other residents on the estate.

 

Our Mission Statement

‘To deliver excellent resident focused housing services and services that contributes to the invigoration and cohesion of our community’



Our Vision

‘A clean green and safe place we are proud to call home, with a thriving community where every voice counts’

Name: The Warsaw House Foundation
Description:
For the fourth year in a row, Poland was ranked the worst country in the EU for LGBTQ+ rights and safety.  

We lead efforts to learn about and serve LGBTQ+ young people (regardless of origin), residing in Poland in the context of a conservative government, the Ukrainian diaspora, and the neighboring reactionary governments of Belarus and Russia. A large, expanding, and diverse group of young people are in need of refuge and support. An estimated 10% of young people (18-29) identify as LGBTQ+, an identity that often brings rejection and exclusion from family and religious establishments, driving homelessness and hopelessness. LGBTQ+ youth are faced with scarcities of food and safe living arrangements, while also being at risk of being forced into transactional sex or actively trafficked, experience suicidal ideation, and are common victims of Gender Based Violence. 

We serve LGBTQ+ youth while simultaneously learning about their particular needs.

Name: Ecowijk Brabant
Description:
In onze ecowijk wonen mensen van alle leeftijden. Ieder heeft zijn eigen woonplek, samen, alleen of in gezinsverband. Je vindt er ook speelruimte voor de kinderen, een moestuin en hopelijk later een voedselbos. We nemen de verantwoordelijkheid voor deze gemeenschappelijke ruimtes en voor de omgeving waarin wij wonen. Dat doen we met mensen, die ja zeggen tegen onze kernwaarden: sociaal leven, duurzaam wonen en natuur herstellen.

Sociaal leven
De basis voor onze wijk is de sociale kernwaarde. Samen werken we aan onze idealen. We zijn ervan overtuigd dat iedereen hieraan kan bijdragen. Binnen ons collectief willen wij omzien naar elkaar met respect voor ieders persoonlijke grenzen. Dat houdt in dat we meer zijn dan alleen buren. De uitdaging hierbij is balans houden tussen individuele vrijheid en samenwerken/samenleven. Diversiteit in leeftijd en samenstelling ervaren we als een rijkdom.

Duurzaam wonen
We willen de aarde zo gebruiken dat de toekomstige generaties er nog met plezier kunnen leven. Duurzaamheid nu garandeert dat de aarde op langere termijn het totaal van onze consumptie kan dragen.

Natuur herstellen
Bij onze plannen houden we rekening met de natuur, we werken er als het ware mee samen. 

Zie onze website voor meer toelichting op deze kernwaarden.

Wil jij meedoen? We ontmoeten graag mensen die deze wijk samen willen bouwen!

Name: Big River Community Land Trust
Description: We are  a non-profit affordable housing organization and use the Community Land Trust model that's also been used around the country.  The basic premise is the non-profit owns the land but sells a home (while leasing the land for a small fee) to someone who makes 80-120% of the area's Median Family Income (MFI) as defined by HUD.  By keeping the price of the land out of the cost of the home the home becomes more affordable.  Furthermore, by keeping control of the land the organization can ensure the house is resold at a price lower than market rate, and also to a buyer who is making 80-120% of MFI.  The best part is this can happen forever.

Vision
A thriving Center where every neighbor knows the value of what they own, the power of what they can build, and the joy of growing our community together.
Center Vitality / Manos a la Obra es un movimiento para despertar la vitalidad de la comunidad de Center — esa energía que se siente en el corazón, que nos une y nos empuja a salir adelante. Apoyamos a nuestra gente para que cumpla sus sueños: ya sea poner o hacer crecer un negocio, mejorar su casa, crear espacios para las nuevas generaciones, o invertir en nuestra comunidad. Todo, paso a paso, codo a codo, con orgullo y con ganas.
Aquí, 'vitalidad' es más que fuerza… es esa chispa que nos da vida, que nos hace sonreír y no rendirnos.
Visión
Un pueblo de Center lleno de vitalidad, donde cada vecino valore lo que tiene, crea en lo que puede lograr, y sienta la alegría de ver cómo nuestra comunidad crece unida. Queremos que esa vitalidad se note en las calles, en las familias y en cada proyecto que hagamos juntos.
Vitalidad es vida en abundancia, energía compartida y el orgullo de decir: 'Aquí es mi tierra, y aquí la hacemos crecer.'

Name: Ice Elements
Description:
Modul Baie aduce în România soluții moderne și eficiente pentru amenajarea băilor, prin module prefabricate complet echipate, produse de Sterchele S.p.A. Italia. Compania se adresează în special proiectelor ample – hoteluri, clădiri rezidențiale, spitale, cămine pentru bătrâni sau cămine studențești – acolo unde este nevoie de peste 20 de unități sanitare.

Fiecare modul este construit pe o structură de oțel ușor, cu panouri tip sandwich sau rigips, și include din fabrică toate finisajele, mobilierul și obiectele sanitare necesare. Procesul este gândit pentru a simplifica și accelera construcția: modulele sunt realizate și testate în fabrică, apoi ambalate și livrate pe șantier, gata pentru a fi montate. Instalarea se face rapid, prin poziționare pe placa de beton și conectarea directă la rețelele de apă, canalizare și electricitate.

Unul dintre marile avantaje este flexibilitatea în design – arhitecții și clienții pot adapta finisajele și dotările în funcție de stilul și cerințele proiectului. Astfel, Modul Baie oferă nu doar eficiență și economie de timp, ci și libertate creativă, fără compromisuri asupra calității.
