Name: Sunrise Master Planned Community
Description:
Sunrise Master-Planned Community spans an impressive 1,467 acres of development property, providing ample space for carefully designed residential and commercial areas. It boasts a harmonious blend of community living, nature's retreat, and the comforts of home, making it an ideal destination for families and individuals seeking a balanced and fulfilling lifestyle. With 21 single-family sub-developments, ample housing options are tailored to different preferences and needs. 

Currently, 3,255 units are already built, with a total of 4,728 units approved for construction. Corliss Management Group has been actively involved in permitting and selling approximately 3,000 single-family lots within the community, with an additional 900 lots in the permitting and development pipeline. The Sunrise Master-Planned Community offers up to 350,000 sq. ft of commercial space. The Sunrise Medical Center is one of three approved commercial sites, and the Sunrise Town Center serves as the second commercial area that is actively being developed. 

Sunrise also caters to the growing demand for multifamily housing options. Presently, there are 3 apartment complexes in the permitting and development pipeline, set to add 580 multifamily units to accommodate a diverse range of residents. To create a well-rounded and enjoyable living experience, Sunrise offers an array of recreational amenities. 

For families and pet owners, there are 2 schools, 2 dog parks, and 7 playgrounds. For fitness enthusiasts, there are tennis and basketball courts as well as a splash pad to beat the heat during warm days. 

Sunrise Master-Planned Community has proven to be a testament to thoughtful planning and responsible development. It stands as a shining example of how progress and conservation can go hand in hand. By embracing the tagline "Create a Place for Community Living, Nature's Retreat, and the Comforts of Home," Sunrise has become a cherished place for Washington residents.

Name: Starthoven
Description:
Initiatief Starthoven is ontstaan uit één simpele waarheid:

Steeds meer mensen komen de woningmarkt niet meer op. En dat verandert niet vanzelf.

We richten ons op mensen die wíllen bouwen aan een toekomst, maar daar geen ruimte voor krijgen.
Soms zijn dat starters, soms herstarters, soms mensen met een goed inkomen maar zonder spaargeld,
soms mensen die nét buiten alle regelingen vallen.
Wat ze delen: de wil om te wonen, om verantwoordelijkheid te nemen, maar een systeem dat hen tegenhoudt.

Wij willen geen tijdelijke opvang.
We bieden perspectief. Een eigen plek. En een langdurige commitment - van beide kanten.

Iedereen die deelneemt aan Starthoven committeert zich voor 10 jaar.
Niet omdat dat moet, maar omdat wonen niet draait om doorstroom;
het draait om wortels, rust, toekomst.

Daarom bouwen we aan kleinschalige, modulaire woonvormen.
Betaalbaar, zorgvuldig, in balans met de omgeving.
We starten in Eindhoven en omgeving. Stap voor stap. Eerlijk. Realistisch.

Starthoven is een stichting in wording.
We zijn een initiatief in ontwikkeling. Kwetsbaar en daadkrachtig tegelijk.
We kunnen niet iedereen helpen.
Maar wie bij ons past, EN bereid is mee te dragen... krijgt ruimte om te leven.

#woningmarkt #wooncrisis #betaalbaarwonen #modulairbouwen #eindhoven #duurzaamwonen #samenbouwen #stichtinginwording #langetermijnvisie

Vårt fokus är att genom innovativa lösningar ge fler människor en enklare och tryggare instegsmodell till ägande och integration. Mer ägande skapar större engagemang, ansvar och i förlängningen trygghet. Detta kommer alla boende till del, oavsett om man äger eller hyr.
GodBo ska medverka till att minska bostadssegregationen.
"Att förvalta fastigheter är enkelt men att förvalta bostäder är svårare." Ulf Nyqvist, ordförande GodBo AB

Name: Earth Keepers Living
Description:
Earth Keepers is a forward-thinking senior living community located in the high desert of Nevada, designed for active, eco-conscious adults aged 55 and up. We are reimagining what it means to grow older—by creating a lifestyle rooted in sustainability, connection, and purpose.
Our 40+ acres community blends natural beauty with environmentally responsible design, offering a variety of affordable living options, including eco-friendly cottages, quadplex-style shared units, and RV accommodations. Powered by solar energy and supported by regenerative systems such as rainwater harvesting and organic permaculture, Earth Keepers is committed to minimizing environmental impact while maximizing quality of life. 

We offer:
 Sustainable Living – Designed with ecological materials and powered by 
      clean energy, with the added benefit of Nevada’s favorable tax 
      environment.
  Intergenerational Engagement – Residents can mentor and collaborate 
       with students and young professionals through partnerships with local  
       universities and volunteer opportunities.
  Community-Centric Lifestyle – Shared amenities, farm-to-table meals, 
       wellness programs, and resident-led events foster a strong sense of 
       belonging.
  Nature-Connected Wellness – Surrounded by open sky and fertile land, 
       our environment encourages outdoor activity, mindfulness, and well- 
       being.

Located just one hour from Reno and 15 minutes from a major hospital, Earth Keepers offers the perfect balance of accessibility and tranquility.
We’re not just building homes—we’re cultivating a regenerative, socially connected model of aging that supports both people and the planet.

          Join the movement toward a more intentional way of living.

Description:
The Place of Purpose Community was founded as a haven for adults with physical disabilities, a space where they can thrive, build meaningful relationships, and navigate their unique life circumstances. We aim to offer an affordable, safe, and joyful living environment while also encouraging our residents to actively engage within the community. Our goal is to foster a socially rich environment where everyone can explore their purpose, connecting our residents with local businesses and utilizing their unique skills. 

For the families of our residents, we offer peace of mind. They can rest assured that their loved ones are living an independent, fulfilling life in a secure, clean, and nurturing setting. Their loved ones are amongst friends, being cared for, and contributing positively to society. We aim to provide comfort for those worried about the future care of their family members. 

The Place of Purpose Community is strictly providing a facility to house selected physically disabled adults.  We provide a facility that is fully accessible for wheelchairs, walkers, etc.  

We are NOT an assisted living home, we are NOT a licensed personal care home.  The Residents (and their families) manage their own care. 

We all need a purpose in life.  The Place of Purpose Community strives to aid our residents find those friendships, grow in confidence and live a fulfilled life.

Specialties: disabled living

Med boliger, næring- og rekreasjonsarealer skal Pappen Sarpsborg utvikles en ny bydel på en bærekraftig måte med bymessige kvaliteter. Dette innebærer utbygging av boliger med fellesfunksjoner, bydelstorg, næring og tjenesteyting. Det skal tilrettelegges for et godt bomiljø, med mangfold av møteplasser og kvalitet på bebyggelse og utearealer.

Gjennom urban utvikling, levende byrom og gode forbindelser, skal Pappen bli en integrert del av lokalområdet og en utvidet sentrumskjerne i Sarpsborg.

Name: Wysteria Properties
Description:
Wysteria Properties – Development with Purpose

Wysteria Properties is a New Orleans–based real estate development company focused on restoring historic buildings, revitalizing neighborhoods, and helping owners and investors unlock the full potential of their properties.

We specialize in residential and commercial development, fee development, and strategic project advisory services—with a particular focus on complex renovations in historic districts. Our work blends thoughtful design, financial discipline, and deep respect for New Orleans’ architectural legacy.

From fire-damaged homes to full-scale adaptive reuse projects, we take on what others overlook—bringing creative, cost-conscious solutions to properties burdened by age, disrepair, or bureaucracy. We’re experienced in navigating HDLC and SHPO guidelines, tax credit compliance, and the nuanced permitting landscape that comes with building in this city.

In addition to our development work, we offer on-market brokerage services through McEnery Residential, where we help clients buy and sell distinctive properties throughout New Orleans.

Wysteria was founded on the belief that preservation and progress can go hand in hand—and that every successful project should improve the life of the people who live in and around it.

Name: BHXModular
Description:
We specialize in factory-manufactured housing solutions that get builders and developers from project start to completion 30-50% faster than traditional construction to builders, developers, and real estate investors managing 10 to 100-plus unit residential projects across Texas and bordering states.

What makes us unique is our proprietary building-block modular system with rigid carrier frames that are precision-built in our controlled factory environment and pass third-party inspections before they ever leave our facility.

Our philosophy is simple: instead of fighting weather delays, labor shortages, and the chaos of coordinating multiple trades on-site for months, we manufacture your entire housing units in our Cisco, Texas facility, where quality is consistent, timelines are predictable, and you can scale projects efficiently while maintaining healthy profit margins.

What's your experience been with trying to meet investor deadlines when Mother Nature and unreliable subcontractors seem determined to work against you?

We also provide a diverse range of supported housing, health and social care services to meet the need of local people across the West Midlands. Based in Walsall, we operate homes in Wolverhampton, Sandwell, Dudley, Walsall and Stoke-On-Trent.

We provide care and support services at more than 20 sites in the West Midlands for young adults, older people, ex-offenders, people with drug and/or alcohol concerns, and people with mental health issues.

Nissi Homes is committed to helping to improve the quality of life for local people by providing homes and support services that meet individual needs.

We believe in sharing the love of God as demonstrated through Jesus Christ. The word Nissi is one of the names of God; Jehova Nissi and it means a banner or a protective shelter. Although we are Christians, we provide care, support and supervision to people of all faiths.

We aim to continuously improve our services by involving our customers in the services that we deliver and our performance is regularly monitored.

Name: Fresh Start Sober Living
Description:
Fresh Start was established in 2011 by Gabriela Hernandez, who had been formerly incarcerated and was given the opportunity to complete part of her sentence at a residential program. Due to the services and support she received, she was able to find permanent employment, get back on her feet, and reintegrate into society. Inspired by her own experience and desire to help others in similar situations, she started Fresh Start, with the vision and values that formerly incarcerated individuals with co-occurring disorders should be given a fair opportunity and treated with dignity, so they can re-enter society and reduce the likelihood of recidivism. 

For the past 13 years, Fresh Start has provided housing, benefits assistance, case management, access to supportive classes, and access to behavioral health and substance abuse treatment for anyone in need. To date, we have been able to provide temporary and long-term housing and services to over 1,000 formerly incarcerated individuals. We continue to provide these services to inmates released from L.A. County Jail through the Mental Health Diversion Program, individuals released from State Prisons and Hospitals with nowhere to live, persons dealing with homelessness, and any individual desiring to receive help.

Name: Inclusio Housing Solutions
Description:
Inclusio Housing Solutions is more than a training provider—we’re a trusted partner for housing professionals who are committed to making a difference. Our consulting services for real estate agents, property managers, developers, and community housing leaders are designed to offer hands-on support, bridging knowledge gaps and ensuring that every step of the housing journey is autism-inclusive, person-centric, and seamless. Whether it’s helping real estate agents and property managers better serve neurodiverse clients or working directly with families on unique housing needs, Inclusio offers tailored solutions at every stage.

New Home Builds: We consult on autism-inclusive design features, from sensory-friendly layouts to accessibility modifications.

Home Renovations: Modifications to existing homes can transform environments, making them supportive and empowering for individuals with autism. 

Buying, Selling, and Renting: We guide real estate professionals and their clients through smooth, informed transactions, ensuring housing meets the needs of neurodiverse individuals and families.

The data speaks volumes—autism rates are on the rise, and the need for autism-friendly housing is growing. At Inclusio, we advocate for the unseen challenges that neurodiverse individuals face in securing safe, comfortable homes. We believe in future-proofing housing by designing and supporting inclusive spaces, preventing future homelessness, and building communities that value every individual.

From expert training to one-on-one consulting, Inclusio Housing Solutions is here to make inclusive housing the standard, not the exception. We’re not just creating homes; we’re building understanding, opportunity, and community. Join us on this journey to raise awareness, create solutions, and build a brighter future.

#InclusioHousingSolutions #AutismInclusiveHousing #CertifiedAutismHousing #InclusiveDesign #HousingForAll #FutureProofHousing #AutismAdvocacy

Name: Souldom
Description:
Souldom – Where Your Soul Feels at Home

Souldom is an innovative initiative that combines creativity, spirituality, and technology to create dynamic spaces and communities worldwide. The name Souldom originates from the words Soulmate and Kingdom, symbolizing a place where like-minded individuals can connect, grow, and thrive together. Our mission is to provide spaces where people can reconnect with themselves, with others, and with nature while co-creating a more sustainable and meaningful way of life.

Our Mission
Souldom hosts events, creates community spaces, and fosters collaborations that prioritize shared growth and inspiration. From retreats and artistic residencies to workshops and co-living experiences, our projects aim to balance individual fulfillment with collective progress.

Our Core Values:
	•	Creativity: Providing a platform for artistic and spiritual expression.
	•	Spirituality: Encouraging connection to natural rhythms and inner wisdom.
	•	Technology: Integrating innovative solutions like web3-based membership systems and decentralized platforms.
	•	Sustainability: Designing spaces and programs that respect and protect the environment.

What We Offer:
	•	Unique events and retreats focused on personal growth and meaningful human connections.
	•	Networking opportunities where you can meet and collaborate with like-minded individuals.
	•	Inspiring community spaces that nurture creativity and spirituality.
	•	An innovative, decentralized membership system to track and reward community contributions.

Our Vision
We are building a global network where people inspire one another and work together towards a sustainable, creative, and conscious future. Souldom seeks to bridge modern technology with ancient wisdom, creating harmony between the digital and natural worlds.

Name: OneMillion.Homes
Description:
onemillion.Homes is a bold, decade-long initiative to produce one million Scale-Free Homes, designed to bridge the economy chasm in 200 cities across the nation. Our mission is to tackle the housing crisis at its roots by addressing systemic barriers that leave distressed census tracts and cost-burdened families underserved.

Every city we’ve engaged with faces the same crisis: tens of thousands of families burdened by housing costs and neighborhoods stuck in cycles of economic stagnation. These challenges persist not due to a lack of resources but because of an outdated business model incapable of addressing the scale and complexity of the problem. At onemillion.Homes, we’ve created that missing model.

Through Scale-Free Housing, we’ve modularized the entire housing value chain—streamlining everything from land acquisition to long-term maintenance. Our approach maximizes production capacity while minimizing costs, delivering climate-resilient homes that are attainable to families across the income spectrum. Each home is more than a shelter—it’s a catalyst for economic opportunity, generating local jobs and empowering residents to build wealth within their communities.

Our work spans 200 cities, partnering with local governments, nonprofits, and private investors to create ecosystems of support that ensure every project contributes to long-term community resilience. onemillion.Homes doesn’t just build houses—we build futures by creating scalable solutions that empower neighborhoods, strengthen local economies, and transform distressed areas into vibrant, thriving communities.

With a vision to produce one million homes in ten years, we’re proving that it’s not just possible to rebuild—it's necessary. Together, we can bridge the economy chasm and create a housing model that works for everyone.

ACR Housing Collective (Access. Community. Rights) is a people-centred housing justice initiative working to make housing just, affordable, and human in Nigeria.

We exist to re-centre people in housing and urban development, using research, storytelling, advocacy, and policy engagement to push for affordable, dignified, and people-centred cities in Nigeria and Africa.

A future where housing is treated as a human right, not merely a commodity, and where cities work for people, not against them.

Description:
At Tiny Homes Built To Last, we specialize in crafting high-quality, affordable tiny homes that combine functionality, comfort, and style for only $32,000. Affordability is at the heart of what we do, without compromising quality. Our homes come prewired with standard RV electrical and plumbing systems, featuring a 110-amp service and a pre-wired mini split for heating and cooling, making them both cost-effective and comfortable.

What sets us apart is our commitment to using premium materials sourced from trusted suppliers, ensuring every home is built to last. At Tiny Homes Built To Last, we believe everyone deserves a beautiful, practical home at an accessible price. Let us help you create the perfect space to live your tiny home dream—without breaking the bank!

Name: New Living Solutions for Africa (NELSA)
Description:
New Living Solutions for Africa (NELSA) is a pan-African development integrator mobilising land, capital, technology and local partners to unlock bankable affordable housing, township regeneration and inclusive economic participation across Africa.

NELSA operates as a structured platform connecting:

• Municipalities and public land custodians
• Development Finance Institutions (DFIs)
• Banks and impact investors
• Community cooperatives, stokvels and housing associations
• Local developers and built-environment professionals
• Technology, construction, manufacturing and logistics partners

Our integrated model converts land into livelihoods by enabling:

• Lease-to-Title and Rent-to-Buy housing pathways
• Mixed-use affordable city developments
• Community co-investment and asset participation
• Tokenized and alternative funding mechanisms
• Scalable township and rural regeneration frameworks

NELSA is not a developer — it is a development integrator building Africa’s next generation of affordable cities through collaboration, accountability and systems innovation.

Name: Fundación Somos
Description:
La Fundación SOMOS Ecuador es una organización no gubernamental y sin fines de lucro, creada en 2009, que promueve el desarrollo sostenible y el derecho al hábitat en Ecuador, con un enfoque en la equidad, la sustentabilidad y la participación ciudadana. Su misión es desarrollar e implementar programas de desarrollo comunitario orientados al derecho a la vivienda, la ciudad y el hábitat, involucrando actores locales y voluntarios.

La fundación trabaja bajo cuatro ejes principales:
* Mejoramiento integral del hábitat: diseño participativo de proyectos de vivienda, barrios, saneamiento e infraestructura comunitaria.
* Gestión integral de recursos naturales: conservación y restauración de ecosistemas, gestión del recurso hídrico y biodiversidad, y fortalecimiento de medios de vida locales.
* Educación integral: fortalecimiento de capacidades locales en construcción sostenible, agroecología y planificación territorial, mediante metodologías participativas y aprendizaje significativo.
* Promoción de derechos y participación: impulso a la organización comunitaria, la participación informada y la incidencia en políticas públicas para el desarrollo sostenible y los derechos humanos.

SOMOS Ecuador también forma parte de redes nacionales e internacionales que promueven la garantía de los derechos humanos y el desarrollo sostenible, y participa activamente en alianzas y plataformas enfocadas en vivienda, inclusión social y sostenibilidad ambiental.

Description:

Our mandate is to fund and or co-fund turnkey housing-solutions that result in significantly more Māori owning quality, energy efficient, financially sustainable homes.

We have a proposed target of 6,500 new homes over the next ten years and we intend to work collaboratively with Māori, Government and the Market to achieve the best possible outcomes through co-design, planning, and executing through a grassroots investment’s strategy.

Whether purchasing one home or one hundred, our financing packages offer homebuyers no deposit finance with the new home providing the security over the capped loan amount, so Māori-owned land won’t be a bottleneck when securing a loan. This package is highly suited to those wanting to add more accommodation space to their present situation quickly and affordably. Our range of UNDER $100k studio, one bedroom, and two bedroom tiny homes are locally manufactured and come backed by a big brand name for total buyer-peace-of-mind.  

Our housing strategies help get multi-dwelling  developments across the financial hard line by significantly lowering the overall construction costs and our typical budget for these projects is from $100K to $15 million dollars. For amounts above this, talk to us.

Name: Phoenix Foundation Of Nevada
Description:
The Phoenix Foundation of Nevada, Inc. is a Henderson-based nonprofit dedicated to transforming the lives of young people aging out of foster care. Founded by Zachary and Amber Gray, the organization exists to address one of the most urgent gaps in the foster care system: the lack of long-term support once youth turn eighteen and state services end. Without proper guidance, many face homelessness, unemployment, poor health outcomes, and the overwhelming challenge of navigating adulthood alone.

To break this cycle, the Phoenix Foundation provides a comprehensive, trauma-informed, and holistic support system. At its core is safe transitional housing, ensuring that youth have stability while they learn and grow. Surrounding this foundation are wraparound services, including:

Life skills training in financial literacy, cooking, household management, and decision-making.

Education and career development, from tutoring and GED prep to job readiness and higher education guidance.

Healthcare and mental health access, focusing on preventive care, wellness, and healing from trauma.

Mentorship and community connections, so youth have trusted adults and positive networks as they step into independence.

The Phoenix Foundation works in partnership with local leaders, organizations, and volunteers to build a supportive community that empowers resilience and success. As Henderson Councilwoman Monica Larson affirmed, their mission directly combats youth homelessness while creating brighter futures for individuals and the community as a whole

03.13.25 Letter of Support_CW L…

.

Rooted in compassion and action, the Phoenix Foundation of Nevada is more than a housing program—it is a launchpad for hope, growth, and lifelong impact.

Name: Baldwin Supportive Housing Service
Description:
Welcome to Baldwin Supportive Housing Service, a non-profit organization dedicated to making a transformative impact in the lives of disenfranchised demographics in the Greater New Orleans area.

At Baldwin Supportive Housing Service, we are driven by a simple yet profound belief: that everyone deserves a safe and stable place to call home, regardless of their circumstances. We operate with a deep sense of purpose, working tirelessly to address the pressing issue of homelessness and provide comprehensive support to individuals facing various challenges.

Our mission is rooted in compassion and guided by a commitment to building a stronger, more inclusive community. Through our shared housing model, we create a supportive environment that promotes collaboration, personal growth, and a renewed sense of hope. By offering safe and affordable shared homes, we provide a lifeline to those in need, helping them break free from the cycle of instability and exclusion.

We have assembled a dedicated team of professionals and compassionate volunteers who are passionate about making a difference. Together, we provide personalized care, case management, and access to essential resources, ensuring that each individual receives the support they need to thrive.

Join us on this extraordinary journey as we strive to bring dignity, hope, and belonging to those who need it the most. Connect with us on LinkedIn to stay updated on our impactful work, engage with our passionate community, and explore ways you can contribute to our mission. Together, we can make a difference, one home at a time.

Beyond housing, we strive to nurture the whole person by connecting residents to programs and services that support their physical, social, educational, and emotional well-being, fostering security, health, and happiness.

We are committed to revitalizing neighborhoods, preserving affordability, and expanding access to opportunity through thoughtful redevelopment, rehabilitation, and collaboration. Name: Tenants First C.I.C
Description:
Tenants First CIC
Independent Support | Housing Disrepair | Awaab’s Law Compliance | Advocacy

At Tenants First, we put people before profit.

We’re a not-for-profit Community Interest Company providing independent, practical support for tenants while helping landlords and housing providers resolve issues quickly, fairly, and cost-effectively.

 What We Do

Disrepair Resolution: Independent inspection reports that protect both tenants and landlords from poor-quality or inflated claims.

Awaab’s Law Compliance: 24-hour tenant contact, rapid damp & mould inspections, and clear reporting to support housing providers in meeting new legal requirements.

Tenant Advocacy: Free support for tenants dealing with repairs, overcrowding, rent disputes, or anti-social behaviour.

Partnership Working: We work with tenants, landlords, solicitors, and community partners to achieve the best outcomes.

 Our Mission
To stand by tenants when they need support most — while also helping housing providers direct resources to where they make the biggest impact for communities.

 Why It Matters
Poor housing harms lives. By resolving cases early, avoiding unnecessary legal costs, and ensuring compliance with new standards, we protect both tenants and the wider tenant community.

Name: Housing Initiative Perth

Housing Initiative Perth is a non-profit initiative focused on developing small-scale, build-to-rent housing models that are financially viable, socially grounded, and aligned with local income realities.

The initiative is guided by the belief that secure housing must be predictable, fair, and economically sustainable - enabling dignity, stability, and long-term community resilience rather than dependency.

Our work centres on early-stage concept development, feasibility assessment, stakeholder alignment, and the disciplined structuring of housing projects that can be delivered when market and policy conditions allow.

Housing Initiative Perth operates with a long-term horizon, prioritising sound economics, responsible governance, and practical execution over speed or scale.

Name: NTX land
Description:
NTX Land specializes in the strategic acquisition and development of rural properties across North Texas. Our core business model focuses on identifying, purchasing, and thoughtfully subdividing larger acreage tracts into smaller, build-ready parcels while maintaining the natural character of the land. We create value by providing thoroughly vetted properties with completed surveys, established access, and necessary infrastructure planning, allowing buyers to immediately begin their building journey.

Our development process encompasses comprehensive due diligence, including thorough title research, environmental assessments, and local jurisdiction compliance. We work closely with county officials, engineers, and utility providers to ensure our developments meet all regulatory requirements while preserving the rural essence of each property. Our strategic approach to land planning maximizes both usability and value, resulting in properties that are both marketable and sustainable.

We pride ourselves on creating opportunities for both residential end-users seeking their piece of country living and investors looking to participate in this growing rural land market. Our developments typically range from 1 to 10 acre parcels, offering the perfect balance of affordability and seclusion.

As active participants in the market, we are working to build strong relationships with local municipalities, utility providers, and industry professionals. This network allows us to efficiently navigate the development process while identifying prime opportunities for future growth. We welcome partnerships with landowners, investors, real estate professionals, and contractors/service providers who share our vision for thoughtful rural development.

Our commitment to maintaining the rural character of North Texas while addressing the growing demand for country properties is what sets NTX Land apart in the land development market.

Description:
HEVA Housing Foundation (the Nonprofit) was formed for the charitable purposes of providing a temporary shared community and transitional or permanent residential housing accommodations for individuals who are (1) living in a drug and alcohol-free environment, (2) experiencing mental health issues, (3) receiving medication-assisted treatment, or (4) experiencing housing insecurities.

The Nonprofit may also be providing quality shared housing to improve the lives of members of charitable classes, such as disabled veterans, individuals transitioning from prison, foster children who have aged out, elderly, special needs adults, disadvantaged women, victims of domestic abuse, etc. Housing costs have dramatically increased even though almost 10 million people are unemployed. As a result, certain groups are having a difficult time finding affordable housing or are vulnerable to becoming homeless. For example, many aged-out foster youths will experience homelessness after they are forced to leave foster care when they turn 18 or 21 because they cannot afford housing. The Nonprofit will provide housing, build their confidence, and help them find employment so they can live independently

Name: UKUA Group
Description:
UKUA Group is a property investment & development company based in Edinburgh and Kyiv. We channel UK investment into building modern low-rise duplex & triplex townhouses around Kyiv (typically 1–5 miles from the capital), where demand for quality housing outstrips supply.
We keep it simple: Invest → Build → Sell → Reinvest. By focusing on small projects (4–8 homes) and an average 9–12-month project cycle—~1 month to acquire land, ~4–5 months construction, and ~4–7 months sales—we aim to turn capital faster while delivering thoughtfully designed homes.
For investors, we target 8–20% returns per project with entry levels from $25,000, supported by clear security options (e.g., land ownership or company shares, depending on level).
Mission: to improve people’s quality of life by pairing compelling property opportunities for partners with well-built mid-market homes for Ukrainian families. If you’re an investor, supplier, or collaborator aligned with ethical, transparent growth—let’s talk.

Woonplan levert de grond, vergunningen en plannen om 5000 woningen per project te bouwen in het groene landschap. Hoge kwaliteit, betaalbare en energie positieve woningen voor alle doelgroepen. Woonplan vermindert de stikstofuitstoot, verbetert de natuur, reduceert CO2 emissies.
**Missie van Woonplan**

Missie:
Bij Woonplan is het onze missie om duurzame en betaalbare woonoplossingen te creëren die de ecologische voetafdruk verkleinen en gemeenschapszin bevorderen. Wij streven ernaar om innovatieve woningbouwprojecten te ontwikkelen die ecologisch verantwoord zijn, door gebruik te maken van groene bouwtechnieken en hernieuwbare energiebronnen. Door intensieve samenwerking met lokale gemeenschappen, overheden en andere belanghebbenden, willen we leefomgevingen creëren waar mensen van alle leeftijden en achtergronden met elkaar verbonden zijn en kunnen floreren. Ons doel is om bij te dragen aan het oplossen van de woningcrisis in Nederland door kwalitatief hoogwaardige, milieuvriendelijke en inclusieve woonwijken te realiseren.
Visie van Woonplan

Onze visie is om voorop te lopen in de transformatie van de Nederlandse woningmarkt door pioniers te zijn in duurzaam en ecologisch bouwen. Wij zien een toekomst waarin iedereen toegang heeft tot betaalbare en hoogwaardige woningen, ingebed in gemeenschappen die bloeien door een harmonieuze relatie met de natuur. Woonplan streeft ernaar om innovatieve en schaalbare modellen te ontwikkelen die als blauwdruk kunnen dienen voor duurzame stads- en dorpsontwikkeling. We willen een wereld creëren waarin duurzame bouwpraktijken de norm zijn, en waarin mensen wonen in gezonde, veerkrachtige en betrokken gemeenschappen die bijdragen aan een betere planeet voor toekomstige generaties.

V naší developerské firmě s celou řadou služeb pro naše klienty. Specializujeme se na výstavbu bytových a rodinných domů v Praze a poskytujeme inženýrské řešení pro developery. S naší vlastní stavební firmou Kodaňská stavební s.r.o. jsme schopni úspěšně realizovat rozsáhlé developerské projekty a další zakázky.

Naše týmy odborníků se s vášní a pečlivostí věnují každému detailu, abychom vytvářeli moderní a kvalitní nemovitosti, které vyhovují potřebám a přání našich klientů. Jsme vždy otevřeni spolupráci s malými i velkými developery, kteří chtějí dosáhnout úspěchu ve svých projektech. Kromě toho nabízíme také prodej nemovitostí a odborné poradenství, abychom našim zákazníkům poskytli komplexní a špičkové služby v oblasti stavebnictví a developerských investic. Naše dlouholeté zkušenosti, transparentnost a profesionální přístup nás řadí mezi nejrespektovanější jména v této oblasti.

Jedním z našich hlavních cílů je vytvářet nemovitosti, které přinášejí dlouhodobou hodnotu a spokojenost našim klientům. Díky pečlivému plánování a preciznímu provedení dosahujeme vynikajících výsledků a tím si získáváme důvěru našich klientů. Jsme hrdi na to, že naše práce přispívá k urbanistickému rozvoji Prahy a okolí.

Pokud hledáte spolehlivou a profesionální společnost, která vám pomůže s realizací vašich stavebních projektů, jsme tu pro vás. S Czech Develop Services a naší vlastní firmou Kodaňská stavební s.r.o. získáte kvalitní služby a spolehlivého partnera na každém kroku vaší stavební cesty. Jsme připraveni vám pomoci s vašimi ambiciózními plány a vytvořit společně skvělé nemovitosti.

Name: Free Housing Act | Housing Education
Description:
Free Housing Act | Housing Education presents an innovative approach to improving living conditions.

1. Global Implementation

Universal Access: By making free housing a global initiative, everyone would have access to shelter, reducing disparities across different regions and socio-economic groups.

Cultural Adaptation: Housing solutions consider cultural and regional differences to ensure they meet the needs and preferences of various communities.

2. Upgrading and Housing Education

Resilience Training: Providing education on building practices, maintenance, and disaster preparedness we can empower communities to withstand natural disasters and climate change effects.

Sustainable Practices: Training on sustainable living, energy efficiency, and resource management contribute to lower utility costs and environmental conservation.

Community Workshops: Engaging community members through workshops one can foster a sense of ownership and responsibility toward their homes and neighborhoods.

3. Community Stability

Reducing Homelessness: Providing free housing significantly decrease homelessness and the associated social problems.

Strengthening Family Units: Secure housing contributes to emotional and financial stability, reducing the stress that can lead to family breakdowns, such as divorce or separation.

4. Utilizing Existing Homes

Existing and Vacant Property Utilization: Can save time and money while also revitalizing neighborhoods and communities.

Community Partnerships: Collaborating with local organizations, nonprofits, and governments can facilitate the repurposing of existing structures into housing.

The combination of free housing and housing education creates a comprehensive strategy to address various social issues, including crime, family stability, and disaster resilience. By focusing on community empowerment, education, and support, we can foster thriving and resilient neighborhoods that withstand challenges and promote well-being for all.

Name: ABOVE THE OBSTACLE FOUNDATION
Description:
“We Believe Housing Brings Hope”

A place to move in. A shelter in storms & snow. A house to call your own.

At Above The Obstacle Foundation, we strive together to rise above the obstacles and make a safe community for people from all walks of life.

> Over 15 million adults aged 65+ years or older are economically insecure with incomes below 200% of the federal poverty level.

> More than half of Black and Hispanic seniors have incomes below the poverty line.

> Older workers of color are at most risk for unemployment, and nearly 70% of older workers (50+) are likely to experience one involuntary job loss.

At ATO, we are constantly exposed to agonizing statistics endured by the marginalized, low-income, disabled, and senior population in Los Angeles, LA County, Fresno, Florida & Louisiana.

We have 2 dedicated programs designed to serve the society:

1.       Under the Home Weatherization program, ATO Foundation identifies energy wastage and provides insulated (from extreme weather) housing units for elderly residents, disabled communities, and low-income families and individuals. Learn more at: abovetof.org/home-weatherization

2.       Under the Transitional Housing service, rooted in its Founder’s horrible story of deceptive law & imprisonment, the ATO Foundation helps mitigate uncertainty and fear by providing recently-released prisoners with a stable and comfortable place to live while they get back on their feet. Learn more at: abovetof.org/transitional-housing

Communities are at their strongest, safest, and most vibrant when individuals have safe, affordable places to live. By ensuring quality homes and associated support are delivered to those who need them the most, Above The Obstacle Foundation believes it is its responsibility to strengthen the weaker parts of the society and create a resilient whole.


Are you going to become part of our mission by donating for a better tomorrow? You’re just one click away: abovetof.org/donate/

Memory Care: Safe, compassionate environments for seniors with Alzheimer’s or dementia

Independent Living: Comfortable, maintenance-free lifestyles for active seniors

In-Home Care: Professional care delivered in the comfort of home

Skilled Nursing: 24/7 medical support and rehabilitation services

Hospice & Palliative Care: End-of-life care with dignity and respect

Our services are offered completely free of charge to families, and our experienced care advisors take the time to understand your unique needs, preferences, and budget before helping you explore your options.

Let’s walk this journey together.

Name: Ecommunitas Co-operative
Description:
Ecommunitas Co-operative is a new way of living. Rooted in Greater Manchester, we are delivering a first-of-its-kind regenerative neighbourhood - affordable homes and essential community enterprises designed for health, nature, and belonging.

Our vision is simple but bold: Homes That Heal • Rooted In Nature • Designed For Life • Truly Affordable. Every home is part of a co-operative housing model, blending affordable purchase, rental, co-housing, and later-living with market-rate and luxury options. This ensures diversity, choice, and permanent affordability across generations.

Each home is crafted with biophilic and salutogenic design, guided by the WELL Building Standard and neuroarchitecture research. Natural light, clean air, and non-toxic materials aren’t extras - they’re the foundation. Utility bills are designed to fall by up to 90% through passive house performance, renewable energy, and rainwater harvesting. Surrounding every street is a food forest: orchards, herbs, gardens, and wild habitats that bring daily connection to nature while restoring biodiversity.

But Ecommunitas is more than housing. At the heart of village life are co-operative businesses: The Eatery, Farmacy Co-op, Health & Wellbeing Centre, Holiday Homes, and the Housing Co-op itself. Together, they provide essentials on the doorstep, create quality jobs, and keep value circulating locally. This integrated model tackles multiple crises at once - housing, health, cost of living, climate resilience, and social care - while protecting residents from future shocks.

Name: Historic Pascoag Grammar, LLC
Description: The Pascoag Grammar School (originally known as Burrillville High School) is located at 265 Sayles Avenue in the village of Pascoag, Town of Burrillville, Rhode Island. Constructed in 1917, it is a 2-1/2 story, Georgian Revival style, T-plan, red brick public school building measuring 110 by 46 feet. The building stands on a raised basement and has a limestone and granite foundation; a steel structure with wood joist/stud framing, hollow core Natco backup brick, and red brick veneer exterior walls; a nearly flat roof with parapet; wood multi-light windows; and limestone trim including a substantial belt course at the 1 st floor level, and another above the 2nd floor windows, as well as window lintels and sills. The symmetrical facade, fifteen bays wide, has a center entrance situated half a level above grade; the wood, multi-light, double-hung windows are separated by brick pilasters. Stair towers at the north and south ends of the building, each recessed about fourteen feet from the facade, contain secondary entrances just a few steps above grade. A three-story, 42 by 42 foot auditorium wing projects from the east (rear) elevation. In size, massing, plan, materials, and architectural detailing, it is a typical example of a Georgian Revival school building of the early 20th century. The school closed in 1995, and stood largely vacant (except for some town school department offices) for the next decade. In 2005-2006 new owners undertook a major rehabilitation project, intending to retain and preserve as many of the building's character-defining exterior and interior historic architectural features as possible while converting it to multi-family residential use.
